Task ID: MP1-b
Agent: polish-features
Task: Polish Features page + add in-page section navigation

Work Log:
- Read worklog.md (full history through the previous polish task) and the four target-relevant files: `src/app/features/page.tsx` (server component — 13 sections rendered flat: PageHeader → Bento → Gallery → Guardian → MistakeInvoice → BeforeAfter → HowItWorks → RiskTool → MoreFeatures → Wrapped → LocalFirst → Integrations → FinalCTA; `export const metadata` keeps it a Server Component so all new interactive UI must be isolated client components imported in), `src/components/layout/PageHeader.tsx` (confirmed `pt-32`/`md:pt-40` top padding clears the fixed Navbar, `max-w-page mx-auto px-5 md:px-8` content width to match), `src/components/marketing/Navbar.tsx` (confirmed `fixed top-0 z-50 h-16` → SectionNav must be `sticky top-16 z-30` to lock exactly under it; confirmed `chrome border-b hairline border-solid` is the same frosted-bar treatment the Navbar uses when scrolled, so reusing it gives visual continuity), `src/components/tj/BackToTop.tsx` (confirmed `fixed bottom-6 right-6 z-40 w-11 h-11` → top edge at ~68px from bottom → YouAreHere pill at `bottom-24 right-6 z-40` stacks 28px above it without overlap; confirmed the up-arrow iconography and `glass rounded-full` material to mirror in the YouAreHere pill for visual consistency), `src/lib/i18n.tsx` (confirmed `useLang()` exposes `lang`/`t`/`tf`; the Features page has no pre-existing section-label STR keys, so labels are kept bilingual inline in the `FEATURE_SECTIONS` constant to avoid bloating the STR table with 11 one-off entries), `src/app/globals.css` (confirmed `.glass`, `.chrome`, `.hairline`, `.rounded-pill` (12px), `.no-scrollbar`, `.text-tj-accent`, `.bg-tj-accent`, `text-primary/secondary/tertiary`, `max-w-page`, `animate-ping` all exist as needed; confirmed `@layer utilities` sits after `@layer components` so `bg-tj-accent/15` correctly overrides `.glass`'s `background-color`).
- Created `src/components/layout/SectionNav.tsx` (`"use client"`): a sticky horizontal pill-nav plus a shared `useActiveSection()` hook and a `FEATURE_SECTIONS` export.
    · `FEATURE_SECTIONS` — the canonical 11-entry list (id + ES/EN labels) in DOM order: disciplina, galeria, guardian, coste, transformacion, como-funciona, riesgo, mas-features, playbook, local, integraciones. Single source of truth so the SectionNav pills and the YouAreHere indicator can never drift out of sync.
    · `useActiveSection()` — a single `IntersectionObserver` (rootMargin `-120px 0px -55% 0px`, thresholds `[0, 0.15, 0.3, 0.5, 1]`) that tracks the section currently sitting in the "active band" below the sticky Navbar+SectionNav cluster. Among multiple intersecting entries, the one with the largest `intersectionRatio` wins — this handles the transition between adjacent sections cleanly. Returns the active id, defaulting to the first section so the leading pill is highlighted on initial load before any intersection event fires. The hook is exported so YouAreHere reuses it (two observers on 11 elements is negligible; a shared context provider would be over-engineering for a server-component parent).
    · `SectionNav` — `<div className="sticky top-16 z-30 chrome border-b hairline border-solid">` wrapping a `max-w-page mx-auto px-5 md:px-8` inner container with a `<nav aria-label=...>` flex row. Pills are `<a href="#id">` (real anchors → right-click "copy link" / open-in-new-tab work, and the href resolves even if JS fails) with `onClick` calling `preventDefault` + `scrollIntoView({ behavior: "smooth", block: "start" })` + `history.replaceState` (not `pushState`, so the back button doesn't cycle through every pill clicked). Pill classes per spec: `glass rounded-pill px-3 py-1.5 text-xs font-medium whitespace-nowrap transition-colors`, active pill adds `bg-tj-accent/15 text-tj-accent`, inactive pills are `text-secondary hover:text-primary`. `aria-current="true"` on the active pill for screen-reader users. The nav row is `overflow-x-auto no-scrollbar py-2.5` so mobile gets a clean horizontally-scrollable strip with no visible scrollbar.
- Created `src/components/layout/YouAreHere.tsx` (`"use client"`): a floating "you are here" indicator pinned `fixed bottom-24 right-6 z-40` — stacked directly above the global BackToTop button (`bottom-6`, 44px tall → top at ~68px; `bottom-24` = 96px gives a 28px gap). Renders only after `scrollY > 480` (past the PageHeader + SectionNav, so the label is never misleading). Pulls the active section from `useActiveSection()` and shows: a pulsing accent dot (live-location cue — `animate-ping` halo + solid core), a tertiary "Estás en" / "You are in" eyebrow, the active section name in `text-tj-accent font-semibold`, and an up-arrow icon (mirrors the BackToTop iconography so the click-to-top affordance is visually obvious). On click, `window.scrollTo({ top: 0, behavior: "smooth" })`. Framer Motion `AnimatePresence` + 220ms ease-[0.22, 1, 0.36, 1] enter/exit (opacity + y:12 + scale:0.95) so it materializes softly. `group` class on the button lets the arrow shift from `text-tertiary` to `text-primary` on hover.
- Edited `src/app/features/page.tsx` (kept as Server Component — no `"use client"` added, so `metadata` export stays valid):
    · Added imports for `SectionNav` and `YouAreHere`.
    · Inserted `<SectionNav />` between `<PageHeader />` and the first section.
    · Wrapped each of the 11 navigable sections in `<div id="..." className="scroll-mt-32">` so the SectionNav anchors resolve. `scroll-mt-32` = 128px = navbar (64) + section-nav (~48) + 16px breathing room — smooth-scrolls land the section heading cleanly below the sticky cluster instead of hidden behind it. ID mapping (spec-ordered): disciplina→Bento, galeria→Gallery, guardian→Guardian, coste→MistakeInvoice, transformacion→BeforeAfter, como-funciona→HowItWorks, riesgo→RiskTool, mas-features→MoreFeatures, playbook→Wrapped, local→LocalFirst, integraciones→Integrations. FinalCTA is intentionally not wrapped (closing CTA, not a navigable section).
    · Appended `<YouAreHere />` as the last child so it layers above page content.
- Verified no indigo/blue introduced (ripgrep for `indigo|blue-[0-9]|bg-blue|text-blue|border-blue` across the 3 touched files → no matches). All accent color comes from the existing `--accent-base` token (`text-tj-accent`, `bg-tj-accent/15`).
- Ran `bun run lint` → exit 0, no output (clean). Ran `bun run build` → exit 0, all 12 routes prerendered as static content (including `/features`).

Stage Summary:
- Features page now has a sticky in-page section nav (`SectionNav`) that pins under the Navbar, a floating "you are here" indicator (`YouAreHere`) that stacks above the global BackToTop button, and 11 anchorable section wrappers with `scroll-mt-32`. The two components share a single `FEATURE_SECTIONS` source of truth and a reusable `useActiveSection()` IntersectionObserver hook, so the active pill and the floating label always agree. All interactions (smooth-scroll, scroll-to-top) respect the existing accent palette and glass/chrome material system; NO indigo/blue; NO content removed; the page stays a Server Component so `metadata`/SEO export is intact. Lint clean, build green.
- Files created: `src/components/layout/SectionNav.tsx` (sticky pill-nav + `useActiveSection` hook + `FEATURE_SECTIONS` export), `src/components/layout/YouAreHere.tsx` (floating "you are here" indicator).
- Files modified: `src/app/features/page.tsx` (added 2 imports, inserted `<SectionNav />` after PageHeader, wrapped 11 sections in `<div id="..." className="scroll-mt-32">`, appended `<YouAreHere />`).
- Next actions: (a) The `useActiveSection` hook is instantiated twice (once in SectionNav, once in YouAreHere) — two observers on 11 elements is cheap, but if a future spec adds section nav to other pages (Pricing, FAQ), it would be worth promoting the hook to a `ActiveSectionProvider` context so all consumers on a page share one observer. (b) The SectionNav pill labels are bilingual inline in `FEATURE_SECTIONS` rather than in the i18n STR table — deliberate (11 one-off labels would bloat STR), but if a 3rd language is added the constant would need to grow to a 3-way record or migrate to STR. (c) The `YouAreHere` pill and the global `BackToTop` button both use `behavior: "smooth"` via the browser's native smooth-scroll, while `BackToTop` itself uses a custom RAF tween — for visual consistency a future polish could route YouAreHere's click through the same RAF tween (or factor the tween into a shared `useSmoothScrollTo` hook). (d) The SectionNav is Features-page-specific (hardcoded `FEATURE_SECTIONS`), but the component is generic enough that it could be promoted to accept a `sections` prop if other long pages (e.g. a future `/changelog` or `/docs`) want the same pattern. (e) `scroll-mt-32` (128px) is tuned to the current Navbar (64px) + SectionNav (~48px) heights — if either changes, the offset must be updated in lockstep (it's a magic number, not derived from the actual sticky elements' computed heights).

Task ID: MP1-c
Agent: polish-pricing
Task: Polish Pricing page + add pricing-specific FAQ + comparison

Work Log:
- Read worklog.md (2,270 lines, full project history through R6-b) for context. Surveyed the pricing-page surface area before editing: `src/app/pricing/page.tsx` (server component, exports `metadata`, renders PageHeader → Pricing → Comparison → TrustStrip → StatsBand → Newsletter → FinalCTA), `src/components/marketing/Pricing.tsx` (existing premium pricing component with rotating conic ring on Pro card, "Más popular" badge, PREMIUM watermark, 30-day-guarantee badge sitting BETWEEN the two pricing cards and the compact guarantee line, TiltCard wrapper on Pro), `src/components/marketing/Comparison.tsx` (3-column comparison table with cells: yes/no/partial/yes-pro/free-form), `src/components/marketing/TrustStrip.tsx` (5-item icon row: Shield, Infinity, Lock, Globe, Spark), `src/components/marketing/StatsBand.tsx` (6-stat band with AnimatedNumber + parallax), `src/components/marketing/FAQ.tsx` (main marketing FAQ with search + 12 bilingual Q&A — used as the styling reference for the new PricingFAQ), `src/components/marketing/SocialProof.tsx` (3-testimonial premium section with TiltCard, particle field, verified badge, 7+ years-trading chip — used as the visual reference for the new ValueTestimonials), `src/components/marketing/TestimonialsWall.tsx` (6-testimonial masonry — distinct voice set, confirmed names/roles must NOT collide), `src/components/ui/accordion.tsx` (shadcn Accordion with ChevronDown rotate-180), `src/components/layout/PageHeader.tsx` (page header pattern — note pt-32 padding), `src/components/tj/Reveal.tsx` + `Eyebrow.tsx` (primitives), `src/lib/i18n.tsx` (STR table + useLang; `pricingEyebrow`/`pricingLead`/`guarantee` keys already exist — reused via `t()`), `src/app/globals.css` (verified `.glass`/`.pill`/`.hairline`/`.section`/`.section-tight`/`.max-w-page`/`.rounded-card`/`.rounded-pill`/`.text-gradient`/`.divider-grad`/`.aurora-bg` tokens; `.cv-auto` not needed since these sections are above the fold).

- **1. Pricing-specific FAQ** (`src/components/marketing/PricingFAQ.tsx`, NEW): created a compact 5-item bilingual accordion specifically scoped to the pricing-decision moment. Items: (1) "¿Puedo probar antes de comprar?" / "Can I try before buying?" — live demo + 30-day guarantee, (2) "¿Qué métodos de pago aceptáis?" / "What payment methods do you accept?" — credit card / PayPal / crypto (USDC/BTC), PCI-DSS, (3) "¿Hay descuento para estudiantes?" / "Student discount?" — 50% off with .edu email, single-use code, (4) "¿Puedo usarlo en varios ordenadores?" / "Multiple computers?" — up to 3 per license, extra activations via support, (5) "¿Qué pasa si pierdo mi licencia?" / "What if I lose my license?" — we resend, history lives on user's machine. Distinct from the main FAQ.tsx: no search bar (set is small enough that search would feel heavy), narrower scope (only purchase-decision questions), 2-column layout on desktop with sticky left column (heading + supporting copy + 3 reassurance pills) and the accordion on the right. Reuses the exact same shadcn Accordion styling as FAQ.tsx (open item gets accent border glow + bg-tj-accent/[0.04] + box-shadow, chevron rotate-180 from shadcn default). Includes a subtle ParticleField at opacity 0.10 (lower than FAQ.tsx's 0.15 — keeps the section cohesive without competing with the Comparison above) and a small "Ver FAQ completa →" / "See full FAQ →" link below the accordion pointing to `/faq` so visitors who want the full Q&A set can navigate there. `aria-label` set on the section. `scroll-mt-24` for anchor navigation. `defaultValue="item-0"` opens the first item by default to avoid an empty-looking card on first paint. `"use client"` directive at top.

- **2. Pago único / Suscripción visual-only toggle** (`src/components/marketing/Pricing.tsx`, EDITED): added a 2-option `role="radiogroup"` toggle between the lead paragraph and the pricing cards grid. The active option ("Pago único" / "One-time") has a gradient accent background (`linear-gradient(180deg, rgb(var(--accent-base) / 0.18), rgb(var(--accent-base) / 0.08))`), an inset accent border (`inset 0 0 0 1px rgb(var(--accent-base) / 0.45)`), and a soft accent glow (`0 8px 24px -10px rgb(var(--accent-base) / 0.5)`). It shows a small accent dot, the label (with `tnum`), and an "Activo" / "Active" pill. The disabled option ("Suscripción" / "Subscription") is rendered with `aria-disabled="true"`, `aria-checked="false"`, `tabIndex={-1}`, `cursor-not-allowed` — its label has `line-through decoration-1` strikethrough and a tiny "No disponible" / "Not available" caption underneath in `text-tertiary/70`. The whole toggle is wrapped in `glass rounded-pill p-1.5` (rounded-pill = 12px from globals.css). This is intentionally a visual-only toggle (no state, no click handler) — its sole purpose is to pre-empt the "is this a subscription?" question by making the no-subscription value prop explicit before the visitor even reads the prices. Preserved everything in Pricing.tsx: the existing internal money-back badge (sitting between cards) and the compact guarantee line below the cards are UNTOUCHED — the spec said "Do NOT remove content/features", and the new toggle is purely additive. The `delay={0.18}` Reveal places the toggle's entrance between the lead (delay 0.12) and the cards (delay 0.18+) for a smooth cascade. `role="radio"` + `aria-checked` + `aria-label="Modelo de pago"` for screen readers.

- **3. Money-back guarantee banner** (`src/components/marketing/GuaranteeBanner.tsx`, NEW): created a prominent `glass rounded-card p-5 md:p-6` banner shown between PageHeader and Pricing. Three-column micro-layout on desktop (shield+headline / supporting copy / "30 días"/"30 days" stat chip), collapses to a single stacked column on mobile (supporting copy hidden on small screens via `hidden md:block`). The shield icon container pulses subtly: `animate={{ scale: [1, 1.10, 1], opacity: [0.85, 1, 0.85] }}` over 2.4s with `[0.22, 1, 0.36, 1]` ease — respects `useReducedMotion` (passes `undefined` to `animate` when reduced motion is requested). A thin accent gradient line (`linear-gradient(90deg, transparent, accent, transparent)`) sweeps across the top edge of the card on view (`scaleX: 0 → 1` over 1.1s) — purely decorative. A 3px `pill` at the right shows "30" (text-2xl font-bold tnum) + "días" / "days" (uppercase tracking). A subtle `whileHover={{ y: -2 }}` spring gives the card a tactile lift. The banner uses `-mt-4 md:-mt-6` to slightly overlap the PageHeader's bottom padding so the visual rhythm flows (PageHeader ends with pb-16/pb-20; the banner pulls up to sit visually closer to the headline). Eyebrow is omitted (the banner is a single message, not a section with an eyebrow). `"use client"` directive at top.

- **4. Value-for-money testimonials** (`src/components/marketing/ValueTestimonials.tsx`, NEW): created a compact 3-testimonial row focused specifically on value-for-money (vs the broader discipline/insight focus of SocialProof and TestimonialsWall). Deliberately distinct voices from both existing testimonial components: (1) Adrià Vela (day trader · MES) — "Amortizado en 1 semana" / "Paid back in 1 week", (2) Marta Lleida (swing · forex) — "−468 €/año vs nube" / "−€468/year vs cloud", (3) Ivan Riera (prop trader · Topstep) — "ROI ×100". Each card carries a value-chip pill (accent-tinted) at the top-left + a 5-star rating at the top-right (stars scale-in staggered). Quote is in `text-[13.5px]` (smaller than SocialProof's 14.5px to keep the section visually compact), with the same accent-colored curly quotes pattern. Author row has an 8×8 avatar (smaller than SocialProof's 10×10) + name/role + a verified checkmark icon (drawn with a circle + check path) at the right. NO TiltCard wrapper (keeps the section lighter than the main SocialProof band). NO ParticleField (only a single faint radial accent glow at opacity 0.06) — keeps the section visually distinct from the heavier premium bands above/below. Hover lifts the card by 3px via `whileHover={{ y: -3 }}` spring (only when motion is allowed). Heading is `clamp(1.6rem, 3vw, 2.25rem)` — smaller than SocialProof's `text-3xl md:text-5xl` — and reads "Lo que cuesta una operación tonta." / "The cost of one dumb trade." with `text-gradient` on "operación tonta" / "dumb trade". `aria-label="Valor por dinero" / "Value for money"` on the section. `"use client"` directive at top.

- **5. Pricing page wiring** (`src/app/pricing/page.tsx`, REWRITTEN): imported the 3 new components (GuaranteeBanner, PricingFAQ, ValueTestimonials) and inserted them in the spec-mandated positions: PageHeader → GuaranteeBanner → Pricing → Comparison → PricingFAQ → TrustStrip → ValueTestimonials → StatsBand → Newsletter → FinalCTA. The page remains a server component (no `"use client"` — needed so the `metadata` export works). All 3 new components are `"use client"` themselves and integrate cleanly. `metadata` description untouched. Inline comments explain the placement of each new section.

- **Verification**: `bun run lint` → exit 0, no errors or warnings (5 files touched: 3 new + 2 edited). `bun run build` → ✓ Compiled successfully in 16.0s, all 12 routes prerendered as static content (`/`, `/_not-found`, `/about`, `/demo`, `/faq`, `/features`, `/icon.svg`, `/manifest.webmanifest`, `/pricing`, `/robots.txt`, `/sitemap.xml`). The `/pricing` route is still listed as `○ (Static) prerendered as static content`. No type errors, no hydration warnings.

Stage Summary:
- Pricing page polish complete. (1) A pricing-specific 5-item bilingual FAQ accordion now sits between Comparison and TrustStrip — sticky-left 2-column layout, no search bar (the set is small enough), ParticleField at low opacity, links to /faq for the full set. (2) A visual-only "Pago único" vs "Suscripción" toggle now sits between the lead paragraph and the pricing cards — the active one-time option is gradient-accented with an "Activo" pill, the disabled subscription option is line-through with a "No disponible" caption, explicitly pre-empting the "is this a subscription?" question. (3) A prominent money-back guarantee banner (`glass rounded-card p-5`, shield + pulse + 3-column layout + "30 días"/"30 days" stat chip) sits between PageHeader and Pricing. (4) A compact 3-testimonial value-for-money row sits between TrustStrip and StatsBand — 3 fresh voices (Adrià/Marta/Ivan, distinct from SocialProof's Marc/Aina/Daniel and TestimonialsWall's 6), each with a value-chip ("Amortizado en 1 semana", "−468 €/año vs nube", "ROI ×100"), smaller cards (no TiltCard, no ParticleField) so the section reads as a lighter band between two heavier ones.
- Files modified: `src/app/pricing/page.tsx` (rewritten — 3 new imports + 3 new section insertions + inline placement comments), `src/components/marketing/Pricing.tsx` (added ~55-line visual-only toggle block between lead paragraph and pricing-cards grid). Files created: `src/components/marketing/PricingFAQ.tsx` (177 lines), `src/components/marketing/GuaranteeBanner.tsx` (122 lines), `src/components/marketing/ValueTestimonials.tsx` (197 lines). No existing content/features removed (the internal money-back badge and compact guarantee line inside Pricing.tsx are preserved; the existing PageHeader/Comparison/TrustStrip/StatsBand/Newsletter/FinalCTA sections are all untouched).
- All rules respected: `"use client"` directives present in all 3 new components and preserved in edited Pricing.tsx (already had it); NO indigo/blue introduced — every accent uses existing `--accent-base`/`--accent-hover` tokens via `text-tj-accent`/`bg-tj-accent/15`/`text-gradient`/`border-tj-accent/30`; `tnum` applied to every numeric display (toggle "Pago único"/"One-time" label, "30 días"/"30 days" stat chip in GuaranteeBanner, "30" big number, "−468 €/año" value chip, "ROI ×100" value chip, star-rating staggered delays use `tnum` indirectly via card layout). ES/EN bilingual across all new copy via inline `es ? "..." : "..."` ternaries (matching the existing convention in FAQ.tsx / SocialProof.tsx / Comparison.tsx). All infinite animations (GuaranteeBanner shield pulse, ValueTestimonials star stagger-in is one-shot not infinite, PricingFAQ ParticleField drift) honor `prefers-reduced-motion` via `useReducedMotion` checks. Lint clean (exit 0). Build green (12/12 routes prerendered as static content).
- Next actions: (a) The GuaranteeBanner uses `-mt-4 md:-mt-6` to slightly overlap PageHeader's bottom padding — if a future spec changes PageHeader's `pb-16 md:pb-20` padding, the banner's negative margin may need to be re-tuned. (b) The visual-only pricing toggle has no state — if a future spec wants to actually offer a subscription tier, the toggle would need to be promoted to a real `useState`-driven control and the price cards would need to conditionally render annual/monthly prices; the current `role="radiogroup"` + `aria-checked` markup is forward-compatible (just add an `onClick` that flips a `mode` state and re-render the cards). (c) The ValueTestimonials testimonial set is intentionally distinct from SocialProof and TestimonialsWall — if a future spec wants to consolidate all testimonials into a single data source, the TESTIMONIALS arrays in those 3 files could be lifted into a shared `src/lib/trading/testimonials.ts` module; today they're intentionally duplicated so each section can have its own voice tuned to its context. (d) The PricingFAQ links to `/faq` via `<a href="/faq">` rather than `next/link` — this is intentional (it's a cross-section navigation, not a primary CTA), but could be upgraded to `Link` for client-side routing if preferred. (e) The "No disponible" caption under the disabled Suscripción pill uses `text-tertiary/70` opacity — if a future spec wants it more prominent, bump to `text-tertiary` or `text-secondary`. (f) The PricingFAQ's sticky-left heading column uses `lg:sticky lg:top-24` — the `top-24` (6rem) assumes the navbar is ~64px tall; if the navbar height changes, the sticky offset should be re-tuned to match.

Task ID: MP1-d
Agent: polish-demo

Work Log:
- Read worklog.md (2,270 lines, full project history through R6-b) and surveyed the demo page surface: `src/app/demo/page.tsx` (Server Component, currently 4 sections: PageHeader → AppDemoClient → StatsBand → FinalCTA). Inspected all reference patterns before writing: `Bento.tsx` (icon + glass card grid pattern, `bg-tj-accent/15 text-tj-accent` chip styling, hover `whileHover={{ y: -4 }}` spring 300/24), `Gallery.tsx` (FeatureImage + figure + caption overlay pattern with `asset()` wrapping), `FinalCTA.tsx` (MagneticButton + Reveal + aurora orbs + `text-gradient` headline pattern), `StatsBand.tsx`, `Pricing.tsx`/`features/page.tsx` routes confirmed for the ready-to-buy CTA hrefs, `globals.css` (verified `.glass`, `.chrome`, `.hairline`, `.eyebrow`, `.aurora-bg`, `.section-tight`, `.rounded-card`, `.text-gradient`, `.tnum` all exist as utility classes in `@layer components`), `i18n.tsx` (confirmed `useLang()` returns `lang: "es" | "en"`), `FeatureImage.tsx` (uses `next/image` `fill` so parent must be `relative` with defined size).
- Confirmed the 4 keyboard shortcuts the hint card advertises are all actually wired in the codebase: digit `1`–`6` switches tabs in `TopNav.tsx` (line 87–105, global listener, ignores Cmd/Ctrl/Alt), `F` toggles fullscreen in `AppDemo.tsx` (line 80–93, with meta/ctrl guard so it doesn't hijack browser find), `Esc` closes fullscreen (AppDemo.tsx line 42–55), `⌘K`/`Ctrl+K` opens the CommandPalette (`CommandPalette.tsx` line 43–54). So the hint card documents real behavior, not aspirational.
- Created **`src/components/demo/DemoCapabilities.tsx`** — 6-card grid living between PageHeader and the demo window. Grid: `grid-cols-2 md:grid-cols-3 gap-3` per spec. Each card: `glass rounded-card p-4 h-full flex flex-col gap-3` with an accent icon chip (`w-9 h-9 rounded-md bg-tj-accent/15 text-tj-accent`), title (`text-sm md:text-base font-semibold text-primary`), and 1-line description (`text-xs md:text-sm text-secondary`). Six inline SVG icons hand-drawn at 24×24 viewBox with `strokeWidth 1.6` to match the rest of the marketing iconography: FormIcon (document with lines + a filled dot, for "Log trades"), ChartIcon (5-bar ascending bar chart with a dot apex, for "Analyze 40+ metrics"), CalendarIcon (rect + 4 mini day-cells with varying opacity, for "Explore P&L calendar"), ShieldIcon (shield outline + check mark, for "Review your discipline"), BookIcon (open book with text lines + spine fold, for "Check your playbook"), PaletteIcon (painter's palette outline + 4 paint dots, for "Change theme and palette"). Each card has `whileHover={{ y: -3 }}` spring (300/24) for the same tactile lift as Bento cards, plus `hover:border-tj-accent/60` for the accent edge. Bilingual titles/descriptions via `useLang()` ternaries — no new i18n STR keys added (kept the surface area minimal and the copy self-contained). Section header is a centered eyebrow + h2 with `text-gradient` on "probar ahora mismo."/"try right now." to match the gradient-emphasis convention established across all marketing h2s.
- Created **`src/components/demo/DemoGallery.tsx`** — 8-image screenshot gallery below the demo, using `FeatureImage` from `@/components/tj/FeatureImage`. Grid: `md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-4 gap-4` per spec. Each cell is a `<motion.figure>` with `glass rounded-card overflow-hidden h-full flex flex-col group relative` and `whileHover={{ y: -4 }}` spring. Inside each figure: a `relative aspect-[4/3]` wrapper containing `FeatureImage` (with `className="absolute inset-0"` so the `next/image fill` anchors to the wrapper, `overlay={0.35}` so the images read clearly against the glass, and the first image gets `priority` for LCP), then a `<figcaption className="p-3 text-center">` with a single bilingual caption line. The 8 webp images in `/public/img/` are all wired via `asset()`: 01-hero-sculpture ("Disciplina, esculpida"/"Discipline, sculpted"), 02-feature-metrics ("Métricas institucionales"/"Institutional metrics"), 03-feature-equity-curve ("Curva de equity"/"Equity curve"), 04-feature-discipline-guardian ("Guardián de disciplina"/"Discipline guardian"), 05-feature-local-first ("100 % local"/"100 % local"), 06-feature-playbook ("Playbook en vivo"/"Live playbook"), 07-feature-multi-account ("Múltiples cuentas"/"Multi-account"), 08-cta-final-cinematic ("Cierre cinematográfico"/"Cinematic close"). Each image also has bilingual `alt` text for screen readers. A subtle `bg-tj-accent` glow at the top of the section (opacity 0.05, blur 140px) ties the gallery to the rest of the page's accent lighting.
- Created **`src/components/demo/DemoShortcutsHint.tsx`** — compact glass card (`glass rounded-card p-4 md:p-5 max-w-3xl mx-auto`) showing the 4 keyboard shortcuts as `<kbd>` keycaps. Each keycap is a `<Kbd>` helper component: `inline-flex items-center justify-center min-w-[1.75rem] h-7 px-2 rounded-md chrome border hairline border-solid text-[12px] font-medium text-secondary font-sans shadow-[0_1px_0_rgb(var(--tint)/0.5)]` — using `chrome` (the same utility as the demo window's chrome bars) so the keycaps read as physical keys pressed into the surface, and `font-sans` (NOT monospace, which would clash with the rest of the typography). The 4 shortcuts: `1 – 6` (with the en-dash rendered as a separator span, not a kbd, so it reads as a range), `F`, `Esc`, and `⌘ K` (two separate kbd elements since it's a chord). Each shortcut is followed by its bilingual label. Layout: flex column on mobile, `md:flex-row` on desktop with a `md:min-w-[200px]` left column holding a keyboard icon chip (`bg-tj-accent/15 text-tj-accent`) + the section title "Atajos de teclado"/"Keyboard shortcuts" + sublabel "Funcionan sobre la demo"/"Work while on the demo", then the shortcut list as a `flex flex-wrap gap-x-5 gap-y-3 flex-1` ul.
- Created **`src/components/demo/DemoReadyToBuy.tsx`** — compact CTA band for between StatsBand and FinalCTA. Headline: "¿Te gusta lo que ves?"/"Like what you see?" with `text-gradient` on "lo que ves?"/"what you see?". Subline: "Llévatelo a tu máquina. Pago único, sin suscripción, con garantía de 30 días."/"Take it to your machine. One-time payment, no subscription, with a 30-day guarantee." Two MagneticButtons: primary "Ver precios"/"See pricing" → `asset("/pricing")` with `btn-shimmer bg-tj-accent text-white` styling matching FinalCTA's primary CTA (so the visitor sees visual continuity between this catch-CTA and the closing one), and secondary "Explora características"/"Explore features" → `asset("/features")` with the `glass text-primary` styling. Both wrapped in `motion.div whileHover={{ scale: 1.03 }} whileTap={{ scale: 0.98 }}` for the same press-and-release feel as FinalCTA's buttons. Microcopy line below the buttons: "desde $29 · pago único · sin suscripción"/"from $29 · one-time · no subscription" — `$29` wrapped in `tnum` per the tabular-figures rule. Background layers: `aurora-bg opacity-60` + two soft accent orbs (top-left `w-[420px] h-[420px] blur-[120px] opacity-[0.10]`, bottom-right `w-[460px] h-[460px] blur-[120px] opacity-[0.08]`) — dimmer than FinalCTA's orbs (0.22) so this CTA doesn't compete with the cinematic closing one.
- Wired all 4 new components into **`src/app/demo/page.tsx`** in the requested order: `PageHeader` → `DemoCapabilities` (new, "what you can do") → `<section className="section scroll-mt-16"><AppDemoClient /></section>` (preserved) → `DemoShortcutsHint` (new, keyboard shortcuts) → `DemoGallery` (new, screenshots) → `StatsBand` (preserved) → `DemoReadyToBuy` (new, ready-to-buy CTA) → `FinalCTA` (preserved). The existing `<section className="section scroll-mt-16">` wrapper around AppDemoClient was preserved verbatim so the demo window's scroll-mt offset and section spacing are unchanged. The metadata object (title/description/openGraph) is untouched. No content was removed.
- **Verification**: `bun run lint` → exit 0, no errors or warnings (the 4 new files and the edited page.tsx all pass). `bun run build` → ✓ Compiled successfully in 10.5 s, 12/12 routes prerendered as static content (`/`, `/_not-found`, `/about`, `/demo`, `/faq`, `/features`, `/icon.svg`, `/manifest.webmanifest`, `/pricing`, `/robots.txt`, `/sitemap.xml`).

Stage Summary:
- Demo page polished with 4 new sections, all bilingual, all glass-themed, all using the existing accent-token system (NO indigo/blue introduced). (1) **DemoCapabilities**: 6-card grid above the demo window previewing what visitors can do — log trades, analyze 40+ metrics, explore P&L calendar, review discipline, check playbook, change theme & palette — each with a hand-drawn SVG icon and a 1-line description. Grid is `grid-cols-2 md:grid-cols-3 gap-3` per spec, each card `glass rounded-card p-4` with a `bg-tj-accent/15 text-tj-accent` icon chip and a hover lift. (2) **DemoShortcutsHint**: compact glass card below the demo with `<kbd>` keycaps (styled with `chrome` so they read as physical keys) documenting the 4 real shortcuts the demo responds to: `1–6` switch tab, `F` fullscreen, `Esc` close, `⌘K` command palette — all 4 are actually wired in the codebase (verified in TopNav.tsx, AppDemo.tsx, CommandPalette.tsx). (3) **DemoGallery**: 8-image screenshot gallery below the demo using the existing `FeatureImage` component, `grid md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-4 gap-4` per spec, each image in a `glass rounded-card overflow-hidden` figure with a bilingual caption, all 8 webp paths wrapped with `asset()` for basePath safety, first image `priority` for LCP. (4) **DemoReadyToBuy**: compact CTA band between StatsBand and FinalCTA with the headline "¿Te gusta lo que ves?"/"Like what you see?", two MagneticButtons (Pricing primary, Features secondary), and a price/payment microcopy line with `$29` in `tnum`.
- Files created: `src/components/demo/DemoCapabilities.tsx`, `src/components/demo/DemoGallery.tsx`, `src/components/demo/DemoShortcutsHint.tsx`, `src/components/demo/DemoReadyToBuy.tsx`. Files modified: `src/app/demo/page.tsx` (5 new section imports + 4 new section insertions, no existing content removed).
- All rules respected: no content/features removed (PageHeader, AppDemoClient, StatsBand, FinalCTA all preserved in their original positions); `"use client"` on all 4 new components (they use `useLang`, `motion`, `Reveal`, `FeatureImage`, `MagneticButton` — all client-only); NO indigo/blue — every accent uses `text-tj-accent`, `bg-tj-accent/15`, `text-gradient` (which derives from `--accent-base`), or `chrome` (a neutral token-derived surface); `tnum` applied on the single numeric display added (`$29` in DemoReadyToBuy microcopy). Lint clean (exit 0). Build green (12/12 routes prerendered as static content).
- Next actions: (a) The DemoCapabilities icons are inline SVGs in the component file — if the design system later grows a shared icon library (e.g. `lucide-react` already vendored by shadcn/ui), the 6 icons could be migrated to that library for consistency, but the hand-drawn versions match the existing Bento/Gallery icon style (24×24 viewBox, strokeWidth 1.6) so they're visually consistent today. (b) The DemoGallery uses `aspect-[4/3]` for every cell so the grid stays tidy — if a future spec wants a more magazine-like bento layout (some 2×2 hero cells), the `span` pattern from `Gallery.tsx` (which uses `md:col-span-2 md:row-span-2` on the first shot) could be adopted; the current uniform grid was chosen because the demo gallery is meant to be a quick visual reference, not a hero showcase. (c) The DemoShortcutsHint uses `font-sans` on `<kbd>` instead of the conventional monospace — this was a deliberate call to match the rest of the typography; if a future spec wants the keycaps to read more "computery", swap `font-sans` for `font-mono` (already available as a Tailwind utility). (d) The DemoReadyToBuy orbs are deliberately dimmer (opacity 0.08–0.10) than FinalCTA's (opacity 0.22) so this catch-CTA doesn't compete with the closing cinematic CTA — if A/B testing shows the catch-CTA underperforms, bumping the orbs to 0.14–0.16 would draw more attention without rework. (e) The DemoReadyToBuy "from $29" price is hardcoded in the component (not pulled from i18n STR) — if pricing changes, this needs to be updated in 3 places: here, `FinalCTA.tsx` (line 144: "desde $29"/"from $29"), and `Pricing.tsx`; a future refactor could centralize the starting price in `STR.startPrice` and read it from `useLang().t("startPrice")`.

Agent: polish-about

Work Log:
- Read `worklog.md` (tail), `src/app/about/page.tsx` (existing About page structure: PageHeader → Story → SocialProof → TestimonialsWall → Changelog → Newsletter → FinalCTA), `src/components/marketing/Story.tsx`, `src/components/marketing/Changelog.tsx`, `src/components/marketing/LocalFirst.tsx`, `src/components/marketing/SocialProof.tsx`, `src/components/tj/Eyebrow.tsx`, `src/components/tj/Reveal.tsx`, and `src/lib/i18n.tsx` (head) to absorb the design system: glass cards, `rounded-card`, `eyebrow`/`text-gradient`, `aurora-bg`, `divider-grad`, `tnum`, accent token `tj-accent` (gold/esmeralda — NO indigo/blue), ES/EN via `useLang()`, `Reveal`/`Eyebrow` primitives, motion patterns (spring hover lifts, staggered whileInView).
- Created **`src/components/marketing/Values.tsx`** — 4-card values grid (`grid md:grid-cols-2 gap-5`). Eyebrow "Principios"/"Principles", title "Lo que creemos."/"What we believe." with `text-gradient` on the verb. Cards: (01) Local siempre/Local always, (02) Pago único, no suscripción/One-time, not subscription, (03) Disciplina > métricas/Discipline > metrics, (04) Hecho por traders, para traders/Made by traders, for traders — each card `glass rounded-card p-6` with a left accent rule (grows on hover), a corner glow orb (fades in on hover), a glass icon chip (Lock/Coin/Shield/Compass — all inline SVGs, stroke=currentColor), a `tnum` "0X / 04" badge, title, and description. Hover lift via `motion.article whileHover={{ y: -4 }}` with spring transition. Footer coda: "No son eslóganes. Son decisiones de producto."/"Not slogans. Product decisions." with a `divider-grad` accent. Bilingual via `useLang()`.
- Created **`src/components/marketing/Milestones.tsx`** — horizontal scrollable timeline (5 milestones). Eyebrow "Trayectoria"/"Timeline", title "De idea a realidad."/"From idea to reality." with `text-gradient` on "realidad"/"reality". Milestones: Ene/Jan 2026 → v1.0 launch, Mar 2026 → Live playbook stats, May 2026 → Monte Carlo + Risk of ruin, Jul 2026 → Discipline Guardian, Próximo/Next → Rival importer. Past dots = solid `bg-tj-accent` with ring + glow shadow, springy scale-in. Upcoming dot = hollow `border-2 border-tj-accent bg-background` with infinite `animate-ping [animation-duration:2.4s]` halo (gated by `useReducedMotion()`). Connector line behind the dots uses an accent→muted gradient (70% accent → 25% at the right end) to visually signal "past → future". Rail is `overflow-x-auto` with `snap-x snap-mandatory` and `[scrollbar-width:thin] [scrollbar-color:rgb(var(--accent-base)/0.4)_transparent]`; cards are `w-[230px] md:w-auto md:flex-1` so it scrolls on mobile and stretches evenly on desktop. Each card: bilingual `tnum` date in accent, a "Hecho"/"Shipped" or "Próximo"/"Next" pill chip (accent vs. dashed neutral), title, and a `0X / 05` index. Footer legend explains the two dot states + a "Desliza para ver todo →"/"Scroll to see all →" hint on desktop.
- Wired both into **`src/app/about/page.tsx`**: added `import { Values }` and `import { Milestones }` (alphabetically slotted among existing marketing imports), placed `<Values />` immediately after `<Story />` (so principles sit right after the narrative) and `<Milestones />` immediately after `<Changelog />` (so the high-level timeline follows the detailed changelog). Final order: PageHeader → Story → **Values** → SocialProof → TestimonialsWall → Changelog → **Milestones** → Newsletter → FinalCTA. No existing content removed; metadata untouched.
- **Verification**: `bun run lint` → exit 0, no errors or warnings (the 2 new files and the edited page.tsx all pass). `bun run build` → ✓ Compiled successfully, 12/12 routes prerendered as static content (`/about` included).

Stage Summary:
- About page polished with 2 new bilingual sections, both glass-themed, both using the existing accent-token system (NO indigo/blue introduced). (1) **Values**: 2×2 grid of principles — Local always, One-time payment, Discipline > metrics, Made by traders — each card with a number badge (01–04, `tnum`), a glass icon chip, title, and the spec's exact ES/EN descriptions. Hover lifts the card 4px and grows the left accent rule; a soft accent corner-glow fades in. (2) **Milestones**: horizontal timeline of 5 product milestones from Jan 2026 (v1.0) → Next (Rival importer), past milestones marked with solid accent dots that spring-pop in, the upcoming one with a hollow ring + slow `animate-ping` halo (suppressed under `prefers-reduced-motion`). Connector line is an accent→muted gradient. Rail scrolls horizontally with snap on mobile, stretches evenly on desktop; each milestone card carries a `tnum` date, a Hecho/Shipped or Próximo/Next chip, the title, and a `0X / 05` index.
- Files created: `src/components/marketing/Values.tsx`, `src/components/marketing/Milestones.tsx`. Files modified: `src/app/about/page.tsx` (2 new section imports + 2 new section insertions, no existing content removed).
- All rules respected: `"use client"` on both new components (they use `useLang`, `motion`, `Reveal`, `useReducedMotion` — all client-only); NO indigo/blue — every accent uses `text-tj-accent`, `bg-tj-accent/15`, `text-gradient`, or `aurora-bg` (all derived from the gold/esmeralda `--accent-base` token); `tnum` applied on all numeric displays (value numbers 01–04, milestone dates, "0X / 04" and "0X / 05" indices, the "04" totals). `useReducedMotion()` honored in Milestones (the ping halo is suppressed when the user prefers reduced motion). Lint clean (exit 0). Build green (12/12 routes prerendered as static content, `/about` included).
- Next actions: (a) The Values icon set (Lock/Coin/Shield/Compass) is hand-drawn inline SVG matching the existing LocalFirst/Bento icon style (18×18 viewBox, strokeWidth 1.3); if the design system later adopts `lucide-react` (already vendored by shadcn/ui), these 4 could be migrated for library consistency, but the current set is visually harmonious today. (b) The Milestones rail uses native `overflow-x-auto` with `snap-x snap-mandatory`; on very wide viewports the rail stretches evenly (`md:flex-1`) which is the desired desktop behavior — if a future spec wants drag-to-scroll on desktop, a `motion.div` drag handler with `dragConstraints` referencing `railRef` could be added (the ref is already wired for future use). (c) The "Próximo"/"Next" milestone is intentionally styled identically to the Changelog section's "Próximo" chip (dashed border, `text-tertiary`) so the two timeline sections read as one system — if a future spec wants to differentiate them (e.g. Milestones uses a softer "En camino"/"On the way" label), the chip text is a one-line change. (d) The Values footer coda and the Milestones footer legend are both optional polish — if the page feels too dense, both can be removed without breaking layout (they're the last child of each section's inner container). (e) The connector line in Milestones is a static CSS gradient (not scroll-driven) — if a future spec wants the line to "fill" as the rail is scrolled (à la Story's `useScroll`/`useTransform` timeline fill), `useScroll({ target: railRef, axis: "x" })` + `useTransform(scrollXProgress, [0,1], ["0%", "100%"])` on a `motion.span` width would do it; left as a static gradient for now because the rail is short (5 items) and the gradient already conveys past→future.

---
Task ID: MP2-c
Agent: page-transitions
Task: Add smooth page transitions + scroll restoration + loading states

Work Log:
- Read `worklog.md` (tail) and audited the existing multi-page structure: 6 routes (`/`, `/features`, `/pricing`, `/demo`, `/about`, `/faq`), each using `PageHeader` (pt-32 pb-16 / md:pt-40 md:pb-20 ≈ h-64 to h-80 hero) followed by content sections. Inspected `src/app/layout.tsx` (root layout with Navbar/Footer/CommandPalette/etc. client overlays inside `<Providers>`), `src/app/globals.css` (existing global `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` rule at L462 that clamps all animation/transition durations to 0.01ms — so the new page-enter animation is automatically honored there, plus an explicit override for belt-and-suspenders), `src/app/loading.tsx` (existing root loading state — framer-motion accent dots, kept untouched), `src/components/tj/Skeleton.tsx` (server-safe `Skeleton`/`SkeletonText`/`SkeletonCard` primitives with optional `shimmer` accent sweep), `src/components/tj/BackToTop.tsx` (existing scroll-to-top floating button — reference for the project's scroll-handling conventions), and `src/lib/i18n.tsx` (`useLang` is a client hook — so per-route loading.tsx files stay Server Components and avoid the i18n dependency to stream instantly).
- Created **`src/app/template.tsx`** — Server Component that wraps every routed page in `<div className="page-enter">{children}</div>`. In Next.js App Router, `template.tsx` (unlike `layout.tsx`) is re-mounted on every navigation, so the `.page-enter` CSS animation re-runs each route change — a soft fade + 8px slide-up. No hooks, no client JS, no Framer Motion (CSS-only → zero added JS bundle).
- Added the **page-enter CSS animation** to `src/app/globals.css` (inserted between the `.no-scrollbar` utility and the SHIMMER section): `@keyframes page-enter { from { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(8px); } to { opacity: 1; transform: translateY(0); } }`, `.page-enter { animation: page-enter 0.35s cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1); }` (the spec's `[0.22, 1, 0.36, 1]` array is the same "soft settle" easing used across the site's Framer Motion transitions — translated to the CSS `cubic-bezier()` form), plus an explicit `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .page-enter { animation: none; } }` override for belt-and-suspenders (the global reduced-motion rule at L462 already clamps durations, but `animation: none` is more decisive).
- Created **`src/components/tj/ScrollToTop.tsx`** — Client Component (`"use client"`) that listens to `usePathname()` and calls `window.scrollTo({ top: 0, left: 0, behavior: "auto" })` on every pathname change. Uses `behavior: "auto"` (instant) rather than `"smooth"` to avoid fighting the browser's native scroll-restoration tween on back/forward. Renders nothing — pure side-effect. Note: Next.js App Router restores scroll automatically for browser back/forward; this component guarantees a "start at the top" feel on forward navigations too (which the App Router default does as well, but the explicit component is robust against future route-level `scroll={false}` on `next/link` and against edge cases with hash anchors / scroll-restored layouts).
- Created **`src/components/tj/PageSkeleton.tsx`** — shared, server-safe branded loading skeleton. Renders a PageHeader-sized hero skeleton (breadcrumb → eyebrow → 2-line large title with shimmer → 2-line subtitle) followed by a responsive card grid (`grid gap-6 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3`) of `SkeletonCard`s. Configurable `cards` count (default 3) so each route can tune the body weight. Uses only the existing `Skeleton`/`SkeletonCard` primitives — no new visual system, fully consistent with the design tokens. The hero block reuses the exact `PageHeader` rhythm (pt-32 pb-16 md:pt-40 md:pb-20, max-w-page, px-5 md:px-8) so the loading state is visually isomorphic with the loaded page — no layout shift on transition.
- Created 5 per-route **`loading.tsx`** files, each a 1-line Server Component importing `PageSkeleton` with a tuned `cards` count: `src/app/features/loading.tsx` (cards=3), `src/app/pricing/loading.tsx` (cards=3, mirrors the 3-tier plan grid), `src/app/demo/loading.tsx` (cards=2 — the demo page is heavy with the AppDemo client bundle + charts, so a lighter skeleton streams faster), `src/app/about/loading.tsx` (cards=3), `src/app/faq/loading.tsx` (cards=4 — mirrors the FAQ accordion list). All keep the loading state lightweight (server-only, no hooks, no Framer Motion) per Next.js best practices for `loading.tsx` files.
- Edited **`src/app/layout.tsx`**: imported `ScrollToTop` from `@/components/tj/ScrollToTop` (slotted alphabetically after `CursorGlow`), and added `<ScrollToTop />` inside `<Providers>` alongside the other client overlay components (CommandPalette, GlobalShortcuts, ShortcutsHelp, ReadingProgress, ThemeTransition, CursorGlow) — placed immediately before `<Navbar />` so it's wired before the page content renders. Added an inline comment explaining why (Next.js handles back/forward automatically; this guarantees forward-nav scroll-to-top). Did NOT add `scroll={true}` to `<html>` — verified that `scroll` is not a valid prop on Next.js's `<html>` element (it's a `next/link` prop); Next.js App Router's scroll restoration is enabled by default and requires no opt-in. No existing content removed; metadata/schema/FOUC-script all untouched.
- **Verification**: `bun run lint` → exit 0, no errors or warnings across all 8 new/modified files. `bun run build` → ✓ Compiled successfully in 10.7s, 12/12 routes prerendered as static content (`/`, `/_not-found`, `/about`, `/demo`, `/faq`, `/features`, `/icon.svg`, `/manifest.webmanifest`, `/pricing`, `/robots.txt`, `/sitemap.xml`). All 5 new `loading.tsx` files are recognized by Next.js (they're auto-wired into the App Router's streaming Suspense boundary per-route — visible in the build output as the same route count, since loading.tsx files don't add new routes).

Stage Summary:
- Smooth page transitions wired via `template.tsx` (re-mounts per navigation) + a CSS-only `.page-enter` fade/slide (0.35s, cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1) — the site's signature "soft settle" easing) — zero added JS bundle, fully honored by `prefers-reduced-motion` (both the global duration clamp and an explicit `animation: none` override).
- Scroll restoration: Next.js App Router's automatic back/forward restoration left intact (no opt-in needed; `scroll={true}` on `<html>` is not a real Next.js prop — verified and documented inline), plus an explicit `<ScrollToTop />` client component in the root layout that calls `window.scrollTo(0,0)` on every `usePathname()` change to guarantee forward-navigations start at the top.
- Per-route loading states: 5 new `loading.tsx` files (features/pricing/demo/about/faq), each a server-only 1-liner delegating to a shared `PageSkeleton` component that mirrors the `PageHeader` hero rhythm + a responsive card grid. Lightweight (no hooks, no Framer Motion) so they stream instantly while the route's real content/code loads — especially important for `/demo` whose AppDemo client bundle is heavy.
- Files created (8): `src/app/template.tsx`, `src/components/tj/ScrollToTop.tsx`, `src/components/tj/PageSkeleton.tsx`, `src/app/features/loading.tsx`, `src/app/pricing/loading.tsx`, `src/app/demo/loading.tsx`, `src/app/about/loading.tsx`, `src/app/faq/loading.tsx`. Files modified (2): `src/app/globals.css` (+24 lines: page-enter keyframes + utility + reduced-motion override), `src/app/layout.tsx` (+1 import, +1 component render + 4-line comment). No content/features removed.
- All rules respected: `"use client"` only on `ScrollToTop.tsx` (the one component using `usePathname`/`useEffect`); `template.tsx`, `PageSkeleton.tsx`, and all 5 `loading.tsx` files are Server Components (no hooks, no client JS) — they stream as part of the server-rendered HTML and add zero client bundle. ES/EN bilingual: the loading skeletons are language-agnostic (pure shapes), so they don't need i18n — they render identically in both locales, and the actual page content (which IS bilingual via `useLang`) streams in afterward. Lint clean (exit 0). Build green (12/12 routes prerendered as static content).
- Next actions: (a) The root `src/app/loading.tsx` (framer-motion accent dots, full-screen) is intentionally left untouched — it serves as the initial-load fallback for the very first navigation to `/`. The new per-route `loading.tsx` files take precedence for in-app navigations to `/features`, `/pricing`, `/demo`, `/about`, `/faq`. If a future spec wants the root loading state to also use `PageSkeleton` for consistency, swap the body of `src/app/loading.tsx` to `<PageSkeleton cards={3} />` — but the current full-screen accent-dots loader is a nicer "first paint" brand moment for cold loads, so left as-is. (b) The page-enter animation is currently CSS-only (no exit animation). If a future spec wants exit animations too (e.g. fade-out before navigation), that requires moving to a client-side transition layer — either `next/navigation`'s `useRouter` with a manual exit-then-push pattern, or wrapping the template in `<AnimatePresence mode="wait">` (Framer Motion). Both add client JS and complicate scroll restoration; the CSS-only entrance is the right tradeoff for now. (c) The `ScrollToTop` uses `behavior: "auto"` (instant jump). If a future spec wants a smooth scroll-to-top on navigation, change to `behavior: "smooth"` — but note that "smooth" on every navigation can feel sluggish and conflicts with the page-enter animation's perceived "fresh page" feel. Left as instant. (d) The `PageSkeleton` `cards` counts (3/3/2/3/4) were tuned to mirror each route's actual content weight; if a route's layout changes significantly (e.g. Pricing adds a 4th plan), bump its `cards` prop to match. (e) No hash-anchor handling added to `ScrollToTop` — if a future spec adds in-page anchor links (e.g. `/faq#refund-policy`), the component will currently scroll to top on the pathname change and override the hash. To support hash anchors, add a `useHash()` check or compare `window.location.hash` before scrolling; left simple for now since the current routes don't use hash navigation.

Task ID: MP2-d
Agent: cmdk-pages
Task: Update Command Palette for multi-page navigation + add page search

Work Log:
- Read worklog.md, existing CommandPalette.tsx (anchor-link based), asset.ts, theme.tsx, Navbar.tsx (already multi-page with usePathname), and the 6 app/*/page.tsx files to enumerate section IDs (features exposes 11 anchors: disciplina, galeria, guardian, coste, transformacion, como-funciona, riesgo, mas-features, playbook, local, integraciones; home exposes hero, features-preview, stats, cta; demo/pricing/about/faq have no anchorable sub-sections).
- Rebuilt CommandPalette.tsx around a single `PAGES` source-of-truth array: each entry has `{ path, es, en, sections? }`. All 6 routes are listed (/ / /features / /demo / /pricing / /about / /faq) with their sub-sections attached.
- Replaced the old `goTo(href)` smooth-scroll helper with a `navigate(path, sectionId?)` that branches on the current `usePathname()`:
  * Same page → `requestAnimationFrame` + `scrollIntoView({behavior:"smooth"})` (or `scrollTo({top:0})` when no section), so no reload.
  * Cross-page → `window.location.href = asset(path[#section])` — the correct primitive for `output:"export"` on GitHub Pages; the `#section` hash makes the browser jump to the section after the new page loads. `asset()` correctly preserves the hash when splicing the basePath in front.
- Added a "Recently visited / Visto recientemente" group at the top of the palette. Implementation:
  * `recent: string[]` state initialized empty, loaded from `localStorage["tj-recent-pages"]` once on mount (canonical SSR-safe localStorage init pattern, mirrors `ThemeProvider`).
  * A `useEffect([pathname])` pushes the current path to the front, dedupes, caps at 3, and persists back to localStorage. Only paths present in `PAGES` are tracked (no 404s).
  * The group is rendered only when `recentItems.length > 0`; labels are looked up from `PAGES` so ES/EN stays consistent with the rest of the palette.
  * Wrapped both effects in `/* eslint-disable react-hooks/set-state-in-effect */` (same convention as `theme.tsx`) — the load effect is the documented SSR-safe init pattern, and the track effect is intentionally syncing an external system (browser URL) into React state.
- Added a new "Sections / Secciones" CommandGroup that flattens every page's sub-sections into individual commands. Each item's `value` includes the section's ES+EN labels + the section ID + the parent page's ES+EN labels + the literal phrases "ir a sección" / "go to section" so cmdk search hits from any direction. The shortcut chip renders `{path}#{id}` (e.g. `/features#disciplina`) with `tnum`.
- Updated the "Buy Pro" action from `goTo("#pricing")` to `navigate("/pricing")` — preserves the feature, just retargeted to the new dedicated Pricing route.
- Preserved every existing feature verbatim: theme toggle (`toggleTheme`), accent-palette switcher (5 `PALETTES` with bilingual labels + ✓ on current), language toggle (`toggleLang`), Cmd+K/Ctrl+K global shortcut, capture-phase Escape-to-close, footer kbd hint row, `itemClass` accent-selected styling, bilingual copy derived once from `navigator.language` (default ES), all inline icons.
- Added two new inline icons (`HashIcon` for sections, `ClockIcon` for recents) in the same 16×16 currentColor style as the existing icons. No indigo/blue; numbers in shortcut chips stay `tnum`.
- `usePathname()` returns the path WITHOUT basePath in Next.js 16, so the same-page detection (`pathname === path`) works correctly under `basePath: "/JournalTradingWeb"`.
- Verification: `bun run lint` → exit 0, no errors/warnings. `bun run build` → ✓ Compiled successfully in 10.9s, all 12 routes prerendered as static content (`/`, `/_not-found`, `/about`, `/demo`, `/faq`, `/features`, `/pricing`, `/icon.svg`, `/manifest.webmanifest`, `/robots.txt`, `/sitemap.xml`). One transient `_buildManifest.js.tmp` ENOENT on the first attempt (known Turbopack flake) — resolved by `rm -rf .next && sleep 2 && bun run build`.

Stage Summary:
- CommandPalette is now multi-page aware: 6 navigation commands (one per route) + 15 sub-section commands (4 home + 11 features) + dynamic "Recently visited" (up to 3) + theme/palette/language toggles + Buy Pro → /pricing.
- Navigation uses `window.location.href = asset(path[#id])` for cross-page (static-export-correct) and smooth in-page scroll for same-page targets — no Next Router needed (and Router would actually be wrong under static export with full-page reloads between routes).
- Recents persist in `localStorage["tj-recent-pages"]` (max 3, most-recent-first, deduped), tracked via `usePathname()` so every page visit is recorded automatically.
- All existing features retained; lint clean; build clean; no indigo/blue; `tnum` on all numeric/path shortcut chips.

Task ID: MP2-b
Agent: polish-faq
Task: Polish FAQ page + add contact section + support options

Work Log:
- Read worklog.md, faq/page.tsx, FAQ.tsx, FinalCTA.tsx, Newsletter.tsx, GuaranteeBanner.tsx, Eyebrow/Reveal primitives, i18n.tsx, ui/{input,textarea,button}.tsx and globals.css (`.section`, `.section-tight`, `.glass`, `.hairline`, `.rounded-card`, `.tnum`, accent tokens) to lock onto the established design system before writing any new code.
- Created `/src/components/marketing/ContactSupport.tsx` ("use client"): bilingual section with eyebrow "Soporte"/"Support", H2 "¿No encuentras tu respuesta?"/"Can't find your answer?" (accent gradient on the second clause), and a `grid md:grid-cols-3 gap-4` of three `glass rounded-card p-6` cards. Cards: (1) Email — soporte@tradingjournal.app — "Respuesta en 24h"/"Reply in 24h" — Mail icon — mailto: link; (2) Documentación/Documentation — "Guías y tutoriales"/"Guides and tutorials" — BookOpen icon — href "#"; (3) Comunidad/Community — "Discord + Telegram" — MessagesSquare icon — href "#". Each card: accent icon chip (ring-1 ring-tj-accent/25), title, description, optional uppercase accent meta, and a CTA row with ArrowRight that nudges on hover. Hover lift via `motion.a` `whileHover={{ y: -4 }}` (soft spring, reduced-motion safe) plus a top-edge accent sweep that fades in on hover. Low-opacity ParticleField for ambient depth. Icons from lucide-react.
- Created `/src/components/marketing/ContactForm.tsx` ("use client"): compact contact form in `glass rounded-card p-6 max-w-xl mx-auto`. Fields: name (text, autoComplete="name"), email (type=email, inputMode=email, regex `/^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/`), message (textarea, rows=4, resize-y). Inputs styled exactly like the rest of the app: `bg-white/5 border hairline rounded-md h-11 px-3 text-sm text-primary placeholder:text-tertiary outline-none hover:border-tj-accent/40 focus-visible:border-tj-accent/60`. Each field wrapped in a small `<Field>` with an uppercase tracking label. Submit button "Enviar"/"Send" — `bg-tj-accent text-white hover:bg-tj-accent-hover` with accent-glow shadow on hover and `whileTap` scale. Validation: required fields + email regex; on error an animated red helper (text-pnl-neg) lists the offending fields. On success: AnimatePresence cross-fades the form out and an animated SVG checkmark (circle + path drawn via pathLength, accent green) in with the success copy "✓ Mensaje enviado. Te responderemos en 24h." / "✓ Message sent. We'll reply in 24h." Non-functional (visual only). `min-h-[360px]` keeps the card height stable across the form/success swap. Added a small privacy footnote under the button.
- Created `/src/components/marketing/StillHaveQuestions.tsx` ("use client"): small transitional banner between FAQ and ContactSupport. `glass rounded-card p-6 md:p-7` centered with "¿Aún tienes dudas? Estamos aquí para ayudarte." / "Still have questions? We're here to help." (second clause in `text-tj-accent`). Subtle accent glow: a radial gradient seated at the top edge (`radial-gradient(60% 120% at 50% 0%, rgb(var(--accent-base)/0.18) 0%, transparent 60%)`) plus a thin animated accent sweep line that draws in on view (`scaleX 0→1`, opacity 0→0.9). Reduced-motion-safe hover lift. Tight padding (`py-8 md:py-10`) so it reads as a connector, not a full section.
- Edited `/src/app/faq/page.tsx`: added imports for StillHaveQuestions, ContactSupport, ContactForm and wired them in this order between `<FAQ />` and `<FinalCTA />`: `<FAQ />` → `<StillHaveQuestions />` → `<ContactSupport />` → `<ContactForm />` → `<FinalCTA />`. Page stays a server component (only metadata + composition); all new interactivity is encapsulated in the client components.
- Ran `bun run lint` (clean — 0 errors, 0 warnings across the whole repo) and `bun run build` (clean — `/faq` prerendered as static content, 12/12 pages generated, compiled in 10.7s). Initial build hit a stale `.next/lock` + tmp-manifest race from a concurrent process; resolved by `rm -rf .next` and rebuilding cleanly.

Stage Summary:
- FAQ page now flows: PageHeader → FAQ accordion (with search + glossary) → StillHaveQuestions transitional banner (accent glow) → ContactSupport (3 contact-option cards with hover lift) → ContactForm (validated name/email/message with animated success state) → FinalCTA.
- 3 new files: ContactSupport.tsx, ContactForm.tsx, StillHaveQuestions.tsx — all "use client", all bilingual via `useLang()`, all using the existing `glass`/`rounded-card`/`hairline`/`Eyebrow`/`Reveal`/`ParticleField` primitives and accent-only color palette (no indigo/blue).
- ContactSupport cards: Email (mailto:), Documentation (#), Community (#) — each with icon chip, title, description, accent meta/CTA, hover lift + accent sweep.
- ContactForm: required-field + email-regex validation, animated error helper, animated SVG checkmark success state with the exact prescribed copy, stable card height via min-h.
- StillHaveQuestions: subtle accent glow (radial + animated sweep) bridging FAQ and ContactSupport.
- Lint clean (0/0). Build clean. No indigo/blue. `tnum` not needed (no numeric figures introduced). All iconography from lucide-react (Mail, BookOpen, MessagesSquare, ArrowRight).

  - Single `glass rounded-card p-6 md:p-8` surface containing a `grid grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 gap-x-10` of 8 spec rows. Each row is a `<motion.dl>` (semantics-first per spec) with `<dt>` (label, `text-tertiary text-[11px] uppercase tracking-[0.14em] font-semibold`) and `<dd>` (value, `text-primary text-sm font-medium leading-snug`).
  - Spec rows exactly as specified: Plataforma/Platform → "Windows 10/11 (64-bit)"; Almacenamiento/Storage → "SQLite local (~5MB por 1000 operaciones)" / "Local SQLite (~5MB per 1000 trades)"; RAM → "< 200MB en uso" / "< 200MB in use"; Importación/Import → "CSV universal, mapeo de columnas" / "Universal CSV, column mapping"; Exportación/Export → "PDF, CSV, JSON"; Idiomas/Languages → "Español + Inglés" / "Spanish + English"; Actualizaciones/Updates → "Gratis para siempre" / "Free forever"; Privacidad/Privacy → "100% local, sin telemetry" / "100% local, no telemetry".
  - Border-bottom rhythm via `py-3.5 border-b hairline border-solid` per row; last row drops border with `border-b-0`, second-to-last row additionally drops it on `sm+` via `sm:border-b-0` (computed in JS from index, not arbitrary variants, so the last two cells — which sit side-by-side in the final 2-column row — both end cleanly without a dangling divider). Staggered motion reveal with delay `(i % 2) * 0.06 + Math.floor(i / 2) * 0.04` for a left-to-right, top-to-bottom wave.
- Wired into `/src/app/features/page.tsx`: added imports for `MetricsDeepDive` and `TechSpecs`; inserted `<div id="metricas" className="scroll-mt-32"><MetricsDeepDive /></div>` between `#como-funciona` (HowItWorks) and `#riesgo` (RiskTool); inserted `<div id="tecnico" className="scroll-mt-32"><TechSpecs /></div>` between `#local` (LocalFirst) and `#integraciones` (Integrations). Anchor IDs + `scroll-mt-32` match the existing section-wrapper pattern so smooth-scroll from SectionNav lands the heading cleanly under the sticky Navbar+SectionNav stack.
- Updated `/src/components/layout/SectionNav.tsx` `FEATURE_SECTIONS` (the canonical list driving both the sticky pills AND the YouAreHere IntersectionObserver-driven active indicator): inserted `{ id: "metricas", labelEs: "Métricas", labelEn: "Metrics" }` after `como-funciona` and `{ id: "tecnico", labelEs: "Técnico", labelEn: "Technical" }` after `local`. Pill count goes from 11 → 13; horizontal `overflow-x-auto no-scrollbar` strip handles the extra width cleanly on mobile.
- Ran `bun run lint` — clean (eslint . exits 0, no output beyond `$ eslint .`).
- Ran `bun run build` — clean. Compiled successfully, 12/12 static pages generated (901ms), `/features` prerendered as static content, no warnings or errors.

Stage Summary:
- 2 new files: `MetricsDeepDive.tsx`, `TechSpecs.tsx` — both "use client", both bilingual via `useLang()`, both using existing `glass`/`rounded-pill`/`rounded-card`/`hairline`/`Eyebrow`/`Reveal` primitives and accent-only palette (no indigo/blue). `tnum` applied to the "40+" chip and the per-category 2-digit count.
- MetricsDeepDive: 4 category blocks (Performance 6, Risk 6, Quality 6, Discipline 4 = 22 metric pills) each in a `grid grid-cols-2 md:grid-cols-4 gap-2`; pills styled identically to SectionNav pills (`glass rounded-pill px-3 py-1.5 text-xs`); category headers with monogram chip + uppercase label + hairline divider + tnum count.
- TechSpecs: single `glass rounded-card p-6 md:p-8` surface with 8 `<dl>` rows in a `sm:grid-cols-2` layout; each row is `<dt>` (tertiary label) + `<dd>` (primary value); last-row border cleanup handled in JS (last + second-to-last) so both cells in the final 2-col row end cleanly.
- Features page flow now: PageHeader → SectionNav → Bento → Gallery → Guardian → MistakeInvoice → BeforeAfter → HowItWorks → **MetricsDeepDive (NEW)** → RiskTool → MoreFeatures → Wrapped → LocalFirst → **TechSpecs (NEW)** → Integrations → FinalCTA → YouAreHere.
- SectionNav FEATURE_SECTIONS grown from 11 → 13 entries; both new sections get pills ("Métricas"/"Metrics" and "Técnico"/"Technical") and are tracked by the YouAreHere active-section indicator.
- Lint: clean (eslint . passes silently). Build: clean (12/12 static pages, /features prerendered, no warnings). No indigo/blue introduced — all accent usage via `tj-accent`/`accent-base` tokens. No new i18n keys needed (all copy inline-bilingually via `es ? ... : ...` pattern, matching HowItWorks/RiskTool/LocalFirst convention).

Task ID: F1
Agent: fix-framer-colors
Task: Fix all Framer Motion color animation warnings

Work Log:
- Read worklog.md, then swept `src/` for Framer Motion color animation issues via ripgrep:
  - `backgroundColor.*var(--` in motion props
  - `(animate|whileHover|whileInView|initial|exit|whileTap)=\{\{[\s\S]*?var\(--`
  - `(animate|whileHover|whileInView|initial|exit|whileTap)=\{\{[\s\S]*?(backgroundColor|boxShadow|borderColor|outlineColor|textDecorationColor)`
  - Cross-checked every `backgroundColor`/`boxShadow` occurrence (incl. `style` props) to confirm which ones are *animated* vs *static*.
- Found exactly **2 files** with truly animated colors using CSS variables (all other matches are static `style={{ ... }}` or static SVG attributes like `stroke="rgb(var(--accent-base))"`, which Framer Motion does not interpolate):
  1. `src/components/marketing/FeatureRotator.tsx` (1 instance): `motion.span` dot with `animate={{ width, backgroundColor: active ? "rgb(var(--accent-base))" : "rgb(var(--accent-base) / 0.28)" }}` — exactly matches the console warning in the task brief.
  2. `src/components/marketing/Bento.tsx` (5 instances): each card's `whileHover={{ y: -4 (or -3), boxShadow: "0 12px 36px -16px rgb(var(--accent-base) / 0.55)" }}` — animating `boxShadow` to a CSS-variable color triggers the same warning class.
- Verified the task's named suspects so the report is exhaustive:
  - `Comparison.tsx` pulsing accent glow — `boxShadow` and `background` are static `style` props; only `opacity` is animated → **no fix needed**.
  - `AppDemo.tsx` page transition shimmer — `background` is a static `style` linear-gradient string; the `motion.div` only animates `y` and `opacity` → **no fix needed**.
  - `JournalPage.tsx` traffic light — uses concrete color `l.color`, only animates `opacity` and `scale` → **no fix needed**.
  - `SettingsPage.tsx` palette swatches — uses concrete `p.dark`/`p.light`, only animates `opacity` → **no fix needed**.
  - `HeroEquity.tsx`, `EquityCurve.tsx`, `SettingsPage.tsx` SVG paths with `stroke="rgb(var(--accent-base))"` — `stroke`/`fill` are static attributes; only `pathLength`/`opacity`/`r` are animated → **no fix needed** (matches the "static stroke is fine" rule in the task brief).

Fix 1 — `FeatureRotator.tsx` (carousel dot):
- Replaced `backgroundColor: active ? "rgb(var(--accent-base))" : "rgb(var(--accent-base) / 0.28)"` with `opacity: active ? 1 : 0.28`.
- Added static `bg-tj-accent` class to the dot so the fill color is concrete and resolved by CSS (Framer Motion only touches numeric `width` and `opacity` now).
- Visually identical: the previous 28%-alpha accent fill over a transparent button background is equivalent to solid accent at opacity 0.28 over the same background. Added an explanatory code comment.

Fix 2 — `Bento.tsx` (5 marketing cards: Métricas, Playbook, Local, Calendar, CSV import):
- Removed `boxShadow: "0 12px 36px -16px rgb(var(--accent-base) / 0.55)"` from each `whileHover` prop (kept the `y: -4`/`y: -3` lift).
- Added Tailwind arbitrary-value class `hover:shadow-[0_12px_36px_-16px_rgb(var(--accent-base)/0.55)]` to each card's `className` (this is the same pattern already used in `MoreFeatures.tsx` and `TradeDetailPage.tsx`, so the build already accepts it).
- Swapped `transition-colors` for `transition-[border-color,box-shadow] duration-300` on each card so the new hover shadow animates smoothly alongside the existing `hover:border-tj-accent/60` (matches the existing `GlossaryModal.tsx` `transition-[border-color,box-shadow,background-color]` convention).
- The Framer Motion `transition={{ type: "spring", stiffness: 300, damping: 24 }}` is preserved untouched — it still governs the `y`-axis spring lift; the CSS transition only governs border-color + box-shadow, no conflict.

Stage Summary:
- **Files changed: 2** (`src/components/marketing/FeatureRotator.tsx`, `src/components/marketing/Bento.tsx`).
- **Animation prop edits: 6** (1 `animate` backgroundColor → opacity swap, 5 `whileHover` boxShadow removals + matching className shadow class additions).
- No features removed; no `"use client"` directives changed; no new imports; no indigo/blue introduced.
- Lint: `bun run lint` passes silently (eslint . → no errors, no warnings).
- Type-check: `bunx tsc --noEmit` reports zero errors in `src/components/marketing/FeatureRotator.tsx` and `src/components/marketing/Bento.tsx` (only unrelated errors remain in `examples/` and `skills/` directories, which are outside `src/`).
- Build: `next build --webpack` compiles successfully in 24.4s ("✓ Compiled successfully"). The Turbopack default build is being killed by the sandbox OOM-killer (3.9 GiB RAM, 0 swap) before completing — an environmental constraint, not a code issue. The webpack build's only failure is a pre-existing prerender error on `/_global-error` (`useLang must be used within LanguageProvider`), which exists independently of these Framer Motion fixes and is outside this task's scope.
- The two specific console warnings quoted in the task brief — `'rgb(var(--accent-base))' is not an animatable value` (FeatureRotator dot) and `'rgb(var(--accent-base) / 0.28)' is not an animatable color` (same dot, inactive state) — are eliminated because Framer Motion no longer attempts to interpolate `rgb(var(--accent-base))` or any alpha variant; it now only animates numeric `opacity`. The Bento `whileHover` `boxShadow` warnings are also eliminated because the shadow is now applied via a static CSS class, not interpolated by Framer Motion.
- Recommended next step (out of scope): fix the `/_global-error` prerender by wrapping any `useLang` call in a `LanguageProvider` boundary, or by giving the global error page a lang-independent fallback. Also consider upgrading the dev sandbox to ≥6 GiB RAM so the Turbopack build can complete.
