Task ID: D14
Agent: general-purpose (R3 accessibility + ARIA pass)
Task: Verify accessibility + ARIA compliance across the site

Work Log:
- Read worklog.md (4756 lines) to absorb prior context. The site is Round 2 polish shipped (D10 commit `aece7b2` on origin/main): trading-journal marketing site + live demo built on Next.js 16 + Tailwind v4, dark-default ónix palette, ES/EN bilingual via `useLang()`, 12 static routes. The R3 task is to verify accessibility + ARIA: images alt, button names, input labels, `aria-current` on navbar active links, `aria-expanded` on mobile menu, `role="tablist"/"tab"/"tabpanel"` on the demo TopNav, focus-visible ring visibility, skip-to-content link.

AUDIT FINDINGS (8-item checklist):
1. **Images have alt text** ✓ — No raw `<img>` tags anywhere in `src/`. All raster imagery flows through `next/image`'s `<Image>` component. `FeatureImage.tsx` requires `alt` as a prop. Callers all pass explicit bilingual alt strings:
   • `Gallery.tsx` (5 shots) — `shot.alt` ES/EN strings, e.g. "Visualización abstracta del panel de trading con métricas institucionales" / "Abstract visualization of the trading dashboard with institutional metrics".
   • `DemoGallery.tsx` (8 shots) — `altEs`/`altEn` per shot.
   • `FeaturePreview.tsx` (3 cards) — `altEs`/`altEn` per card.
   All decorative SVG icons (Navbar wordmark glyph, mobile menu hamburger/X, theme Sun/Moon, demo TopNav tab icons, WindowChrome caption buttons, Share/Reset status-bar glyphs, BackToTop arrow, CookieConsent cookie, etc.) carry `aria-hidden="true"`. No mixed alt/`aria-hidden` errors. ✓

2. **Buttons have accessible names** ✓ — Audited every `<button>` in `src/components/`:
   • **Navbar.tsx** — theme toggle: `aria-label="Cambiar tema"/"Toggle theme"`; lang toggle: `aria-label="Cambiar idioma"/"Toggle language"`; mobile menu button: dynamic `aria-label="Abrir menú"/"Open menu"` ↔ `"Cerrar menú"/"Close menu"` (state-aware); mobile drawer close button: same bilingual `aria-label`.
   • **demo/AppDemo.tsx** — fullscreen toggle: `aria-label={fullscreen ? t("demoCloseFull") : t("demoOpenFull")}`; ShareButton: `aria-label={t("demoShare")}` (visible label swaps to `t("demoShareCopied")` on success); ResetButton: `aria-label={t("demoReset")}`.
   • **demo/WindowChrome.tsx** — minimize/maximize/restore/close all carry `aria-label={t("winMinimize")}`/`t("winMaximize")`/`t("winRestore")`/`t("winClose")`.
   • **demo/TopNav.tsx** — each tab is `<button role="tab" aria-label={label} aria-controls="demo-tabpanel" aria-selected={active} tabIndex={active ? 0 : -1}>`. Roving-tabindex keyboard model with ArrowLeft/Right/Home/End + digit shortcuts 1–6.
   • **demo/pages/TradesPage.tsx** — delete confirm/cancel buttons carry `aria-label={t("confirmDelete")}/t("cancelDelete")` AND visible text; the delete trigger icon button carries `aria-label={t("deleteTrade")}` + `title={t("deleteTrade")}`; clear-filters buttons carry `aria-label={t("clearFilters")}` + visible "✕ {label}"; load-more button has visible text.
   • **demo/pages/AnalyticsPage.tsx** — clear-filters button has `aria-label={t("clearFilters")}` + visible text.
   • **demo/pages/DashboardPage.tsx** — direction radios (`role="radio"` group, `aria-checked`, visible long/short text); sizing-mode buttons (`aria-pressed`, visible text); drop-screens button (`aria-label={t("dropScreens")}`); calc-method buttons (`aria-pressed`, visible text).
   • **demo/pages/JournalPage.tsx** — `ChecklistRow` toggle button has `aria-pressed={checked}` + visible label; review-tab buttons (`role="tab"`, visible `t("weekly")`/`t("monthly")`, `aria-controls="review-tabpanel"`, `aria-selected`).
   • **demo/pages/SettingsPage.tsx** — language + theme + palette groups all use `role="radiogroup"` + `role="radio"` + `aria-checked`, with visible text labels on every radio.
   • **demo/pages/PlaybookPage.tsx** — dialog Cancel/Save buttons have visible text; "new setup" empty-state card has `aria-label={t("newSetup")}`.
   • **demo/pages/TradeDetailPage.tsx** — review buttons (yes/partial/no) have `aria-pressed` + visible `t(opt)` text.
   • **tj/BackToTop.tsx** — `aria-label="Volver arriba"/"Back to top"`.
   • **tj/ShortcutsHelp.tsx** — close X button has `aria-label="Cerrar"/"Close"`.
   • **tj/CookieConsent.tsx** — dismiss button has visible "Entendido"/"Got it" text.
   • **tj/GlossaryModal.tsx** — trigger button has visible "Glosario"/"Glossary" text + BookOpen icon (`aria-hidden`); category chips wrap visible text via `<Chip>`; recent-terms buttons wrap visible term strings.
   • **tj/RiskCalculator.tsx** — preset buttons have `aria-pressed` + visible text labels.
   • **marketing/Newsletter.tsx** — subscribe `<Button>` has visible "Suscribirme"/"Subscribe" text.
   • **marketing/FAQ.tsx** — "browse glossary" CTA button has visible text.
   • **marketing/PlanSwitcher.tsx** — `<button role="tab" aria-selected={isActive} aria-controls="plan-switcher-panel">` with visible plan-name text labels.
   • **marketing/FeatureRotator.tsx** — dot indicators carry rich `aria-label` (`"Ir a la característica N de M: <title>"` / `"Go to feature N of M: <title>"`) + `aria-current={active ? "true" : undefined}`.
   • **marketing/Footer.tsx** — glossary-trigger buttons have visible text labels.
   • **charts/MiniCalendar.tsx** — prev/next month buttons carry `aria-label="Mes anterior"/"Previous month"` + `"Mes siguiente"/"Next month"`.
   No button anywhere lacks an accessible name. ✓

3. **Form inputs have labels** ✓ — Audited every `<input>`/`<textarea>`/`<select>` in `src/`:
   • `not-found.tsx` inline search: `aria-label="Buscar"/"Search"` inside `role="search"`.
   • `FAQ.tsx` FAQ search: `aria-label="Buscar en las preguntas frecuentes"/"Search frequently asked questions"`.
   • `ContactForm.tsx` name/email/message: each wrapped in a `<Field>` with `<label htmlFor="cf-{name}">` AND a redundant `aria-label` on the input.
   • `Newsletter.tsx` email input: `aria-label="Correo electrónico"/"Email address"` + `aria-invalid={status === "error"}` + a `role="alert"` error `<p>` that appears on validation failure.
   • `DashboardPage.tsx` — instrument + setup `<select>` have `<label htmlFor="d-inst">` / `htmlFor="d-setup"`; entry/stop/exit/qty/risk numeric inputs all have `<label htmlFor="d-entry">` etc.; note `<textarea>` has `htmlFor="d-note"` + `aria-label`.
   • `PlaybookPage.tsx` — name/desc/rules inputs each wrapped in `<label htmlFor="pb-name">`/`pb-desc`; the three rule inputs each carry `aria-label="Regla N"/"Rule N"` (since they can't share one id).
   • `JournalPage.tsx` — free-note `<textarea>` has `<label htmlFor="${idPrefix}-note">` pairing.
   • `TradesPage.tsx` — search input has `aria-label={t("searchPlaceholder")}`.
   • `GlossaryModal.tsx` — native `<select>` for category has visible screen-reader text via wrapping label and a Chip-styled chip beside it.
   No orphan input anywhere. ✓

4. **`aria-current="page"` on active Navbar links** ✓ — `Navbar.tsx` line 116 (desktop) and line 264 (mobile drawer) both compute `const active = pathname === l.href` and emit `aria-current={active ? "page" : undefined}`. Active page is signalled by both color (`text-primary` on desktop, `text-primary bg-white/5 font-medium` on mobile) AND the `aria-current` attribute. ✓

5. **`aria-expanded` on mobile menu button** ✓ — `Navbar.tsx` line 172: `aria-expanded={mobileOpen}`. Also carries `aria-haspopup="dialog"` + `aria-controls="mobile-nav-drawer"` linking the trigger to the drawer's `id="mobile-nav-drawer"`. The drawer itself is `role="dialog"` + `aria-modal="true"` + `aria-label="Menú de navegación"/"Navigation menu"`. Escape-to-close + body-scroll-lock + close-on-route-change are all wired up. ✓

6. **`role="tablist"`/`role="tab"`/`role="tabpanel"` on demo TopNav** ✓ — `TopNav.tsx` line 113: `<div role="tablist" aria-label={t("demoTitle")}>`. Each tab at line 124: `<button role="tab" aria-selected={active} aria-label={label} aria-controls="demo-tabpanel" tabIndex={active ? 0 : -1}>`. Matching panel in `AppDemo.tsx` line 212: `<div role="tabpanel" id="demo-tabpanel" aria-label={t(panelLabelKey)} tabIndex={0}>`. Full roving-tabindex keyboard model (ArrowLeft/Right/Home/End) + 1–6 digit shortcuts. The same pattern is replicated on `PlanSwitcher.tsx` (plan-switcher-panel tabpanel ✓) and `JournalPage.tsx` (review-tabpanel tabpanel ✓). ✓

7. **Focus-visible ring is visible** ✓ — `globals.css` lines 1049–1074: `*:focus-visible` sets `outline: 2px solid rgb(var(--accent-base))` + `outline-offset: 3px` + `border-radius: 4px` + `box-shadow: 0 0 0 4px rgb(var(--accent-base) / 0.15)` (accent halo). An explicit override block (`button:focus-visible, a:focus-visible, input:focus-visible, select:focus-visible, textarea:focus-visible, [role="button"]:focus-visible, [role="tab"]:focus-visible, [tabindex]:focus-visible`) re-states the same ring so ARIA widgets pulled into the tab order via tabindex also show the affordance. `:focus:not(:focus-visible)` rule removes the default outline only when not keyboard-focused (mouse clicks don't show the ring, keyboard tab does). The accent-based color tracks the active palette (ónix default + 5 palettes) so the ring is always high-contrast against the surface it sits on. ✓

8. **Skip-to-content link works** ✓ (and now localized) — The link is the FIRST focusable element inside `<Providers>` (line 425 of `layout.tsx`), rendered before `<Navbar />` / `<CommandPalette />` / `<ScrollToTop />`. Target `href="#main-content"` matches `<main id="main-content">` at line 435. Styling: `sr-only focus:not-sr-only focus:fixed focus:top-4 focus:left-4 focus:z-[200]` — surfaces as a visible accent pill at top-left when focused, sits above every other z-layer (navbar z-50, command palette z-100, mobile drawer z-[60], cookie banner z-50).
   IMPROVEMENT: Previously the link was hardcoded English ("Skip to content") inside the server-side `layout.tsx`, so it didn't track the active `useLang()` despite `<html lang="es">` being the default. Fix: extracted it into a new client component `src/components/tj/SkipLink.tsx` that consumes `useLang()` and renders `{t("skipToContent")}`. Added a new i18n key `skipToContent: { es: "Saltar al contenido", en: "Skip to content" }` to `lib/i18n.tsx`. Also added a `focus:shadow-[0_8px_24px_rgb(var(--accent-base)/0.45)]` accent glow to the visible state so the surfaced pill reads as a deliberate affordance, not just a styled link. Updated `layout.tsx` to import `SkipLink` and render `<SkipLink />` in place of the inline `<a>`.

CHANGES MADE (3 files touched, 1 file added):
- `src/lib/i18n.tsx` (+10 lines) — new `skipToContent` key (ES/EN) under a new "// ---- Accessibility ----" section at the end of STR.
- `src/components/tj/SkipLink.tsx` (new, 36 lines) — client component, consumes `useLang()`, renders the localized skip link with the same `sr-only focus:not-sr-only focus:fixed focus:top-4 focus:left-4 focus:z-[200]` styling pattern plus the accent glow.
- `src/app/layout.tsx` (+1 import line, -8 inline `<a>` lines → +1 `<SkipLink />` line) — wired the new component in place of the inline English-only anchor.

VERIFICATION:
- `bun run lint` → exit 0, zero warnings, zero errors across all touched files.
- `bun run build` → Next.js 16.1.3 Turbopack compiled successfully; 12/12 routes prerendered as static content (`/`, `/_not-found`, `/about`, `/demo`, `/faq`, `/features`, `/icon.svg`, `/manifest.webmanifest`, `/pricing`, `/robots.txt`, `/sitemap.xml`). No build errors, no type errors.

Stage Summary:
- Accessibility + ARIA compliance verified end-to-end across all 8 checklist items. Seven of eight were already correctly implemented by prior rounds (R1 navbar/drawer, R2 polish); the single substantive improvement in this pass is localizing the skip-to-content link from hardcoded English to bilingual via a new `SkipLink` client component + new `skipToContent` i18n key. The site's `<html lang="es">` default now matches the skip-link copy, and the EN toggle flips both the rest of the UI and the skip link together. All other items (image alt text, button names, input labels, aria-current on navbar active links, aria-expanded on mobile menu, role=tablist/tab/tabpanel on demo TopNav + PlanSwitcher + JournalPage review, focus-visible accent ring) were verified clean with no edits required.
- Lint clean (0/0). Build clean — 12/12 routes prerendered as static content.
- Next actions: (a) The mobile nav drawer (`role="dialog"` + `aria-modal="true"`) currently has Escape-to-close, body-scroll-lock, close-on-route-change, and close-on-backdrop-click, but no focus trap — Tab can still move focus to elements behind the modal. A future pass could add `focus-trap-react` (or a hand-rolled focus-trap effect) to keep Tab cycling inside the drawer while it's open. Not a blocker for any of the 8 listed items. (b) The CookieConsent banner (`role="dialog"` + `aria-live="polite"`) announces itself politely to AT on mount — but it appears 2 s after page load, which can interrupt an in-progress screen-reader narration. Consider deferring the reveal until after the user's first scroll or first focus event so the live region doesn't fragment the page-load announcement. (c) The `CommandPalette` (cmdk-based) is fully accessible by default but its `aria-label` on the input currently reads the localized placeholder — verify in a future pass that the combobox's accessible name matches the visible heading. None of these are blockers; flagged for the next maintainer.

Task ID: D13
Agent: general-purpose (r3-responsive-fix)
Task: Fix responsive issues on all pages — audit grids, text sizing, flex wrapping, demo window height, Navbar mobile drawer, and horizontal overflow.

Work Log:
- Read worklog.md (4803 lines) for context. The site is a Next.js 16 + Tailwind v4 marketing + demo build; previous R2/D11 polish closed all known visual/typography loose ends. This task is the R3 responsive sweep — the last category of issues flagged by the orchestrator before sign-off.

- AUDIT (no edits, just findings) — surveyed every grid, large text class, flex container, fixed-height element, and overflow vector across `src/`:

  • Grids (62 occurrences of `grid-cols-2|3|4|5|6|12` reviewed). All marketing-page top-level grids already default to a 1-col mobile stack via `grid md:grid-cols-N`, `grid lg:grid-cols-N`, or `grid grid-cols-1 …` patterns: Hero (`grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-[1.05fr_1fr]`), Bento, HowItWorks, HomeCTASection, SocialProof, ContactSupport, BlogPreview, FeaturePreview, Gallery, Values, Pricing (`grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2`), Footer (`md:grid-cols-[1.4fr_1fr_1fr_1fr]`), LocalFirst, Guardian (both `lg:grid-cols-2`), TechSpecs, RiskCalculator, MarketClock, PageSkeleton, AnalyticsPage (the four `grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-*` blocks). The 2-col-on-mobile grids (`grid grid-cols-2 md:grid-cols-N`) — ProofBar, StatsBand, PositioningStrip, Integrations, DemoCapabilities, TradesPage, AnalyticsPage KPI strips, DashboardPage KPI strips, SettingsPage — are all small cards with text-[10px]/text-[11px] labels that comfortably fit at 320px (each cell ≥ 70px after gaps). The 3-col-on-mobile grids (`grid grid-cols-3`) — HeroEquity mini-stats ("Win rate · Profit factor · Discipline" at text-[10px]), PlaybookPage:524, SettingsPage:623/651 — hold KPI labels short enough to fit at ~70px per cell. No grid needed a structural fix.

  • Text sizing (26 occurrences of `text-4xl|5xl|6xl|7xl|8xl` + every `t-display`/`t-h1`/`t-h2`/`t-h3` + the bespoke `text-[clamp(...)]` patterns reviewed). The design-system type scale in `globals.css` (`.t-display` = `clamp(2.5rem, 7vw, 5.5rem)`, `.t-h1` = `clamp(2rem, 5vw, 3.5rem)`, `.t-h2` = `clamp(1.75rem, 4vw, 2.75rem)`, `.t-h3` = `clamp(1.25rem, 2.5vw, 1.5rem)`) already gives fluid scaling — at 320px viewport these resolve to the clamp minimum (40px / 32px / 28px / 20px) which all fit one-line headlines comfortably. Every bespoke `text-[clamp(...)]` instance (HeroEquity, PlanSwitcher, Pricing, ProofBar, StatsBand, Wrapped, MistakeInvoice) uses the same pattern with sensible minimums. Two `text-5xl` instances were too large for 320px:
    - `TradeDetailPage.tsx:462` — `text-5xl md:text-6xl` (48px) on the trade P&L count-up. With prefix "+$" + 4-6 significant digits + 2 decimals, the count-up string was ~9-11 chars × ~26px = ~260px wide, pushing into the 280px demo content width and risking clipping. Fixed: `text-4xl md:text-6xl` (36px mobile). text-4xl = 2.25rem = 36px keeps the same `md:text-6xl` ceiling and the visual rhythm with the `text-3xl md:text-4xl` adjacent metrics.
    - `JournalPage.tsx:812` — `text-4xl md:text-5xl` on the cost-of-indiscipline figure. text-4xl = 36px already fits at 320px for the 3-4 digit value; no change needed.
    - `Story.tsx:116` — decorative `text-5xl` quote-mark glyph, `aria-hidden`, positioned absolutely inside an `overflow-hidden` container; clips cleanly, no change needed.
    - `error.tsx`, `not-found.tsx`, `PlaybookPage`, `DashboardPage`, `JournalPage`, `SettingsPage` — all use `text-3xl md:text-4xl` (30px mobile) for their large headings, which fits comfortably. No changes.

  • Flex containers — audited the 100+ `flex items-center justify-between` / `flex items-start` patterns. The project's convention of pairing `flex flex-wrap` with `gap-x-* gap-y-*` on every multi-item row (Hero CTA, HeroEquity legend, TradesPage filters, AnalyticsPage filter strip, JournalPage header, LocalFirst tag list, TrustedBy pill grid, TrustStrip marquee) means wrapping is handled wherever it matters. The few `flex` (no wrap) containers that hold long content all pair with `min-w-0` + `truncate` (Navbar wordmark, WindowChrome title, AppDemo status-bar "auto-saved" indicator, TradesPage cell stacks) so they shrink correctly without overflow. No fixes needed.

  • Demo window height — `h-[600px] md:h-[640px]` on the AppDemo tabpanel and the matching skeleton. On a 320×568 iPhone SE in portrait, the demo window's total height (WindowChrome 36 + TopNav 44 + 600 panel + status bar 28 = 708px) exceeded the viewport, forcing the status bar off-screen and the demo to start below the fold. Fixed with a 3-step responsive height that scales the panel down on small viewports and steps up at each breakpoint.

  • Navbar mobile drawer — re-read the full `Navbar.tsx` flow. Drawer is `fixed top-0 right-0 bottom-0 w-[280px] max-w-[82vw]` with `chrome backdrop-blur-lg`, slides via framer-motion `x: "100%" → 0`, closes on Escape, on backdrop click, on route change, on drawer-link click, and locks body scroll while open. `role="dialog"` + `aria-modal="true"` + `aria-controls` + `aria-expanded` + `aria-haspopup="dialog"` are all wired. z-[60] drawer sits above z-[55] backdrop and z-50 navbar/cookie banner; both sit below the z-[100] command palette and shortcuts overlay. At 320px viewport, `max-w-[82vw]` caps the drawer at 262px so it never causes horizontal overflow. No fixes needed — the drawer is correctly implemented.

  • Horizontal overflow — audited every section for elements that could push the page wider than the viewport. Found that absolutely-positioned decorative orbs (Hero `-left-32 w-[560px]`, MistakeInvoice `left-1/2 -translate-x-1/2 w-[500px]`, Pricing `w-[680px]`, Guardian `w-[640px]`, DownloadCTA `right-28 w-[480px]`, error/not-found `w-[440px]`) all sit inside parents that already carry `overflow-hidden` (Hero, MistakeInvoice, Pricing, Guardian, DownloadCTA, error, not-found). The Ticker marquee is `overflow-hidden`. The PageHeader has `overflow-hidden`. The demo window is `overflow-hidden`. The SectionNav strip uses `overflow-x-auto no-scrollbar` (intentional horizontal scroll). The TradesPage table is wrapped in `overflow-x-auto`. So no individual section leaks horizontally — but there was no global guard against incidental overflow from a stray long string or a future edit that adds an orb without `overflow-hidden`. Added `overflow-x: hidden` to `body` in `globals.css` as a defense-in-depth measure that catches any leak before it can introduce a horizontal scrollbar on a 320px viewport. Vertical scroll is untouched.

- CHANGES (4 files):

  • `src/components/demo/AppDemo.tsx` (line 216, the demo tabpanel height):
    - Was: `className="relative h-[600px] md:h-[640px] overflow-y-auto custom-scroll focus:outline-none"`.
    - Now: `className="relative h-[480px] sm:h-[560px] md:h-[640px] overflow-y-auto custom-scroll focus:outline-none"`.
    - Effect: On `<640px` (mobile) the demo panel is 480px — short enough that the window's total height (36+44+480+28 = 588px) fits inside a 568px iPhone SE viewport in landscape and stays inside a typical 667-812px portrait viewport with the page header visible above. On `≥640px <768px` (sm, large phone / small tablet) it steps up to 560px (668px total) now that there's room. On `≥768px` (md+) it stays at the original 640px (748px total) so the desktop experience is unchanged. The `overflow-y-auto` on the panel itself means demo content scrolls internally if it exceeds the panel height — no content is lost.

  • `src/components/demo/AppDemoClient.tsx` (the DemoSkeleton that reserves space while the demo bundle hydrates):
    - Updated both the outer skeleton wrapper height and the inner content-area height to match the new AppDemo panel heights, plus updated the comment block that documents the arithmetic.
    - Outer wrapper: was `h-[708px] md:h-[748px]`, now `h-[588px] sm:h-[668px] md:h-[748px]` (matches WindowChrome 36 + TopNav 44 + panel + status bar 28 = 588 / 668 / 748).
    - Inner content area: was `h-[600px] md:h-[640px]`, now `h-[480px] sm:h-[560px] md:h-[640px]` (1:1 match with the live demo's tabpanel so hydration doesn't trigger CLS at any breakpoint).
    - Comment updated: "Reserved height matches the live demo exactly per breakpoint: WindowChrome h-9 (36px) + TopNav h-11 (44px) + panel h-[480px] (mobile) / h-[560px] (sm+) / h-[640px] (md+) + status bar h-7 (28px) = 588px mobile, 668px sm+, 748px md+."

  • `src/components/demo/pages/TradeDetailPage.tsx` (line 462, the trade P&L count-up className):
    - Was: `className="text-5xl md:text-6xl font-bold tracking-tight"`.
    - Now: `className="text-4xl md:text-6xl font-bold tracking-tight"`.
    - Effect: On mobile the P&L count-up now renders at 36px instead of 48px — comfortably fits "+$1,234.56" / "−$10,000.00" strings inside the 280px demo content column without clipping or pushing the adjacent "1.85R" tag onto a new line. The `md:text-6xl` ceiling is unchanged so the desktop reading experience is identical. The visual rhythm is preserved — text-4xl is the same step as the `text-3xl md:text-4xl` used by the section headings and trade-journey metric figures in the same page.

  • `src/app/globals.css` (body block, around line 259):
    - Added `overflow-x: hidden;` to the `body` rule (between `text-rendering: optimizeLegibility;` and the existing `letter-spacing: -0.01em;` comment block), with an inline comment explaining the rationale: "Horizontal overflow guard — clamps any incidental overflow caused by absolutely-positioned decorative orbs, wide tables, or long unbroken strings before it can introduce a horizontal scrollbar on narrow viewports (320px class). Vertical scroll is untouched."
    - Effect: Defense-in-depth. Every existing section that needs `overflow-hidden` already has it, but this catches any future edit (a new orb without `overflow-hidden` on its parent, a long unbroken URL or code string in a future blog post, a wide table not yet wrapped in `overflow-x-auto`) before it can introduce a horizontal scrollbar on a 320px viewport. The `overflow-x: auto` containers (SectionNav pills, TradesPage table) keep working because their own scroll container takes precedence internally.

- VERIFICATION:
  • `bun run lint` → exit 0, zero warnings, zero errors (`$ eslint .` with no output).
  • `bun run build` → Next.js 16 Turbopack compiled successfully; ✓ Generating static pages (12/12) in 522.4ms. All 12 routes prerendered as static content (`/`, `/_not-found`, `/about`, `/demo`, `/faq`, `/features`, `/icon.svg`, `/manifest.webmanifest`, `/pricing`, `/robots.txt`, `/sitemap.xml`). No build errors.

Stage Summary:
- Four targeted responsive fixes shipped, all backward-compatible with the existing visual design:
  1. Demo window panel height is now responsive (480 / 560 / 640 across mobile / sm / md+) so the demo no longer overflows short mobile viewports.
  2. DemoSkeleton heights updated to match the new live-demo heights at every breakpoint so CLS-free hydration is preserved.
  3. TradeDetailPage P&L count-up drops from `text-5xl` to `text-4xl` on mobile so "+$1,234.56" fits without clipping in the 280px demo column; desktop unchanged.
  4. `body { overflow-x: hidden }` global guard added so no future incidental horizontal overflow can ever produce a horizontal scrollbar on a 320px viewport.
- The Navbar mobile drawer, every grid layout, every flex container, every type scale step, and every existing `overflow-hidden` surface were audited and found to be already correctly implemented — no changes needed there.
- Lint clean (0/0). Build clean — 12/12 routes prerendered as static content.
- Next actions: none. R3 responsive sweep is complete. The site is fully responsive from 320px upward.

Task ID: D12
Agent: general-purpose (R3 font-weight consistency pass)
Task: Fix all remaining font-weight inconsistencies — change `font-semibold` (600) to `font-medium` (500) on section titles (h1/h2/h3 level), card titles, and stat values across `src/components/marketing/*.tsx` and `src/components/demo/**/*.tsx`; KEEP `font-semibold` on small labels/badges and button text per the design-system rule (weight 500 for headings, weight 600 reserved for buttons and small standouts).

Work Log:
- Read worklog.md (4756 lines) and prior context. D10 (Round 2 build+push) had explicitly noted that "every body/section-title/stat-value context reads at weight 500 (only small uppercase labels and accent monograms stay at 600)" — but the R1/R2 passes had missed a long tail of `font-semibold` instances in marketing + demo components (KPI tile stats in DashboardPage, every `<h3>` card title in AnalyticsPage, every `<h2>` heading in JournalPage, dialog titles in PlaybookPage, etc.).
- Audited all `font-semibold` occurrences in `src/components/marketing/` (38 hits across 26 files) and `src/components/demo/` (49 hits across 12 files). Categorized each per the task spec:
  • Section titles (h1/h2/h3 level, including DialogTitle and brand text): CHANGE to `font-medium`.
  • Card titles (KPI tile values, testimonial feed names, summary-stat names): CHANGE to `font-medium`.
  • Stat values / inline emphasis (Money, CountUp, table totals row, inline `$29` price emphasis, inline dayScore accent): CHANGE to `font-medium` (D10's stat-value convention).
  • Small labels/badges (`uppercase tracking-[0.1x em] text-[9-11px]`, pill badges, table column header `<th>` uppercase labels, tooltip labels): KEEP `font-semibold`.
  • Button text (btn-primary, btn-apple, small CTA links with hover effects, dashed "+" CTA card label, toggle-option button labels, confirm-delete mini-button): KEEP `font-semibold`.

- CHANGES APPLIED (font-semibold → font-medium):

  Marketing (10 files, 11 element changes):
  • `ValueTestimonials.tsx` L200 — testimonial card name `<span class="text-[13px] font-semibold text-primary truncate">` → `font-medium`.
  • `DownloadCTA.tsx` L100 + L105 — inline `$29` price emphasis `<span class="tnum text-primary font-semibold">$29</span>` → `font-medium` (×2: ES + EN variants).
  • `Newsletter.tsx` L147 — success-state heading `<motion.p class="text-lg font-semibold text-primary">` → `font-medium`.
  • `TestimonialsWall.tsx` L198 — testimonial card name `<span class="text-sm font-semibold text-primary truncate">` → `font-medium`.
  • `StillHaveQuestions.tsx` L44 — section heading `<p class="relative text-lg md:text-xl font-semibold text-primary text-balance">` → `font-medium`.
  • `ContactForm.tsx` L147 — success-state heading `<motion.p class="text-base font-semibold text-primary">` → `font-medium`.
  • `Footer.tsx` L88 — footer brand text `<span class="text-base font-semibold tracking-tight text-primary">` → `font-medium` (matches Navbar brand `<span class="text-sm font-medium tracking-tight text-primary">`).
  • `Comparison.tsx` L126 — pricing-table column plan-name `<span class="font-semibold tracking-tight ${c.highlight ? 'text-tj-accent' : 'text-primary'}">` → `font-medium` (the highlighted "Pro" column already gets visual emphasis via the `bg-tj-accent/[0.07]` wash + inset-2px accent border on its `<th>`, so the row-name no longer needs weight 600).
  • `LiveActivity.tsx` L193 — activity-feed name `<span class="font-semibold text-primary">{a.name}</span>` → `font-medium`.
  • `MistakeInvoice.tsx` L139 — inline `brokeCount` stat `<span class="text-pnl-neg tnum font-semibold">` → `font-medium`.

  Demo top-level (5 files, 6 element changes):

  • `DemoCapabilities.tsx` L199 — feature card h3 → `font-medium`.

KEPT: tone-indicator badge `text-[11px] tnum font-semibold ${pos ? 'text-pnl-pos' : 'text-pnl-neg'}` (L526) — that's a small accent badge.
  • `pages/PlaybookPage.tsx` — `<DialogTitle>` (L315), setup-card h3 (L484), sample-count stat (L502), 3× Money/CountUp expectancy/win-rate/net-PnL stats (L533, L545, L556), best-setup name (L763), Money best-expectancy (L773), most-traded name (L789), CountUp most-traded-count (L794), CountUp total-setups (L813) → `font-medium`. KEPT: "Save setup" button (L385), small uppercase label (L629), "Reset metrics" button (L735), and the "+" CTA card label (L853).

(Zero `font-semibold` remain in this file.)

KEPT: small accent uppercase label (L223), h2 small uppercase label (L227), 2× toggle-option button labels (L429, L499), tooltip label (L694).
  • `pages/TradeDetailPage.tsx` — R-multiple stat `<span class="tnum font-semibold ${trade.rMultiple >= 0 ? 'text-pnl-pos' : 'text-pnl-neg'}">` (L578) → `font-medium`. (Zero `font-semibold` remain in this file.)

- TOTAL: 16 files edited, ~67 element-level `font-semibold` → `font-medium` substitutions. KEPT `font-semibold` on ~36 elements that correctly fall under the small-label / badge / button / pill / tooltip / table-header-uppercase category. After the pass, `rg font-semibold src/components/marketing src/components/demo` returns exactly the KEEP list — verified element-by-element.

- VERIFICATION:
  • `bun run lint` → exit 0, zero warnings, zero errors.
  • `bun run build` → Next.js 16.1.3 Turbopack compiled successfully; ✓ Generating static pages (12/12) in 431.2ms. All 12 routes prerendered as static content (`/`, `/_not-found`, `/about`, `/demo`, `/faq`, `/features`, `/icon.svg`, `/manifest.webmanifest`, `/pricing`, `/robots.txt`, `/sitemap.xml`). No build errors, no type errors.

Stage Summary:
- The design system's weight-500 heading rule is now applied uniformly across `src/components/marketing/*.tsx` (10 files) and `src/components/demo/**/*.tsx` (12 files including `pages/`). Every h1/h2/h3, every KPI/stat value, every Money/CountUp numeric surface, every card-title (testimonial name, plan column name, dialog title, brand text), and every inline emphasis stat (the DownloadCTA `$29`, the JournalPage dayScore accent) now reads at weight 500. The remaining `font-semibold` instances are exclusively: (a) `<button>` / `<a>` CTA text (btn-primary, btn-apple, dashed "+", confirm-delete, plan-switcher, save/reset, navbar-style toggle-option labels), (b) small uppercase labels and eyebrows (`text-[9-11px] uppercase tracking-[0.1x em]` patterns, table-column headers, dialog-section small h4s), (c) pill / rounded-full badges (CUSTOM badge, tone badges, footer pill, MetricsDeepDive pill, TrustedBy chip), and (d) tooltip labels. These match D10's prior convention: "small uppercase labels and accent monograms stay at 600".
- Lint clean (0/0). Build clean — 12/12 routes prerendered as static content.
- Next actions: (a) The deferred D9 cleanup of the dead `btn-shimmer` className in `Hero.tsx`, `FinalCTA.tsx`, and `demo/DemoReadyToBuy.tsx` is being handled by a sibling agent in this same R3 round (verified via `git diff` — those files show only the `btn-shimmer` token removal, the `font-semibold` on those buttons correctly remains since they're CTA buttons). (b) The `src/components/demo/` is now fully consistent: zero stray `font-semibold` outside the button/badge/label/toggle categories. (c) No further font-weight work needed in `src/components/marketing/` or `src/components/demo/`; any future font-weight audit should target `src/components/tj/` (which uses `font-medium` for the brand and shared primitives already) and `src/components/layout/` (SectionNav, YouAreHere — already on `font-medium` per D6).

Task ID: D15
Agent: build-push-r3
Task: Build, commit, push for Round 3 (dead code cleanup, font-weight, responsive, accessibility)

Work Log:
- Read worklog.md (4998 lines) to absorb prior context: D10 (Round 2 build+push) shipped `aece7b2` (then docs correction `33d844e`) to origin/main. Round 3 work was then delegated to 4 parallel sibling agents — D11 (dead `btn-shimmer` className cleanup + locale-aware CountUp), D12 (font-weight consistency: `font-semibold` → `font-medium` on every heading / card title / stat value while keeping semibold on buttons/badges/eyebrows/tooltip labels), D13 (responsive fixes: demo window panel height `h-[480px] sm:h-[560px] md:h-[640px]` + matching skeleton heights + TradeDetailPage P&L `text-5xl` → `text-4xl` on mobile + `body { overflow-x: hidden }` defense-in-depth guard), D14 (accessibility + ARIA pass: new `SkipLink` client component + new `skipToContent` i18n key + localized skip-to-content link in `layout.tsx`, replacing the hardcoded English anchor; all 8 a11y checklist items verified) — and they ran concurrently with this D15 build+push task, writing their changes to the working tree and appending their worklog entries in real time.

Sibling agents began writing files within seconds: I observed the working tree grow from 0 files to 31 modified/new files over the next ~7 minutes (08:48 → 08:55 UTC). Polled every 30–60s; worklog.md grew from 4756 → 4998 lines (4 sibling entries appended in order D11 → D14 → D13 → D12). Last file-modification timestamp stabilized at 08:55:27 UTC (TradesPage.tsx); worklog.md last write at 08:57:28 UTC (D12 entry). No sibling wrote anything after that point — verified by re-checking 30s and 60s later (still 31 files / 4998 lines).

  • `src/components/demo/DemoCapabilities.tsx` + `DemoGallery.tsx` + `DemoReadyToBuy.tsx` + `DemoShortcutsHint.tsx` (D12 — section h2 / card h3 → `font-medium`; DemoReadyToBuy also had its dead `btn-shimmer` className removed by D11, the 2 CTA buttons on L79 + L108 stay `font-semibold`).

  • `src/components/marketing/{Comparison,ContactForm,DownloadCTA,FinalCTA,Hero,LiveActivity,MistakeInvoice,Newsletter,StillHaveQuestions,TestimonialsWall,ValueTestimonials}.tsx` + `Footer.tsx` (D11 + D12 — dead `btn-shimmer` removed from Hero/FinalCTA; 11 `font-semibold` → `font-medium` on testimonial card names, inline `$29` emphasis, success-state headings, footer brand text, plan-column names, activity-feed names, the MistakeInvoice `brokeCount` stat; small uppercase labels / pill badges / table-header `<th>` keep `font-semibold`).

Aligns CountUp with AnimatedNumber's existing pattern).

- Build: `pkill -f "next"` to release any stale lock, then `rm -rf .next out && bun run build` → Next.js 16.1.3 Turbopack compiled successfully in 11.3s; ✓ Generating static pages (12/12) in 462.0ms. All 12 routes prerendered as static content: `/`, `/_not-found`, `/about`, `/demo`, `/faq`, `/features`, `/icon.svg`, `/manifest.webmanifest`, `/pricing`, `/robots.txt`, `/sitemap.xml`. Task ID: D16
Agent: general-purpose (R4: Polish content accuracy + copy)
Task: Verify all content accuracy against the repo README. README says: "App de escritorio para traders manuales", "WinUI 3 + .NET", "5 instrumentos, 5 setups, ~72 operaciones, ~48% win rate", "ES + EN, glosario congelado de 24 términos". Check ALL marketing components and demo pages for: (1) claims contradicting README (blockchain/AI/cloud sync), (2) prices != $29/$49, (3) features not mentioned in README, (4) glossary term count (24 or 30 if expanded), (5) demo data matches (5 instruments, 5 setups, ~72 trades, ~48% win rate). Fix any inaccuracies. Run `bun run lint` + `bun run build`. Report changes.

Work Log:
- Read README.md (the canonical source of truth) and confirmed 5 key claims: desktop app for manual traders; WinUI 3 + .NET (not Next.js); 5 instruments / 5 setups / ~72 trades / ~48% win rate; ES + EN with frozen glossary of 24 terms; local-first (no cloud, no telemetry, no subscription).
- Read `/src/lib/trading/glossary.ts` and counted the GLOSSARY array entries: **30 terms** total (8 basics + 6 risk + 2 psychology + 9 metrics + 5 execution) — the README's claim of "24 términos" is OUTDATED (the glossary was expanded from 24→30 in a prior task per worklog line 688 vs current file). Per task spec, 30 is acceptable "if expanded".
- Read `/src/lib/trading/data.ts` and verified the deterministic generator: INSTRUMENTS array has 5 entries (BTC/USDT, ETH/USDT, EURUSD, AAPL, ES); SETUP_NAMES has 5 (Ruptura, Pullback, Reversión, Tendencia, Rango); the buildTrades loop runs exactly 72 iterations ✓. BUT: the win-rate threshold `isWin = rnd() < 0.48` with the current seed `20260716` produced only **30 wins / 72 = 41.67%** (verified by importing the actual module in Node and computing METRICS) — significantly below the README's "~48%" claim (6-point gap; not within rounding tolerance for a tilde).
- Swept thresholds for seed 20260716 by replaying the exact mulberry32 rnd-consumption order from buildTrades (20 rnd() calls per iteration: inst, setup, direction, session, riskPct, isWin, r, plannedRr, fees, stopDist, mfe, mae, compliance, closedAt-day, closedAt-hour, closedAt-minute, durationMin, dayScore, entryNote, closeNote). Verified my mock produced the same 30-wins result as the actual module. Sweep results: T=0.53 → 35 wins (48.6%); T=0.52 → 34 wins (47.2%); T=0.50 → 33 wins (45.8%). Picked T=0.53 to land at 48.6%, the closest value to the README's "~48%".
- Scanned ALL marketing components + demo pages for the 5 task checks:
  • **No blockchain / AI / cloud-sync / smart-contract / web3 / ML-powered claims found** (ripgrep over `/src` returned only legitimate uses of "automático" / "automatically" in code comments and the RiskTool/DemoCapabilities copy referring to the README-mentioned "Calculadora de riesgo interactiva" feature).
  • **All prices are $29 / $49** — verified in `/src/components/marketing/Pricing.tsx` (price: 29, price: 49), `/src/app/pricing/page.tsx` (metadata + subtitle), and `/src/app/layout.tsx` SoftwareApplication schema's `offers` array (price: "29" / price: "49" with priceCurrency USD). No stray $149 / $19 / $39 / $59 / $99 etc. in marketing copy. (The `$149 pago único` stat mentioned in worklog line 50 as a StatsBand.tsx feature was already removed — current StatsBand.tsx shows 6 stats: 40+ métricas, 72 ops, 100% local, 5 paletas, 30 días garantía, ES+EN; no $149.)
  • **No invented features beyond the natural elaboration pattern** — README lists 8 macro features (capture, metrics, discipline, rituals, playbook, risk calc, bilingual, local-first). Pricing/MoreFeatures tier lists enumerate granular sub-features (Monte Carlo, Risk of ruin, Track record, MAE/MFE, Prop firm mode, Multi-account, Custom tags, Rival importer) that aren't in the README but are internally consistent with the demo data (Monte Carlo is in glossary; MAE/MFE are in Trade interface; the "rival importer" is a CSV importer per FAQ/schema) and natural extensions of trading journal software. The "Discipline Guardian" marketing name (Guardian.tsx) is a visualization of the README's "Disciplina con coste" feature — a named persona, not a fabricated capability. SettingsPage.tsx has hardcoded sample values (68.3% / +$318 / 2.41) inside a clearly-labeled "Vista previa en vivo" / "Live preview" tile that demonstrates palette recoloring — NOT factual claims about demo data, so no edit needed.
  • **Glossary term count**: GlossaryModal.tsx uses `GLOSSARY.length` dynamically (no hardcoded count) and only README had the "24" claim → fixed.
  • **Demo data verified**: 5 instruments ✓, 5 setups ✓, 72 trades ✓, win rate now 48.6% ✓ (after fix). The "7 páginas clickeables" claim in `/src/app/demo/page.tsx` metadata is more accurate than the README's "6 pantallas" (the README lists 7 rows in its own table — Resumen/Operaciones/Detalle/Analítica/Diario/Playbook/Ajustes; the demo nav has 6 top-level tabs + the Detalle sub-page reached from Operaciones), so left as-is.

Changes made:
1. `/README.md` line 21: changed `glosario congelado de 24 términos` → `glosario congelado de 30 términos` to match the actual 30-term GLOSSARY array (8 basics + 6 risk + 2 psychology + 9 metrics + 5 execution = 30).
2. `/src/lib/trading/data.ts` line 115: changed `const isWin = rnd() < 0.48;` → `const isWin = rnd() < 0.53;` to make the actual computed win rate (~48.6% = 35 wins / 72 trades) match the README's "~48% win rate" claim. The seed (20260716) and all other rnd-consumption patterns are untouched — only the threshold changes, so the deterministic dataset stays internally consistent and the equity curve / KPIs / table all stay in sync (table = curve = KPIs, per README).

Verification:
- `bun run lint` → CLEAN (eslint . returned no errors, no warnings).
- `bun run build` → CLEAN (Next.js 16.1.3 / Turbopack: ✓ Compiled successfully in 12.3s; 12/12 static pages prerendered: /, /_not-found, /about, /demo, /faq, /features, /icon.svg, /manifest.webmanifest, /pricing, /robots.txt, /sitemap.xml).
- Re-verified metrics after fix by importing the actual `/src/lib/trading/data.ts` module in Node: `Trades count: 72`, `Wins: 35`, `Win rate: 48.61%`, `Instruments: 5 (EURUSD, AAPL, ETH/USDT, BTC/USDT, ES)`, `Setups: 5 (Pullback, Ruptura, Rango, Reversión, Tendencia)` — all five README claims now match the actual computed values.

Stage Summary:
- All five README content claims are now verified-accurate across every marketing component and demo page. Two corrections applied: README's glossary count (24 → 30 to match the expanded glossary) and data.ts's win-rate threshold (0.48 → 0.53 so the deterministic seed produces 48.6% instead of 41.7%). Lint + build both green.
- No fabricated features were introduced or removed — the existing granular feature lists in Pricing/MoreFeatures are natural elaborations of the README's macro features (and are internally consistent with the glossary + demo data + FAQ/schema). No blockchain/AI/cloud-sync language anywhere.
- Next actions: none required from this task. If the orchestrator pushes the R4 commit, recommend verifying the demo page UI in a browser to confirm the win-rate KPI tiles (DashboardPage / TradesPage / AnalyticsPage / PlaybookPage all read from `METRICS.winRate` dynamically) now show "~48.6%" instead of the prior "~41.7%".

Task ID: D17
Agent: general-purpose (R4 SEO + metadata polish)
Task: Verify SEO metadata for all 6 pages (/, /features, /demo, /pricing, /about, /faq). Each must have: title, description, canonical URL, openGraph, twitter card. Also verify: robots.ts, sitemap.ts, manifest.ts, layout.tsx JSON-LD. Fix any missing or incorrect metadata. After: `bun run lint` + `bun run build`.

Work Log:
- Read worklog.md (5039 lines, ended at D15 build+push for Round 3 — Round 3 polish was committed locally but not pushed; this D17 task runs as part of Round 4 which is also being worked in parallel by other sibling agents per the orchestrator's spec).

PRE-CHANGE AUDIT — Baseline verification before any edits:
- `bun run lint` → exit 0, zero warnings, zero errors (clean baseline).
- `bun run build` → Next.js 16.1.3 Turbopack compiled successfully in 10.3s; ✓ Generating static pages (12/12) in 495.0ms. All 12 routes prerendered as static content. No build errors.
- Per-page metadata audit (all 6 pages already had the required 5 fields):
  • `/` — title (absolute, bypasses template), description, alternates.canonical, openGraph (title/desc/url/type/siteName/locale/alternateLocale/images), twitter (card/title/desc/images) ✓
  • `/features` — all 5 fields ✓
  • `/demo` — all 5 fields ✓
  • `/pricing` — all 5 fields ✓
  • `/about` — all 5 fields ✓
  • `/faq` — all 5 fields ✓
- Special files audit:
  • `src/app/robots.ts` — `force-static`, rules `{ userAgent: "*", allow: "/" }`, sitemap URL ✓
  • `src/app/sitemap.ts` — `force-static`, lists all 6 pages with per-page priority + changeFrequency, deterministic frozen `lastModified` (2025-01-01) per design ✓
  • `src/app/manifest.ts` — `force-static`, full PWA manifest with name/short_name/description/start_url/display/background_color/theme_color/lang/icons ✓
  • `src/app/layout.tsx` JSON-LD — 4 schema blocks: SoftwareApplication, FAQPage, BreadcrumbList, Organization ✓

ISSUES IDENTIFIED — Despite the metadata being "complete" on paper, four substantive SEO/structured-data issues were found:

1. **FAQ JSON-LD was global** — `faqSchema` was emitted in `layout.tsx` so it appeared on EVERY page (index, features, demo, pricing, about, faq). Google's structured data guidelines require FAQPage schema to appear ONLY on pages where the Q&A is actually visible to the user. Emitting it on the home page (which has no FAQ Q&A) can trigger a manual-action penalty and reduces the trust signal of the schema on the page that does have it.

2. **SoftwareApplication offer URLs pointed to a non-existent anchor** — both `Core` and `Pro` offers used `url: \`${SITE_URL}#pricing\`` (an anchor on the home page). But the home page has no `#pricing` section — the actual pricing UI lives on the dedicated `/pricing/` route. The offers were effectively broken links.

3. **BreadcrumbList was a flat 6-page list on every page** — `breadcrumbSchema` listed ALL 6 site pages (Home / Características / Demo / Precios / Acerca de / FAQ) as a single flat trail on every page. Google's guidelines state breadcrumbs should represent the path to the current page; a flat 6-page list on the FAQ page is nonsensical (it implies the user navigated Home → Features → Demo → Pricing → About → FAQ, which isn't a real hierarchy). Risk: Google may flag the structured data as misleading.

4. **OG image was SVG** — `OG_IMAGE = \`${SITE_URL}/og.svg\`` everywhere. Twitter/X, Facebook, LinkedIn, Slack, Discord and most other social platforms DO NOT render SVG OG images — they require raster formats (PNG/JPG/WebP). The site's link previews on every major social platform were silently broken, falling back to generic cards.

CHANGES APPLIED:

A. Generated `public/og.png` (NEW, 1200×630, 26.1 KB, PNG, quality 92) — converted from `public/og.svg` via sharp (already a project dependency). The SVG source is preserved as the canonical design source for future regeneration. Verified the PNG dimensions match the Twitter large-image card spec (1200×630, minimum 1200×600).

B. `src/app/layout.tsx` (4 edits):
   • Swapped `OG_IMAGE` from `${SITE_URL}/og.svg` → `${SITE_URL}/og.png` and updated the explanatory comment to document WHY PNG (social platforms silently fail to render SVG OG images — Twitter/X, Facebook, LinkedIn, Slack, Discord all reject SVG).
   • Removed the `faqSchema` const (lines 109-246 of the pre-change file — the 16-Q&A FAQPage schema) AND its `<script type="application/ld+json">` block in `<body>`. Moved to `/faq/page.tsx` (see change D below) so it appears only on the page that actually renders the visible Q&A.
   • Removed the `breadcrumbSchema` const (the flat 6-page BreadcrumbList) AND its `<script type="application/ld+json">` block. Replaced with per-page `[Home, Current]` breadcrumb schemas in each of the 5 non-home page.tsx files (home page doesn't need a breadcrumb — single-item breadcrumb lists aren't useful per Google's guidelines).
   • Fixed `softwareApplicationSchema.offers[].url` from `${SITE_URL}#pricing` → `${SITE_URL}/pricing/` for both Core and Pro offers.
   • Added `themeColor: "#8291AF"` to the `viewport` export (matches the `theme_color` in `manifest.ts` and the brand's aurora/sage accent). Colors the Android Chrome tab bar and Safari status-bar background for a more "native app" feel on mobile.

C. `src/app/{features,demo,pricing,about,faq}/page.tsx` (5 edits, one per file):
   • Swapped `OG_IMAGE` from `${SITE_URL}/og.svg` → `${SITE_URL}/og.png` (with the same PNG-rationale comment as layout.tsx, abbreviated).
   • Added a page-specific `breadcrumbSchema` const — exactly 2 `ListItem` entries: `[Inicio → ${SITE_URL}/, <PageName> → ${SITE_URL}/<path>/]`. Matches the actual page hierarchy (Home → Current) instead of the flat 6-page list.
   • Rendered the breadcrumb schema as a `<script type="application/ld+json">` immediately after `<PageHeader />` (the first child of the page fragment), following the same pattern layout.tsx uses for the global schemas.

D. `src/app/faq/page.tsx` (1 edit, in addition to the OG image + breadcrumb changes from C):
   • Added the `faqSchema` const (16-Q&A FAQPage schema) — verbatim copy of what was removed from layout.tsx, with a new explanatory doc-comment noting WHY it lives here and not in layout.tsx (Google guidelines + manual-action penalty risk).
   • Rendered it as a second `<script type="application/ld+json">` after the breadcrumb script, before `<FAQ />`.

E. `src/app/page.tsx` (home — 1 edit):
   • Swapped `OG_IMAGE` from `${SITE_URL}/og.svg` → `${SITE_URL}/og.png` and updated the comment.
   • No breadcrumb schema added — single-item breadcrumb lists (just `[Home]`) aren't useful per Google's guidelines. The home page is the root of the hierarchy so it doesn't need a breadcrumb trail.

VERIFICATION:

- `bun run lint` → exit 0, zero warnings, zero errors. ✓
- `bun run build` → Next.js 16.1.3 Turbopack compiled successfully in 11.3s; ✓ Generating static pages (12/12) in 437.5ms. All 12 routes prerendered as static content (`/`, `/_not-found`, `/about`, `/demo`, `/faq`, `/features`, `/icon.svg`, `/manifest.webmanifest`, `/pricing`, `/robots.txt`, `/sitemap.xml`). ✓

- Per-page output verification (grepping the prerendered HTML in `.next/server/app/`):
  • **OG image**: 28 references to `og.png` across all 6 HTML files, 0 references to `og.svg` in any HTML output. Every `<meta property="og:image">` and `<meta name="twitter:image">` tag now points to `https://mmortexx.github.io/JournalTradingWeb/og.png`. ✓
  • **FAQPage schema**: Present ONLY on `faq.html` (1 occurrence). 0 occurrences on index/features/demo/pricing/about. Google guideline compliance restored. ✓
  • **BreadcrumbList schema**: 0 occurrences on `index.html` (home, correct — no breadcrumb needed). 1 occurrence each on features/demo/pricing/about/faq, each with exactly 2 ListItem entries (Home + Current) verified via Python regex count. ✓
  • **SoftwareApplication schema**: 1 occurrence on every page (global in layout.tsx, unchanged in scope). ✓
  • **Organization schema**: 1 occurrence on every page (global in layout.tsx, unchanged in scope). ✓
  • **SoftwareApplication offer URLs**: Both `Core` and `Pro` offers now point to `https://mmortexx.github.io/JournalTradingWeb/pricing/` (was `${SITE_URL}#pricing` — broken anchor). ✓
  • **Twitter card**: All 6 pages emit `<meta name="twitter:card" content="summary_large_image">`. ✓
  • **Canonical URL**: All 6 pages emit `<link rel="canonical">` with the correct per-page URL (`/`, `/features/`, `/demo/`, `/pricing/`, `/about/`, `/faq/`). ✓
  • **og:title / og:description / og:url / twitter:title / twitter:description**: Each page emits unique, page-specific values (e.g. `og:title` for index = "Trading Journal — Tu operativa, medida."; for features = "Características — Trading Journal"; for demo = "Demo en vivo — Trading Journal"; etc.). ✓
  • **`<title>` tag**: Home uses `title: { absolute: "..." }` so it renders verbatim without the `%s · Trading Journal` template suffix ("Trading Journal — Tu operativa, medida."); the other 5 pages render with the template applied ("Características · Trading Journal", "Demo en vivo · Trading Journal", "Precios · Trading Journal", "Acerca de · Trading Journal", "FAQ · Trading Journal"). ✓
  • **theme-color meta**: `<meta name="theme-color" content="#8291AF">` now present in `<head>` of every page (Next.js auto-generates from `viewport.themeColor`). ✓
  • **robots.txt**: `User-Agent: * / Allow: / / Sitemap: https://mmortexx.github.io/JournalTradingWeb/sitemap.xml`. ✓
  • **sitemap.xml**: Lists all 6 URLs with correct per-page priority (1.0/0.9/0.9/0.8/0.6/0.7 for home/features/pricing/demo/about/faq) and changeFrequency (weekly for home+features, monthly for the rest). Frozen `lastmod` 2025-01-01 (intentional per the existing design — see sitemap.ts comment). ✓
  • **manifest.webmanifest**: Full PWA manifest with name, short_name, description, start_url `/`, display `standalone`, background_color `#121418`, theme_color `#8291AF` (matches the new viewport.themeColor), lang `es`, and an inline SVG icon. ✓

Stage Summary:
- All 6 pages verified to have title, description, canonical URL, openGraph (title/description/url/type/siteName/locale/alternateLocale/images), and twitter card (card/title/description/images) — all 5 required fields present and unique per page.
- `robots.ts`, `sitemap.ts`, `manifest.ts` verified correct and unchanged in scope.
- 4 substantive SEO/structured-data issues fixed in this round:
  1. FAQ JSON-LD moved from global `layout.tsx` to `/faq/page.tsx` only (Google guidelines compliance — eliminates the manual-action penalty risk from emitting FAQ schema on non-FAQ pages).
  2. `SoftwareApplication` offer URLs fixed from broken `${SITE_URL}#pricing` anchor → `${SITE_URL}/pricing/` (the offers now point to the actual pricing page).
  3. `BreadcrumbList` schema made page-specific ([Home, Current]) instead of the misleading flat 6-page list on every page (Google guidelines compliance — breadcrumbs now reflect the actual page hierarchy).
  4. OG image converted from SVG → PNG via sharp (Twitter/X, Facebook, LinkedIn, Slack, Discord all silently fail to render SVG OG images; the new 1200×630 PNG renders correctly on every social platform).
- Plus one polish item: `themeColor: "#8291AF"` added to the viewport export, matching the manifest's `theme_color`, for native-app-feel mobile browser chrome coloring.
- Lint clean (0/0). Build clean — 12/12 routes prerendered as static content.
- Next actions: none — SEO metadata polish is complete. The site is now correctly indexable, has valid structured data on every page (SoftwareApplication + Organization globally, BreadcrumbList per-page on non-home routes, FAQPage only on /faq), and the OG image renders correctly on every major social platform. If a sibling R4 agent commits this work, the diff to stage is: `public/og.png` (new), `src/app/layout.tsx`, `src/app/page.tsx`, `src/app/{features,demo,pricing,about,faq}/page.tsx`.

Task ID: D18
Agent: general-purpose (R4 performance + lazy-loading verification)
Task: Verify performance optimizations: (1) heavy sections lazy-loaded with next/dynamic on Home page, (2) all chart components use React.memo, (3) content-visibility: auto on below-fold sections, (4) all useEffect cleanups (no memory leaks), (5) images use next/image with proper sizes, (6) no unnecessary re-renders in demo pages (useMemo on derived data). Fix any issues. After: `bun run lint` + `bun run build`.

Work Log:
- Read worklog.md (5161 lines) to absorb prior context: D15 (Round 3 build+push) shipped commit `821fce3` locally but the push to origin/main was blocked (no GitHub token in sandbox). Round 4 work is being done in parallel by multiple sibling agents — D16 (per-page BreadcrumbList + OG-image SVG→PNG + FAQ schema location fix), D17 (full SEO metadata audit) — both reported lint-clean and build-clean. This D18 task runs concurrently as the performance + lazy-loading verification pass.

PRE-CHANGE AUDIT — Baseline verification before any edits:
- `bun run lint` → exit 0, zero warnings, zero errors (clean baseline, matching D17's findings).
- `bun run build` → Next.js 16.1.3 Turbopack compiled successfully in 11.8s; ✓ Generating static pages (12/12) in 478.6ms. All 12 routes prerendered as static content.

PER-CHECKLIST VERIFICATION:

1. Heavy sections lazy-loaded with next/dynamic on Home page — VERIFIED ✓
   • `src/app/page.tsx` (home) splits the 4 heavy below-fold sections into separate JS chunks via `next/dynamic` with a `sectionFallback` skeleton (360px-tall spacer to avoid CLS while the chunk loads): `Bento`, `FeaturePreview`, `HomeCTASection`, `FinalCTA`. The two above-fold sections (`Hero`, `Ticker`) and the `PageTransition` wrapper are imported statically so they ship in the initial bundle.
   • Same pattern applied on the other 4 marketing pages — `/about` (5 dynamic sections: SocialProof, TestimonialsWall, Changelog, Milestones, Newsletter), `/features` (14 dynamic sections — every heavy section is dynamic), `/demo` (4 dynamic sections: DemoGallery, StatsBand, DemoReadyToBuy, FinalCTA), `/pricing` (8 dynamic sections: Comparison, PricingFAQ, TrustStrip, ValueTestimonials, StatsBand, Newsletter, DownloadCTA, FinalCTA), `/faq` (4 dynamic sections: StillHaveQuestions, ContactSupport, ContactForm, FinalCTA).
   • `/demo` additionally wraps `<AppDemoClient />` in a `next/dynamic({ ssr: false })` import (in `src/components/demo/AppDemoClient.tsx`) so the entire demo bundle (7 pages + Framer Motion + canvas charts) NEVER ships in the SSR payload — it only loads on the client when the demo section is reached. A `DemoSkeleton` reserves the full window height (588px mobile / 668px sm+ / 748px md+) to avoid CLS during hydration.
   • Verified the dynamic chunks actually appear in the build manifest: `page_client-reference-manifest.js` lists `Bento.tsx`, `FinalCTA.tsx`, `FeaturePreview.tsx`, `HomeCTASection.tsx` as separate client modules with 7 chunks each — confirming the code-splitting is in effect.

2. All chart components use React.memo — FIXED (HeroEquity was missing memo) ✓
   • Audited `src/components/charts/`: 5 chart components total — `EquityCurve`, `MiniCalendar`, `Histogram`, `Heatmap`, `HeroEquity`.
   • `EquityCurve` ✓ — wrapped in `memo(function EquityCurve(...))`, uses `useMemo` for the SVG path math keyed on `[equityCurve, drawdownCeiling, padL, padR, padT, padB, H]`.
   • `MiniCalendar` ✓ — wrapped in `memo(function MiniCalendar(...))`, uses `useMemo` for `dailyPnlForMonth` keyed on `[trades, offset]`.
   • `Histogram` ✓ — wrapped in `memo(function Histogram(...))`, uses `useMemo` for `maxCount` keyed on `[data]`.
   • `Heatmap` ✓ — wrapped in `memo(function Heatmap(...))`, uses `useMemo` for `grid` (heatmap), `countGrid` (trade counts), and `maxAbs` keyed on `[trades]`/`[grid]`.
   • `HeroEquity` ✗ → FIXED — was NOT wrapped in `memo()` despite using `useMemo` for its SVG path. Since it takes no props the memoization is technically a no-op today, but wrapping it future-proofs against re-renders if props are added later AND keeps the chart-component API surface consistent. Added `export const HeroEquity = memo(function HeroEquity() { ... })` and updated the doc comment to explain the rationale.

3. content-visibility: auto on below-fold sections — FIXED (4 home sections + 4 other-page sections missing cv-auto) ✓
   • Audited `cv-auto` usage: the `.cv-auto` utility class is defined in `globals.css` (`content-visibility: auto; contain-intrinsic-size: auto 500px;`) and was applied to 7 sections: `TestimonialsWall`, `SocialProof`, `Milestones`, `Wrapped`, `Changelog`, `BlogPreview`, `Comparison`.
   • The home page's 4 below-fold sections were MISSING `cv-auto`: `Bento`, `FeaturePreview`, `HomeCTASection`, `FinalCTA`. Each was already lazy-loaded via `next/dynamic` (so the JS chunk is split), but `next/dynamic` without `ssr: false` STILL server-renders the component's HTML — so the SSR markup is shipped in the initial HTML payload and the browser would still pay layout/paint cost for the rendered content. Adding `cv-auto` lets the browser skip layout/paint for these sections until the user scrolls them into view.
   • Added `cv-auto` to the `<section>` className of all 4 home page below-fold sections: `Bento.tsx`, `FeaturePreview.tsx`, `HomeCTASection.tsx`, `FinalCTA.tsx`.
   • Extended the same fix to the heaviest below-fold sections on the other marketing pages: `Pricing.tsx` (the main pricing cards section on /pricing), `FAQ.tsx` (the long accordion on /faq), `DemoGallery.tsx` (8 images on /demo), `DemoCapabilities.tsx` (6 feature cards on /demo).
   • Considered adding `cv-auto` to `Guardian.tsx` but REVERTED — Guardian uses `useScroll({ target: sectionRef, offset: ["start end", "end start"] })` for a scroll-linked parallax (shield translates slower than copy). With `cv-auto`'s `contain-intrinsic-size: auto 500px` placeholder, the section's bounding box is initially 500px (placeholder) and snaps to the real ~700px size only when it scrolls into view. This size jump would cause `useScroll`'s progress calculation to rebind mid-scroll and produce visible parallax jitter. The other cv-auto sections only use one-shot `whileInView` animations (IntersectionObserver-fired), which are robust to size changes. Confirmed by checking `grep -l "useScroll"` — only Guardian, StatsBand, Ticker use scroll-linked animations, and none of them have cv-auto.
   • Verified the built HTML output: `cv-auto` now appears in `index.html` (4× — Bento/FeaturePreview/HomeCTASection/FinalCTA), `about.html` (5× — SocialProof/TestimonialsWall/Changelog/Milestones/FinalCTA, the first 4 pre-existing), `pricing.html` (3× — Comparison/Pricing/FinalCTA), `faq.html` (2× — FAQ/FinalCTA), `demo.html` (3× — DemoCapabilities/DemoGallery/FinalCTA).

4. All useEffect cleanups (no memory leaks) — VERIFIED ✓
   • Audited all 37 files containing `useEffect` across `src/`. Categorized each effect:
     - **Effects that register a listener / observer / RAF / timeout and return a cleanup** — VERIFIED CLEAN:
       • `AppDemo.tsx` — 2 keydown listeners (Escape + F-key) + 2 unmount-timer clearers (ShareButton, ResetButton) ✓
       • `TopNav.tsx` — keydown listener (digit shortcuts 1–6) ✓
       • `TradesPage.tsx` — debounce `setTimeout` cleared ✓
       • `SettingsPage.tsx` — `uiTimers` array of `setTimeout` IDs all cleared on unmount ✓
       • `Navbar.tsx` — 3 effects (scroll listener, keydown listener, body-scroll-lock restoration) all cleaned ✓
       • `CommandPalette.tsx` — Cmd+K keydown listener + Escape capture-phase listener ✓
       • `ShortcutsHelp.tsx` — custom event listener + Escape capture-phase listener ✓
       • `GlobalShortcuts.tsx` — T/L/? keydown listener ✓
       • `CookieConsent.tsx` — 2s reveal `setTimeout` cleared ✓
       • `CursorGlow.tsx` — matchMedia change listener + pointermove/pointerout/pointerover listeners ✓
       • `ComparisonSlider.tsx` — pointerup + pointermove listeners (drag-end handlers) ✓
       • `CountUp.tsx` — IntersectionObserver disconnect + RAF cancel ✓
       • `AnimatedNumber.tsx` — RAF cancel ✓
       • `useInView.ts` — IntersectionObserver disconnect ✓
       • `BackToTop.tsx` — scroll listener ✓
       • `YouAreHere.tsx` — scroll listener ✓
       • `SectionNav.tsx` — IntersectionObserver disconnect ✓
       • `LiveActivity.tsx` — `setInterval` cleared + IntersectionObserver disconnect ✓
       • `FeatureRotator.tsx` — `setInterval` cleared ✓
       • `MarketClock.tsx` — `setInterval` cleared ✓
       • `PlanSwitcher.tsx` — RAF canceled on unmount + before next frame ✓
       • `ParticleField.tsx` — RAF cancel + resize/mousemove/mouseleave listeners removed ✓
       • `SlowMoChart.tsx` — RAF cancel ✓
       • `MarketBackground.tsx` — RAF cancel + resize listener removed ✓
       • `ThemeTransition.tsx` — MutationObserver disconnect ✓
       • `use-mobile.ts` — matchMedia change listener ✓
       • `use-toast.ts` — interval cleared on unmount ✓
       • `ui/sidebar.tsx` — keydown listener ✓
       • `ui/carousel.tsx` — RAF + visibilitychange + focus listeners ✓
     - **Effects that don't need a cleanup** (verified correct — they only set DOM/localStorage/state once and don't register listeners):
       • `i18n.tsx` — `document.documentElement.lang = lang` (DOM mutation, no listener) ✓
       • `theme.tsx` — 3 effects (read saved prefs on mount, sync theme to DOM+localStorage, sync palette to DOM+localStorage) — pure DOM/localStorage writes ✓
       • `error.tsx` — `console.error` on the error prop (one-shot logging) ✓
       • `FAQ.tsx` — pre-fill search query from `?q=` URL param on mount (one-shot read) ✓
       • `Navbar.tsx` L74 — `setMobileOpen(false)` on pathname change (state reset) ✓
       • `RiskCalculator.tsx` — `target.set(value)` on MotionValue (the MotionValue cleans itself up on unmount via Framer Motion's internals) ✓
       • `GlossaryModal.tsx` — 4 effects (load recent terms, reset filters on open, clamp activeIndex, scrollIntoView) — all state mutations ✓
       • `ScrollToTop.tsx` — `window.scrollTo` on pathname change (one-shot side effect) ✓
       • `ui/calendar.tsx` — shadcn primitive, one-shot focus management ✓
   • No memory leaks identified. Every effect that registers a side-effecting primitive (listener, observer, RAF, interval, timeout) returns the appropriate cleanup function.

5. Images use next/image with proper sizes — FIXED (FeatureImage sizes was hardcoded; per-use override now possible) ✓
   • Audited `next/image` usage: only 2 files import `next/image` directly — `src/components/tj/FeatureImage.tsx` (the reusable wrapper) and `src/components/marketing/FeaturePreview.tsx` (uses `next/image` directly, not via `FeatureImage`).
   • All `<Image>` instances use `fill` + `unoptimized` (correct for Next.js `output: "export"` static export) + `sizes` (for browser layout hint).
   • `FeaturePreview.tsx` already had correct `sizes="(max-width: 768px) 100vw, 33vw"` matching its `grid md:grid-cols-3` layout — VERIFIED ✓.
   • `FeatureImage.tsx` had a hardcoded `sizes="(max-width: 768px) 100vw, (max-width: 1200px) 50vw, 33vw"` — fine for the `FeaturePreview` 3-up grid but WRONG for the 2 callers that use different grids:
     • `DemoGallery.tsx` uses `grid md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-4` → the correct sizes are `(max-width: 768px) 100vw, (max-width: 1024px) 50vw, 25vw` (not 33vw at lg+).
     • `Gallery.tsx` uses a bento-style `grid md:grid-cols-3` with some cards spanning 2 columns — the default 33vw is acceptable since the largest image (2/3 width) is approximated by the responsive hint.
   • FIXED: added an optional `sizes?: string` prop to `FeatureImageProps` (defaulting to the existing 3-up hint for backward compatibility) and forwarded it to `<Image sizes={sizes} />`. Updated `DemoGallery.tsx` to pass `sizes="(max-width: 768px) 100vw, (max-width: 1024px) 50vw, 25vw"` matching its 2-up/4-up responsive grid.
   • Note: since `unoptimized` is set, `next/image` doesn't generate srcset variants — the `sizes` attribute serves only as a layout hint to the browser for the placeholder reservation. Still good practice to have correct values per-use so the browser's pre-render layout calculations match the final painted size.

6. No unnecessary re-renders in demo pages (useMemo on derived data) — VERIFIED ✓
   • Audited each of the 7 demo pages in `src/components/demo/pages/`:
     • `DashboardPage.tsx` — `useMemo` on `riskUsd` (calc) + `sessionTrades` (filter); uses `useMemo` for derived KPI data ✓
     • `TradesPage.tsx` — `useMemo` on `customIds` + `allTrades` (sorted merge) + `filtered` (filter) + `metrics` (computeMetrics); also `memo()`'s `TradeRow` and `PnlCell` row components to prevent re-renders when the parent re-renders but the row's props are stable ✓
     • `AnalyticsPage.tsx` — `useMemo` on `filteredTrades`, `m` (metrics), `rHist`, `pnlHist`, `durHist`, `setupRanks`, `instRanks`, `sparkPnl`, `sparkR`, `sparkEquity`, plus `sorted`, `maxAbs`, `rows` for the sub-components (WeekdayBreakdown, MonthlyBreakdown, RHistogram) ✓
     • `JournalPage.tsx` — `useMemo` on `weekly` (weekdayBreakdown), `monthly` (monthlyBreakdown), `history` (buildHistory) — all keyed on `[]` (stable since they derive from the module-level `TRADES` constant) ✓
     • `PlaybookPage.tsx` — `useMemo` on `path` (buildSparkPath), `trades`, `metrics`, `balances`, `ranking`, `mostTraded` ✓
     • `TradeDetailPage.tsx` — no `useMemo` needed; the only derived value is `const trade = TRADES.find(...)` which is a single-pass lookup on a 60-item array returning a stable reference ✓
     • `SettingsPage.tsx` — no `useMemo` needed; the page is pure form-state UI with no derived data transformations ✓
   • `DemoContext.tsx` (the shared state holder) — `useMemo` on the context `value` object keyed on `[page, selectedTradeId, filters, fullscreen, tourActive, dismissTour]`, plus `useCallback` on `dismissTour` for stable identity ✓
   • `CommandPalette.tsx` — `useCallback` on `navigate` and `run` for stable function identity ✓
   • No unnecessary re-renders identified — every page that computes derived data from `TRADES` / `METRICS` / filters / user input wraps the computation in `useMemo` with the correct dependency array.

Stage Summary:
- All 6 checklist items verified. 3 issues found and fixed:
  1. **HeroEquity was missing `React.memo`** — wrapped in `memo()` to match the other 4 chart components. No re-render behavior change today (component takes no props) but the API surface is now consistent and future-proof.
  2. **Home page below-fold sections were missing `content-visibility: auto`** — `next/dynamic` code-splits the JS chunks but still SSRs the section HTML, so the browser still pays layout/paint cost on first paint. Adding `cv-auto` lets the browser skip layout/paint for offscreen sections until the user scrolls them into view. Applied to all 4 home below-fold sections (Bento, FeaturePreview, HomeCTASection, FinalCTA) plus 4 high-impact sections on other pages (Pricing, FAQ, DemoCapabilities, DemoGallery).
  3. **`FeatureImage` had a hardcoded `sizes` attribute** — wrong for `DemoGallery`'s 2-up/4-up responsive grid (was emitting 33vw at lg+ but actual width is 25vw). Added an optional `sizes` prop to `FeatureImage` (backward-compatible default) and pass the correct per-grid value from `DemoGallery`.
- The remaining 3 checklist items (next/dynamic lazy-loading, useEffect cleanups, useMemo on derived data) were already fully in place — verified by exhaustive audit and no fixes needed.

- Files touched: 10 (1 chart component + 7 marketing components + 2 demo components + 1 tj primitive). All changes are additive (no behavior changes to existing functionality).

Task: Build, commit, push Round 4 — content accuracy, SEO, performance, demo interactivity.

Work Log:
- Read worklog.md (5039 lines initially) and prior context: D15 (Round 3 build+push) reported a local-only commit `821fce3` because the push step was blocked by missing GitHub token in sandbox. Re-checked git state on entry — `git rev-list --left-right --count origin/main...HEAD` returned `0   0`, meaning D15's commit had since been pushed externally to origin/main (HEAD `821fce3c18aa0b9d94d3e700342a2e0b0c31b507` confirmed via `git ls-remote origin main`); sandbox was fully in sync.
- Polled the working tree every 30–60 s for ~12 minutes to detect parallel sibling R4 agents. Initial poll showed working tree clean; within ~30 s of starting, sibling agents began flushing files — first D16 (content-accuracy) and D17 (SEO metadata) reported lint-clean and build-clean and appended their worklog entries, then D18 (performance + lazy-loading verification) appended its entry, and a fourth sibling made file-level edits to `src/lib/trading/demoStore.ts` (cache-invalidation pre-dispatch) and `src/components/demo/pages/TradeDetailPage.tsx` (merge custom trades into detail view) for demo interactivity but had not appended a worklog entry by the time the working tree stabilized.
- Stabilization timestamp: last source-file write at 09:24 UTC (`src/lib/trading/demoStore.ts`); last worklog.md write at 09:23:17 UTC (D18 entry); both re-verified unchanged 60 s and 90 s later. Total of 22 modified + 1 new (untracked) file in the working tree at commit time (23 entries plus `worklog.md` itself = 24 once I append this D20 entry).

- Inventory of the working tree at commit time:
  • `README.md` (D16 — line 21 glossary count `24 términos` → `30 términos` to match the actual 30-term GLOSSARY array).
  • `src/lib/trading/data.ts` (D16 — line 115 win-rate threshold `rnd() < 0.48` → `rnd() < 0.53` so the deterministic seed (20260716) produces 35 wins / 72 trades = 48.6%, matching the README's "~48% win rate" claim; previously 41.7%).
  • `public/og.png` (D17 — NEW, 1200×630, 26.7 KB PNG, 8-bit colormap, converted from `public/og.svg` via sharp; Twitter/X/Facebook/LinkedIn/Slack/Discord all silently fail to render SVG OG images).
  • `src/app/layout.tsx` (D17 — `OG_IMAGE` `${SITE_URL}/og.svg` → `${SITE_URL}/og.png`; removed global `faqSchema` (16-Q&A FAQPage) and flat 6-page `breadcrumbSchema`; fixed `softwareApplicationSchema.offers[].url` from `${SITE_URL}#pricing` → `${SITE_URL}/pricing/`; added `themeColor: "#8291AF"` to viewport export).
  • `src/app/{features,demo,pricing,about,faq}/page.tsx` (D17 — 5 edits each: OG_IMAGE swap + new page-specific `breadcrumbSchema` const (exactly 2 ListItem entries: [Inicio, Current]) rendered as `<script type="application/ld+json">`).
  • `src/app/faq/page.tsx` (D17 — additional edit: added `faqSchema` const + script block so FAQPage schema appears ONLY on /faq, Google-guideline compliant).
  • `src/app/page.tsx` (D17 — home: OG_IMAGE swap; no breadcrumb schema per Google's "single-item breadcrumb lists aren't useful" guideline).
  • `src/components/charts/HeroEquity.tsx` (D18 — wrapped in `memo()` to match the other 4 chart components (EquityCurve, MiniCalendar, Histogram, Heatmap); future-proofs against parent re-renders).
  • `src/components/marketing/{Bento,FeaturePreview,HomeCTASection,FinalCTA,Pricing,FAQ}.tsx` (D18 — added `cv-auto` utility className to root `<section>` so `content-visibility: auto; contain-intrinsic-size: auto 500px` skips layout/paint for these below-fold sections until scrolled into view; net SSR HTML still ships but browser avoids the cost; verified `cv-auto` appears in built HTML — index.html ×4, about.html ×5, pricing.html ×3, faq.html ×2, demo.html ×3).
  • `src/components/demo/{DemoCapabilities,DemoGallery}.tsx` (D18 — same `cv-auto` addition; DemoGallery also passes `sizes="(max-width: 768px) 100vw, (max-width: 1024px) 50vw, 25vw"` matching its `md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-4` responsive grid).
  • `src/components/tj/FeatureImage.tsx` (D18 — added optional `sizes?: string` prop defaulting to `(max-width: 768px) 100vw, (max-width: 1200px) 50vw, 33vw` (3-up hint), forwarded to `<Image sizes={sizes} />`; backward-compatible).
  • `src/lib/trading/demoStore.ts` (sibling — pre-dispatch `invalidateSnapshot()` calls in both `writeRaw` and `resetToSample` so the in-memory cache is fresh even when no `useCustomTrades()` consumer is mounted yet to receive the dispatched event; fixes the bug where a trade added via the Dashboard composer (no TradesPage mounted) would write to localStorage but leave the cache stale, so a TradesPage that mounts afterwards wouldn't see the new trade until the next external mutation).
  • `src/components/demo/pages/TradeDetailPage.tsx` (sibling — imports `useCustomTrades, customTradeToTrade` from `@/lib/trading/demoStore`; merges custom trades into the lookup via `useMemo` so a custom-trade row clicked in TradesPage routes here with a `selectedTradeId` (Date.now() timestamp, not 1..72) and now finds the matching custom trade instead of rendering the empty state).
  • `worklog.md` (D16 + D17 + D18 sibling entries appended; this D20 entry).

- Pre-flight checks (run before staging):
  • `bun run lint` → exit 0, zero warnings, zero errors (`eslint .` with no output) across all 22 modified + 1 new source file.
  • `rm -rf .next out && bun run build` → Next.js 16.1.3 Turbopack compiled successfully; ✓ Generating static pages (12/12) in 449.7ms. All 12 routes prerendered as static content: `/`, `/_not-found`, `/about`, `/demo`, `/faq`, `/features`, `/icon.svg`, `/manifest.webmanifest`, `/pricing`, `/robots.txt`, `/sitemap.xml`. No build errors, no type errors.
  • Verified `public/og.png` is a valid PNG (1200×630, 8-bit colormap, non-interlaced, 26.7 KB) via `file` command — passes the Twitter large-image card spec (minimum 1200×600).
  • Killed any stale `next dev` server before the build to release the `.next/lock` and avoid the documented Turbopack `_buildManifest.js.tmp.XXX` ENOENT race condition (none was running at build time, but the `pkill -f "next dev" / next-server` cleanup was run defensively).

- Staging + commit (excluding dev artifacts per orchestrator spec):
  • `git add -A && git reset HEAD -- .env .zscripts agent-ctx tool-results db/custom.db mini-services/.gitkeep dev.log dev.pid start-dev.sh download upload out .next` — staged 23 source files + worklog.md (24 entries total); `.zscripts/`, `agent-ctx/`, `tool-results/`, `db/`, `dev.log`, `dev.pid`, `start-dev.sh`, `download/`, `upload/`, `out/`, `.next/`, `.env` excluded.
  • `git commit -m "fix: Round 4 — content accuracy, SEO, performance, demo interactivity"` — committed with the orchestrator-prescribed message capturing all 4 R4 sibling contributions: D16 (README glossary count 24→30 + win-rate threshold 0.48→0.53 to match README's ~48% claim) / D17 (per-page BreadcrumbList + OG-image SVG→PNG + FAQ schema moved from layout.tsx to /faq/page.tsx only + SoftwareApplication offer URLs fixed from broken #pricing anchor to /pricing/ + themeColor added to viewport) / D18 (HeroEquity wrapped in memo + cv-auto added to 8 below-fold marketing/demo sections for content-visibility: auto + FeatureImage sizes prop for responsive grid hints) / unnamed demo-interactivity sibling (demoStore pre-dispatch invalidateSnapshot + TradeDetailPage merges custom trades so dashboard-composed trades route correctly into the detail page).

- Push sequence (orchestrator-prescribed `set remote with token` → `push` → `reset remote`):
  • Unlike D15 (which had no token source available in its sandbox), this D20 task description supplied the GitHub token inline in the push command, so the orchestrator-prescribed token URL pattern could be applied directly.
  • `git remote set-url origin https://mmortexx:ghp_***@github.com/mmortexx/JournalTradingWeb.git` — set the remote URL with the embedded Basic-auth token.
  • `git push origin main` — pushed successfully.
  • `git remote set-url origin https://github.com/mmortexx/JournalTradingWeb.git` — restored the clean public HTTPS remote URL.

- Sync verification: `git rev-list --left-right --count origin/main...HEAD` → `0   0` (0 commits behind, 0 commits ahead). Local HEAD is now in perfect sync with origin/main; the R4 commit is on the remote.

Stage Summary:
- Round 4 polish is fully staged, committed, lint-clean, build-clean, AND pushed to origin/main. The single atomic commit captures all 4 sibling R4 contributions in the order they were observed (D16 → D17 → D18 → unnamed demo-interactivity sibling). The D15-deferred R3 push loose-end (D15's commit was local-only at the time of its report) had already been externally completed before this task started, and this task's R4 push uses the token-in-URL pattern to avoid D15's "no token available" failure mode.
