### Price fix (11 edits across 9 files)
- **HeroVideo.tsx**:59 — `"Comprar — $29" / "Buy — $29"` → `"Comprar — desde $149" / "Buy — from $149"` (also embedded in the hero rewrite below).
- **Pricing.tsx**:73,83 — `price: 29` → `price: 149` (Core); `price: 49` → `price: 249` (Pro). Plan names, taglines, features, CTAs all preserved verbatim. The `<CountUp to={plan.price} />` and `text-4xl tnum` rendering unchanged — the count-up will now tween to 149/249 instead of 29/49.
- **PlanSwitcher.tsx**:106,116 — same Core 29→149, Pro 49→249 changes (the PlanSwitcher maintains its own `plans` record, separate from `Pricing`'s `plans` array). Also updated the 2 stale doc-comments at lines 24 (`29 → 49 or 49 → 29` → `149 → 249 or 249 → 149`) and 131 (`$ 29.4321` → `$ 149.4321`) for consistency. The rAF price-tween (`easeOutExpo`, `PRICE_TWEEN_MS = 700`) now animates between 149 and 249 — same UX, correct numbers.
- **pricing/page.tsx**:40,46,65 — SEO metadata strings updated from `"Core $29 · Pro $49"` → `"Core $149 · Pro $249"` in the `metadata.description`, `openGraph.description`, and `twitter.description`. Three strings, identical structure, only the numbers changed.
- **layout.tsx**:77,85 — schema.org `SoftwareApplication.offers[].price` updated from `"29"` / `"49"` → `"149"` / `"249"`. JSON-LD structured data, strings (not numbers) per schema.org spec. Same `priceCurrency: "USD"`, `availability`, `url` preserved.
- **FinalCTA.tsx**:72 — `<span className="tnum">$29</span>` → `<span className="tnum">$149</span>`. Already had the `"Comprar — desde "` prefix, so the CTA now reads "Comprar — desde $149 →" (matching the hero exactly).
- **DownloadCTA.tsx**:99,104 — `<span className="tnum text-white font-medium">$29</span>` → `$149` (both ES and EN strings). Already had the `"Pago único desde "` / `"One-time payment from "` prefix.
- **DemoReadyToBuy.tsx**:116 — `<span className="tnum text-gray-300 font-medium">$29</span>` → `$149`. The microcopy below the CTAs now reads "desde $149 · pago único · sin suscripción".
- **Comparison.tsx**:20 — `"Único · desde $29"` → `"Único · desde $149"` in the `Pago` row's Trading-Journal cell. Other two cells (Suscripción mensual, Gratis) untouched.
- **ValueTestimonials.tsx**:69,71 — Iván Riera's testimonial quote updated from `"Cincuenta dólares por algo que uso cinco horas al día..."` / `"Fifty bucks for something I use five hours a day..."` → `"$249 por algo que uso cinco horas al día..."` / `"$249 for something I use five hours a day..."`. The quote was previously referencing the Pro plan price ($49 → "fifty bucks"); now references the new Pro price ($249). The "ROI es obsceno" framing still works — for a serious trader using the app 5 hours a day, $249 pays for itself fast. The chip "ROI ×100" untouched. The other 2 testimonials (Adrià Vela "amortizado en 1 semana", Marta Lleida "−468 €/año vs nube") don't reference Trading Journal's own price — left untouched.

### Hero institutional upgrade (`src/components/marketing/HeroVideo.tsx`, 85 → 351 lines)
Rewrote the entire component via Write tool. Bilingual via `useLang()` preserved. `next/image` for background with `asset()` for basePath. No new CSS in globals.css — every visual layer uses existing utilities (`.liquid-glass`, `.eyebrow`, `.text-gradient`, `.grain`, `--accent-base`). Detailed layer-by-layer changes:

1. **Layered background stack** (4 z-indexed layers, all `pointer-events-none`):
   - z-0: `<Image>` golden pathway webp, `object-cover`, `fill`, `priority`, `unoptimized`, `sizes="100vw"`. Preserved verbatim from the old hero.
   - z-[1] top scrim: 40px-tall `linear-gradient(180deg, rgba(0,0,0,0.55) 0%, rgba(0,0,0,0.25) 50%, transparent 100%)` from the top edge. Softens the navbar boundary for legibility without darkening the image.
   - z-[1] radial accent glow: 840×420 blurred orb at `bottom-[18%]` centered, `radial-gradient(circle, rgb(var(--accent-base)), transparent 70%)` at 14% opacity, `blur-[150px]`. Gives the gold a "lit" quality behind the headline without an obvious orb.
   - z-[1] bottom scrim: 2/3-height `linear-gradient(180deg, transparent 0%, rgba(0,0,0,0.35) 35%, rgba(0,0,0,0.75) 70%, #000000 100%)`. Anchors headline legibility + blends the hero into the next dark section.
   - z-[2] `.grain` overlay: applies the opt-in `.grain::after` 3% fractalNoise texture across the whole hero (`<div className="grain absolute inset-0 z-[2] pointer-events-none" />`). The "expensive printed" feel.

2. **Eyebrow / kicker pill** (NEW): small inline-flex pill `rounded-full border border-white/15 bg-white/[0.04] backdrop-blur-sm px-3 py-1.5 mb-6` containing:
   - An accent dot: a 1.5×1.5 px relative span with an outer `animate-ping` ring at 60% opacity + an inner solid dot, both `background: rgb(var(--accent-base))`. The ping reads as a "live" / "broadcasting" signal — institutional product language (Stripe / Vercel).
   - The eyebrow text via `<span className="eyebrow !text-[10.5px] !text-gray-300">`: `"DIARIO DE TRADING · NATIVO DE WINDOWS"` / `"TRADING JOURNAL · WINDOWS NATIVE"`. Uses the `.eyebrow` utility for typography (uppercase 0.16em tracking 600) but overrides size to 10.5px and color to gray-300 for pill-context legibility. FadeIn delay=150ms duration=800ms.

3. **Headline** (refined): kept `AnimatedHeading` with `text={es ? "Tu operativa,\nmedida." : "Your trading,\nmeasured."}` and added the `highlight={es ? "medida." : "measured."}` prop — the AnimatedHeading component matches that substring within the second line (no `\n` inside the highlight — supported per the component's contract) and applies `.text-gradient` to those characters' own `display: inline-block` spans. The gradient renders correctly because each char gets its own gradient-clipped span. Style preserved: `fontSize: clamp(2.5rem, 7vw, 5.5rem)`, `letterSpacing: -0.04em`, `lineHeight: 1.0`, `text-white font-normal mb-5`. Institutional — not bold/screamy.

4. **Sub-headline** (refined): `text-base md:text-lg text-gray-300 mb-7 max-w-xl leading-relaxed`. Copy preserved verbatim ("El diario de trading profesional, nativo de Windows. Métricas institucionales..."). Spacing bumped from mb-5 → mb-7 for more breathing room above the CTAs (the trust strip now sits below them).

5. **CTAs** (refined):
   - Primary `"Comprar — desde $149" / "Buy — from $149"`: `group inline-flex items-center gap-2 rounded-lg bg-white text-black px-8 py-3.5 font-medium shadow-[0_1px_2px_rgb(0_0_0/0.20)] hover:-translate-y-0.5 hover:shadow-[0_4px_14px_rgb(0_0_0/0.30)] transition-all duration-200`. Same height as the secondary CTA (`py-3.5`). The shadow + `-translate-y-0.5` lift on hover gives the Stripe/Vercel premium affordance. Added a `→` arrow SVG (18×18, viewBox 0 0 16 16, `M3 8h9M8 4l4 4-4 4` path, currentColor stroke 1.6) with `transition-transform duration-200 group-hover:translate-x-1` so the arrow slides right on hover.
   - Secondary `"Probar la demo" / "Try the demo"`: `liquid-glass border border-white/20 text-white inline-flex items-center gap-2 px-8 py-3.5 rounded-lg font-medium hover:bg-white hover:text-black transition-all duration-200`. Same `py-3.5` for vertical alignment with the primary. Added a play-triangle SVG (16×16, `M5 3.5v9l7-4.5-7-4.5Z` path, currentColor fill + stroke 1.3) for the demo affordance.
   - Both wrapped in `flex flex-wrap items-center gap-4` (was `gap-4` — kept). FadeIn delay=1200ms duration=1000ms preserved.

6. **Trust strip** (NEW): inline `<ul>` of 4 small-icon credibility signals below the CTAs, separated by faint `·` dots (`text-gray-600`). Container: `mt-7 flex flex-wrap items-center gap-x-5 gap-y-2 text-xs text-gray-400`. Each `<TrustItem>` is `inline-flex items-center gap-1.5` with a 12×12 SVG (`text-gray-400`) + label. The 4 items:
   - Lock glyph (`M4 7V5a3 3 0 016 0v2...` — padlock outline) + "100% local" / "100% local"
   - Check glyph (`m4 8 2.5 2.5L12 5` — check mark) + "Pago único" / "One-time payment"
   - Globe glyph (circle + meridians + equator) + "ES + EN"
   - Shield glyph with check (`M8 2 2.5 4.5v3.5c0 3 2.4 5.2 5.5 6...` + `m6 8 1.5 1.5L10 7`) + "Garantía 30 días" / "30-day guarantee"
   - FadeIn delay=1400ms duration=1000ms. This is the institutional credibility row — quiet credentials, not feature cards.

7. **Right-column glass tag** (refined): the existing `liquid-glass border border-white/20` card with "Métricas. Disciplina. Local." kept, refined:
   - Now `lg:col-span-4` (was implicit half) within a 12-col grid (`lg:grid lg:grid-cols-12 lg:items-end lg:gap-8`). Left column is `lg:col-span-8`. Clean 8/4 institutional ratio.
   - Card classes: `liquid-glass border border-white/20 rounded-xl px-6 py-4 max-w-[320px]`. Added an accent left-border via inline style: `borderLeft: "2px solid rgb(var(--accent-base) / 0.55)"`. The accent-tinted left border reads as a "callout" treatment.
   - Heading text: `text-lg lg:text-xl font-light text-white leading-snug whitespace-nowrap` — `whitespace-nowrap` ensures "Métricas. Disciplina. Local." stays on ONE line (VLM confirmed this in the verification pass).
   - Added a second line: `mt-2 text-[11px] uppercase tracking-[0.16em] text-gray-400` reading `"Tres promesas, una app."` / `"Three promises, one app."` — a small institutional subtitle that ties the three keywords together.
   - Card container: `hidden lg:flex lg:col-span-4 items-end justify-end mt-0` — hidden below `lg` (where the layout collapses to single-column). FadeIn delay=1400ms duration=1000ms.

8. **Scroll cue** (NEW): centered at the bottom of the section (`absolute inset-x-0 bottom-5 z-10 flex flex-col items-center gap-1.5 pointer-events-none`). Contains:
   - Label: `text-[10px] uppercase tracking-[0.18em] text-gray-500` reading "Desplázate" / "Scroll"
   - SVG (18×22, viewBox 0 0 18 22) with `animate-bounce` (custom duration 2.4s for a calmer, more institutional cadence than the default 1s):
     - Rounded rect outline (`rect x=1 y=1 w=16 h=20 rx=8` — a mouse shape) at `stroke="rgb(255 255 255 / 0.25)"`
     - A vertical line (`M9 5v5` — the scroll wheel) at `stroke="rgb(255 255 255 / 0.7)"`
     - A chevron at the bottom (`M5 13l4 3 4-3` — downward arrow) at `stroke="rgb(var(--accent-base) / 0.85)"` — the only accent touch in the cue.
   - FadeIn delay=1800ms duration=1000ms (last to appear).

9. **Pixel-perfect**: `min-h-screen flex flex-col overflow-hidden bg-black pt-16` preserved. Content container: `relative z-10 flex-1 flex flex-col justify-end px-6 md:px-12 lg:px-16 pb-16 lg:pb-20` (was `pb-12 lg:pb-16` — bumped per task hint "pb-16 lg:pb-20"). The 12-col grid only kicks in at `lg` (`lg:grid lg:grid-cols-12`); below that, the left and right columns stack naturally (right card hidden via `hidden lg:flex`). No horizontal overflow — verified by VLM at 1280×577 viewport.

### Verification
- **Lint**: `cd /home/z/my-project && bun run lint` → EXIT=0, zero errors, zero warnings.
- **Dev server**: `curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:3000/` → 200.

  - `rg '\$29\b' src/` → ZERO matches ✓
  - `rg '\$49\b' src/` → ZERO matches ✓
  - `rg -i 'cincuenta dólares|fifty bucks' src/` → ZERO matches ✓
  - `rg '\$149' src/` → 10 matches across 7 files (HeroVideo, FinalCTA, DownloadCTA, DemoReadyToBuy, Comparison, pricing/page.tsx ×3, plus a HeroVideo.tsx doc-comment) ✓
  - `rg '\$249' src/` → 5 matches (ValueTestimonials ×2, pricing/page.tsx ×3) ✓
  - `rg 'price: (149|249)' src/` → 4 matches (Pricing.tsx + PlanSwitcher.tsx, Core + Pro) ✓
- **SSR HTML verification**: `curl -s http://localhost:3000/` → contains "Comprar — desde $149", "DIARIO DE TRADING", "NATIVO DE WINDOWS", "Métricas. Disciplina. Local.", "Garantía 30 días", "100% local", "Pago único", "Desplázate". All new hero markers present in the SSR'd HTML.
- **VLM visual verification** (z-ai vision, glm-4.6v, screenshot 1280×577):
  - First pass (with `max-w-[260px]` right card): VLM reported all 7 elements present but flagged that "Métricas. Disciplina. Local." appeared "stacked vertically" (text wrapped to 2 lines at the 260px max-width).
  - Fix: bumped right card `max-w-[260px]` → `max-w-[320px]` + added `whitespace-nowrap` to the heading text.
  - Second pass: VLM confirmed "Right card text: On one line (not wrapped) ✔️". Final verification pass returned ALL 6 elements present + well-aligned:
    • Headline "Tu operativa, medida." with "medida." in gold gradient ✔️
    • Primary CTA "Comprar — desde $149" (white background, dark text, right-arrow) ✔️
    • Secondary CTA "Probar la demo" (dark glass background) ✔️
    • Trust strip: "100% local · Pago único · ES + EN · Garantía 30 días" (4 items, dots) ✔️
    • Right-side glass card: "Métricas. Disciplina. Local." on one line, gold left border ✔️
    • Eyebrow pill: "DIARIO DE TRADING · NATIVO DE WINDOWS" with small gold dot ✔️
    • CTA buttons: Same height, vertically aligned ✔️
    • Trust strip text: Legible ✔️

Stage Summary:
- **Price fix (CRITICAL bug resolved)**: Reconciled 11 price references across 9 files. The wrong $29 Core / $49 Pro legacy prices are now ZERO matches across `src/`. The new $149 Core / $249 Pro prices are consistently rendered in: HeroVideo CTA ("Comprar — desde $149" / "Buy — from $149"), FinalCTA ($149), DownloadCTA ($149 ES+EN), DemoReadyToBuy ($149), Comparison row ("Único · desde $149"), ValueTestimonials Iván Riera quote ($249 ES+EN), pricing/page.tsx SEO metadata (Core $149 · Pro $249 ×3 strings), layout.tsx schema.org SoftwareApplication offers (price:"149" + price:"249"), Pricing.tsx plan prices (149 + 249), PlanSwitcher.tsx plan prices (149 + 249 + 2 stale doc-comments fixed). The count-up tween in PlanSwitcher now animates between 149 and 249 (same UX, correct numbers). Testimonial "Cincuenta dólares" / "Fifty bucks" updated to "$249" to match the new Pro price — the ROI framing still works for a serious trader.
- **Hero institutional upgrade**: 85 → 351 lines. Replaced the "generic web hero" with a Bloomberg/Stripe/Linear/Arc-launch aesthetic. Layered background (image + top scrim + radial accent glow + bottom scrim + .grain texture). Eyebrow pill with pulsing accent dot + tracked label. AnimatedHeading with `.text-gradient` highlight on "medida." / "measured." (via the component's existing `highlight` prop — no CSS changes). Refined CTAs (white primary with arrow translate-on-hover + shadow+lift; liquid-glass secondary with play icon; both `py-3.5` for vertical alignment). NEW trust strip with 4 small-icon credibility signals (100% local · Pago único · ES + EN · Garantía 30 días). Right-column glass tag refined with `lg:col-span-4` (within a new 12-col grid), accent left-border (`borderLeft: 2px solid rgb(var(--accent-base) / 0.55)`), `whitespace-nowrap` heading, and a small "Tres promesas, una app." / "Three promises, one app." subtitle. NEW animated scroll cue at the very bottom center (mouse-shape SVG with `animate-bounce` at 2.4s custom duration + accent chevron). Bilingual via `useLang()`. No new CSS in globals.css. No indigo/blue. Accent gold used sparingly: eyebrow dot, gradient headline, radial glow, tag left-border, scroll-cue chevron.
- **Lint**: exit 0, zero errors, zero warnings.
- **Dev server**: HTTP 200 on `/`. VLM (glm-4.6v) confirms all 6 hero elements render correctly with proper alignment, the right-card text on one line, the gradient "medida." visible, and the price showing "desde $149" (consistent with Pricing page).
- **Issues found**: None functionally. The sandbox dev server died once during verification (same artifact noted by R1-b/R1-c — the orchestrator's process supervisor kills detached node processes); restarted via `setsid` for the screenshot pass. The first VLM pass flagged the right-card text wrapping — fixed by widening `max-w` to 320px + adding `whitespace-nowrap`; second VLM pass confirmed the fix. All other CTAs across the site now show consistent $149 / $249 pricing. The orchestrator's J0 audit note ("Hero says $29 but Pricing says $149") was actually a wider problem — the entire codebase had $29/$49 — and is now fully reconciled.

- Read `src/app/globals.css` (1358 lines post-R1-a) end-to-end for the design-token system: `.liquid-glass` (rgba(0,0,0,0.4) + blur(4px) + machined inset 1px white top + 1px black bottom + `::before` rim gradient), `.eyebrow` (uppercase 0.16em 0.625rem 600 txt-tertiary), `.text-gradient` (linear-gradient(120deg, accent-base 0% → accent-hover 50% → accent-base 100%, 200% bg-size, transparent text fill), `.grain::after` (3% fractalNoise overlay via mix-blend-mode: overlay), `.aurora-bg`, `.divider-grad`, `.max-w-page` (1200px), `.rounded-card` (8px), `.text-primary/.text-secondary/.text-tertiary` (theme-adaptive semantic colors), `.tnum` (tabular-nums + slashed zero), `.pill` (inline-flex chip with 999px radius, 0.72rem 600 weight), `--accent-base: 199 167 107` (gold #C7A76B), `--pnl-pos/-neg/-warn` (#34D399/#EF4444/#E0932B), `depth-1/2/3/4` `@utility` shadows. Confirmed all utilities already in globals.css — no new CSS needed (task constraint: "don't add CSS to globals.css").
- Read `src/components/marketing/HeroVideo.tsx` (351 lines, post-R1-d) end-to-end to match the new hero language: eyebrow pill with accent dot + tracked label, AnimatedHeading with `.text-gradient` highlight, refined CTAs with arrow translate-on-hover + shadow+lift, trust strip with 4 small-icon credibility signals, right-column glass tag with accent left-border. Adopted the same eyebrow-pattern (icon well + `.eyebrow` kicker + `text-xl md:text-2xl font-semibold text-primary` title + `text-sm text-secondary` description) for Bento card headers so the two sections read as one system.
- Read both target files end-to-end:

No mini invoice, no calendar, no CSV card, no eyebrow kickers, no proper bento layout. Used `bg-white/8` icon wells (non-standard), `t-h3 font-medium text-white` titles, generic hover only.

No numbered eyebrow chips, no kicker labels, no `border-b` between image and content, hover used `group-hover:scale-[1.04]` (close to spec's `scale-105` but with old duration-700 + 4:3 aspect).
- Confirmed supporting context: `MistakeInvoice.tsx` already uses the same `METRICS.costOfIndiscipline` / `expectancyInPlan` / `expectancyBrokePlan` data with a "FACTURA" invoice pattern (dashed dividers, red TOTAL with `prefix="−$"` count-up). Adopted the same data source + invoice aesthetic for the Bento hero card's mini preview so the two sections tell the same story. `Integrations.tsx` already has the same 10-broker list (IB/M4/M5/NT/TV/BN/CB/OA/IG/TD) — reused verbatim for the Bento CSV card's broker chips. `Money.tsx` confirmed to use `fmtMoney` which prepends the unicode minus `−` for negative values (important for the invoice TOTAL rendering).
- Dev server verified running at start: `curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:3000/` → 200. Stayed 200 throughout all edits.

### Bento.tsx rewrite (165 → 608 lines, +443 net)
Wrote the entire component via Write tool + iterative MultiEdit. Bilingual via `useLang()` preserved. No new CSS. Detailed layer-by-layer changes:

1. **Section container** — `section-tight cv-auto bg-black relative overflow-hidden` preserved. Added `<div className="grain absolute inset-0 pointer-events-none" />` for the opt-in 3% fractalNoise texture (matches HeroVideo's grain layer). Kept the existing radial accent glow at the top (`radial-gradient(60% 50% at 50% 0%, rgb(var(--accent-base) / 0.08), transparent 70%)`) — anchors the section to the hero's dark video aesthetic. The grain overlay sits at z-0 so the `relative z-10` content stack floats above.

2. **Bento grid** — replaced the old `mt-10 grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 gap-4` 2×2 grid with a proper responsive 3-col bento: `mt-10 grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-4 md:gap-5`. Layout:
   - Row 1: `[ Disciplina hero (lg:col-span-2) ][ Métricas ]` — both at h=295 (consistent row height via `h-full`)
   - Row 2: `[ Playbook ][ 100 % local ][ Calendario P&L ]` — all 3 at h=460 (consistent row height)
   - Row 3: `[ Importación CSV (lg:col-span-3) ]` — h=210, full-width
   Verified via DOM eval: all 3 cards in each row share identical heights (295/460/210). The col-span-2 hero is 751px wide (2×365 + gap), the col-span-3 CSV card is 1136px wide (3×365 + 2×gap). Pixel-perfect.

3. **Card primitive refactor (CRITICAL bug fix)** — initially wrote `BentoCard` with the `className` prop landing on the inner `motion.div`. The `Reveal` wrapper (which is `motion.div` itself) was the actual grid item, so `lg:col-span-2` / `lg:col-span-3` classes had no effect — the layout collapsed to a flat 3×2 grid. DOM eval confirmed all cards were 365×983 (same width, all in one row). Fixed by splitting the prop into `outer` (grid-track classes — col-span, h-full — that land on the `Reveal` wrapper) + `inner` (surface classes — depth tokens — that land on the inner `motion.div`). Re-verified via DOM eval: Disciplina card now 751×295, CSV card 1136×210. Bug resolved.

4. **Card surface language** — every card uses `liquid-glass rounded-card p-6 h-full flex flex-col transition-[background-color,border-color,box-shadow,transform] duration-300 hover:border-white/20 hover:shadow-[0_8px_30px_rgb(var(--accent-base)/0.08)]`. The hover lift comes from framer-motion's `whileHover={{ y: -4, ... }}` (spring physics, stiffness 300, damping 24 — matches the rest of the site). The accent border glow at 8% opacity reads as a "premium engagement" affordance without breaking the dark-first palette.

5. **Card header rhythm** — every card opens with `<div className="flex items-center gap-3">` containing an `IconWell` + an `.eyebrow` kicker label. Then `mt-4 text-xl md:text-2xl font-semibold text-primary leading-tight` title. Then `mt-2 text-sm text-secondary leading-relaxed` description. Strict vertical rhythm — every card uses the exact same spacing so the grid reads as one system.

6. **IconWell primitive** (NEW) — `shrink-0 w-10 h-10 rounded-lg flex items-center justify-center bg-white/5 border border-white/10 shadow-[inset_0_1px_0_rgb(255_255_255/0.08)]` with the icon in `text-primary` (default) or `text-[rgb(var(--accent-base))]` (accent variant, used for Disciplina + Importación to signal the "hero" cards). The machined 1px white top highlight matches `.liquid-glass`'s edge language at a smaller scale. Replaces the old `bg-white/8` (non-standard opacity) + `text-white` (not theme-adaptive) icon container.

7. **NEW icons** — added `CalendarIcon` (rect + month-tabs + 5 small dots representing days) and `ImportIcon` (download arrow into a tray) for the two new cards. All icons inline SVG, `viewBox="0 0 24 24"`, `strokeWidth="1.6"` (matches the existing DisciplineIcon/MetricsIcon/PlaybookIcon/LocalIcon). 20-22px glyphs. No external icon deps.

8. **Disciplina hero card (lg:col-span-2, depth-3)** — the focal card of the bento. Internal 2-col layout (`flex flex-col md:flex-row md:items-start gap-6 h-full`):
   - Left (`flex-1 min-w-0`): IconWell(accent) + DisciplineIcon + `.eyebrow` "Disciplina" → `mt-4` title "Disciplina que cuesta dinero" → `mt-2` description.
   - Right (`md:w-[260px] shrink-0`): the `MiniInvoice` preview component.
   The card uses `depth-3` (vs `depth-2` for the other cards) so it visually pops as the focal element.

9. **MiniInvoice component (NEW)** — compact "FACTURA" preview that makes "discipline that costs money" literal:
   - Outer container: `rounded-lg border border-white/10 bg-black/40 overflow-hidden` + `box-shadow: inset 0 1px 0 rgb(255 255 255 / 0.04)` (machined edge at smaller scale).
   - Header row: `text-[9px] uppercase tracking-[0.2em] font-semibold text-tertiary` "Factura" / "Invoice" label + monospace `text-[10px] tnum text-secondary` `#IND-2026-07` invoice ID. Bg `bg-white/[0.02]` + bottom border.
   - Column headers: `grid-cols-[1fr_auto]` with `.eyebrow`-style "Concepto" / "Concept" + "Importe" / "Amount" — dashed bottom border.
   - 4 line items (Fuera de horario / Tamaño > plan / Sin stop / FOMO) — each row has `text-[11px] text-secondary` label + `text-[11px] text-pnl-neg tnum font-medium text-right` amount in monospace. Dashed dividers between rows. Amounts are the same `METRICS.costOfIndiscipline * pct` allocation used by MistakeInvoice so the two sections tell the same story.
   - Subtotal row: `text-[10px] text-tertiary` "Gap por operación × {brokeCount}" label + `text-[10px] text-pnl-warn tnum` gap amount (orange — warning tone). Bg `bg-white/[0.015]`.
   - TOTAL row: `flex items-center justify-between px-3 py-2 border-t border-pnl-neg/40 bg-pnl-neg/[0.06]` (subtle 6% red tint) + `text-[10px] uppercase tracking-[0.16em] font-semibold text-secondary` "Total" label + `text-base tnum font-semibold text-pnl-neg` (red, bold, larger) `<Money value={total} />` amount. The `value={total}` (raw signed value, since `METRICS.costOfIndiscipline` is already negative) lets Money prepend the unicode minus sign so the TOTAL reads as "−577,10 US$" — verified via DOM eval.
   All numbers use `fontFamily: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, monospace` + `.tnum` for tabular-nums + slashed zeros (financial typography).

10. **Métricas card (depth-2)** — IconWell + MetricsIcon + `.eyebrow` "Métricas" → title "Métricas institucionales" → description. `mt-auto pt-5` (pushes the chip strip to the bottom for visual rhythm) + `flex flex-wrap items-center gap-x-4 gap-y-1 text-[11px] uppercase tracking-[0.14em] text-tertiary` chip strip listing the 4 institutional metrics: `Sharpe · Sortino · Calmar · RF` (with `tnum` on each name + `text-gray-600` middots as separators).

11. **Playbook card (depth-2)** — IconWell + PlaybookIcon + `.eyebrow` "Playbook" → title "Playbook con stats en vivo" → description. (Kept compact — no extra visual element.)

12. **100 % local card (depth-2)** — IconWell + LocalIcon + `.eyebrow` "Privacidad" → title with `<span className="tnum">100</span> %` (tabular-num on the number) + "local, sin nube" / "local, no cloud" → description.

13. **Calendario P&L card (depth-2)** — IconWell + CalendarIcon + `.eyebrow` "Calendario" → title "Calendario P&L" → description → `mt-auto pt-5` + `MiniCalendarHeatmap` component.
    - `MiniCalendarHeatmap`: `grid grid-cols-7 gap-1` of 35 `aspect-square rounded-[2px]` cells. Deterministic pattern (mix of pos/neg/neutral, skews slightly positive) so it reads as a "profitable month with the usual red days". Cells use `bg-pnl-pos/55` / `bg-pnl-neg/55` / `bg-white/5` (same PnL tokens as the rest of the site).
    - Legend row (NEW, added after VLM feedback): `mt-3 flex items-center justify-between text-[10px] uppercase tracking-[0.12em] text-tertiary` with 3 inline-flex items (color dot + label): `Win` (pos dot) · `Loss` (neg dot) · `Flat` (white/10 dot). Anchors the calendar to meaning so it never reads as a decorative pattern.

14. **Importación CSV card (lg:col-span-3, depth-2)** — the wide closing card. Internal 2-col layout (`flex flex-col lg:flex-row lg:items-start gap-6 lg:gap-10 h-full`):
    - Left (`lg:w-[320px] shrink-0`): IconWell(accent) + ImportIcon + `.eyebrow` "Importación" → title "Importación CSV universal" → description.
    - Right (`flex-1 min-w-0`): `flex flex-wrap gap-2` of 10 broker chips + `mt-4 text-xs text-tertiary` microcopy ("¿Tu broker no está en la lista? Si exporta a CSV, este diario lo importa.").
    - Each broker chip uses `.pill bg-white/5 text-secondary border border-white/10 text-[11px] uppercase tracking-[0.08em]` containing a monospace bold `text-primary` monogram (IB / M4 / M5 / NT / TV / BN / CB / OA / IG / TD) + a `text-tertiary font-medium normal-case tracking-normal text-[10.5px]` full name (Interactive Brokers / MetaTrader 4 / etc.). The 2-letter monogram + name pattern reads as institutional (matches the existing `Integrations.tsx` broker grid).

### FeaturePreview.tsx rewrite (188 → 238 lines, +50 net)
Bilingual via `useLang()` preserved. `next/image` for the 3 product screenshots with `asset()` for basePath. No new CSS. Detailed layer-by-layer changes:

1. **Section container** — `section-tight cv-auto relative overflow-hidden` preserved. Kept the existing `SlowMoChart` opacity-0.04 backdrop (faint slow equity-curve drifting behind the whole strip). Added `<div className="grain absolute inset-0 pointer-events-none" />` for the opt-in 3% fractalNoise texture (matches Bento's grain layer so the two sections read as one continuous surface).

2. **Header row** — `text-center max-w-2xl mx-auto mb-10` preserved. `<Eyebrow className="justify-center">` "Lo más potente" / "Most powerful" + `mt-5 t-h2 text-white` headline with `.text-gradient` highlight on "no salir sin comprar." / "not to leave without buying." (existing copy preserved verbatim).

3. **Animated accent gradient line** — preserved verbatim. `h-px w-full origin-left mb-6` with `linear-gradient(90deg, transparent 0%, rgb(var(--accent-base) / 0.6) 50%, transparent 100%)` that draws across the top on view (`scaleX: 0 → 1`, opacity `0 → 1`, duration 1.1s, ease `[0.22, 1, 0.36, 1]`).

4. **Grid** — `grid md:grid-cols-3 gap-4 md:gap-5` (was `gap-4` only). Matches the Bento grid's gap language so the two sections have visual parity.

5. **Card surface language** — every card uses `liquid-glass rounded-card overflow-hidden flex flex-col group transition-[background-color,border-color,box-shadow,transform] duration-300 ease-[cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1)] hover:border-white/20 hover:shadow-[0_8px_30px_rgb(var(--accent-base)/0.08)]`. The hover lift comes from framer-motion's `whileHover={{ y: -4, ... }}` (same spring physics as Bento). The accent border glow at 8% opacity matches Bento exactly.

6. **Image frame (refined)** — `relative aspect-[16/10] overflow-hidden border-b border-white/10` (was `aspect-[4/3]` with no border-b). The new 16/10 ratio is more cinematic / Linear-Vercel-style. The hairline `border-b border-white/10` between image and content is the Linear / Vercel feature-card pattern — gives the image a "framed" feel without a hard separator.
   - Image: `object-cover transition-transform duration-500 ease-out group-hover:scale-[1.05]` (was `duration-700 group-hover:scale-[1.04]`). Faster duration (500ms) + slightly larger zoom (1.05 vs 1.04) per task spec.
   - Bottom gradient: preserved verbatim — `linear-gradient(180deg, transparent 55%, rgb(var(--card) / 0.85) 100%)` blends the image with the card body.
   - NEW: numbered eyebrow chip floating top-left of the image. `absolute top-3 left-3 inline-flex items-center justify-center min-w-[1.75rem] h-7 px-2 rounded-md bg-black/50 backdrop-blur-sm border border-white/15 text-[11px] font-semibold tracking-[0.06em] tnum text-white` + `fontFamily: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, monospace`. The 01/02/03 numbering reads as a "curated sequence" rather than 3 unrelated cards (Linear changelog pattern). DOM-verified: all 3 chips at top:50, left:84/469/855 (consistent top, increasing left by card width).

7. **Content body (refined)** — `p-5 md:p-6 flex flex-col flex-1 gap-3` (was `p-5 flex flex-col flex-1` with no gap). The strict `gap-3` vertical rhythm + `p-5 md:p-6` padding per task spec. Content stack:
   - `.eyebrow` kicker label (NEW per card): "Métricas" / "Metrics", "Equity", "Disciplina" / "Discipline". Gives each card a small contextual label above the title.
   - `text-lg font-semibold text-primary leading-tight` title (was `t-h3 text-white leading-tight mb-3`). Tighter size (`text-lg` vs `t-h3`'s `clamp(1.25rem, 2.5vw, 1.5rem)`) for the smaller card context. `text-primary` (theme-adaptive) instead of raw `text-white`.
   - `text-sm text-secondary leading-relaxed` description (was `text-sm text-gray-300 leading-relaxed line-clamp-2`). `text-secondary` (theme-adaptive) instead of raw `text-gray-300`. Removed `line-clamp-2` since descriptions are short enough to never need truncation.
   - "Learn more" / "Más información" link: `mt-auto pt-2 inline-flex items-center gap-1.5 text-sm font-semibold text-primary hover:text-[rgb(var(--accent-base))] transition-colors duration-200` (was `mt-4 inline-flex items-center gap-1.5 text-sm font-semibold text-white hover:text-gray-300 hover:gap-2.5 transition-all`). `mt-auto` pushes the link to the bottom for consistent card-height rhythm. Hover color is now accent gold (was gray-300). Arrow SVG `group-hover:translate-x-1` (was `group-hover:gap-2.5` via the parent's gap utility — cleaner with explicit translate). Changed link text from "Ver más" / "See more" to "Más información" / "Learn more" — more institutional copy.

8. **CARDS data** — added `num`, `kickerEs`, `kickerEn` fields to each card. Existing `src` / `altEs` / `altEn` / `titleEs` / `titleEn` / `descEs` / `descEn` preserved verbatim. The 3 cards remain: gems-gold.webp (Métricas), equity-curve.webp (Curva de equity), discipline-guardian.webp (Guardián de disciplina).

All new markers present in the SSR'd HTML.

  - Row 1: Disciplina hero (col-span-2) = 751×295, Métricas (col-span-1) = 365×295 — SAME HEIGHT ✓
  - Row 2: Playbook = 365×460, 100 % local = 365×460, Calendario P&L = 365×460 — SAME HEIGHT ✓

- **DOM eval — invoice numbers**: line items render as positive amounts in red (`text-pnl-neg`): "184,67 US$", "155,82 US$", "103,88 US$", "132,73 US$". TOTAL renders as negative with unicode minus: "−577,10 US$" ✓.
- **VLM visual verification** (z-ai vision, glm-4.6v):
  • Bento top screenshot (hero card + Métricas): VLM confirmed "wide hero card 'Disciplina que cuesta dinero' spans 2 columns of a 3-column grid" ✓, "mini invoice preview on its right side with monospace numbers, dashed dividers, and a RED TOTAL line at the bottom" ✓, "single 'Métricas institucionales' card to the right of the hero" ✓, "consistent padding and the same machined-glass aesthetic" ✓. On the brutal-honesty review: VLM confirmed "TOTAL row shows a negative amount with a minus sign: '−577,10 US$' in red" ✓, "line items shown as positive amounts in red" ✓, "FACTURA header with the invoice ID '#IND-2026-07'" ✓, "Gap por operación × 15 subtotal row in orange/yellow text (−38,47 US$)" ✓.
  • Bento row 2 + CSV screenshot: VLM confirmed "3 cards in a row: Playbook / 100 % local / Calendario P&L" ✓, "Calendario P&L card has a small legend below the calendar heatmap showing color dots + labels for Win / Loss / Flat" ✓, "wide 'Importación CSV universal' card spanning the full width with broker chips displaying monograms + names (IB · Interactive Brokers, M4 · MetaTrader 4, M5 · MetaTrader 5, NT · NinjaTrader, TV · TradingView, BN · Binance, CB · Coinbase, OA · OANDA, IG · IG, TD · TD Ameritrade)" ✓.
  • FeaturePreview screenshot: VLM confirmed "3 cards in a horizontal row, each with a full-width image at the top" ✓, "consistent 16:10 aspect ratio" ✓, "kicker labels (MÉTRICAS, EQUITY, DISCIPLINA) in all caps" ✓, "title, description, 'Más información' link with right arrow" ✓, "uniform height, consistent padding around text/content, matching image aspect ratios" ✓, "thin, subtle border separates the image from the text/content below each card" ✓. (VLM initially missed the 01/02/03 numbered chips — DOM eval confirmed they exist at top:50/left:84/469/855 with correct styling; VLM perception limitation due to the chips being 32×28px on a complex image background, not an actual issue.)
- **Issues found**: One real bug during development — the initial `BentoCard` primitive put the `className` prop on the inner `motion.div` instead of the outer `Reveal` wrapper (which is the actual grid item). This made `lg:col-span-2` / `lg:col-span-3` ineffective — the layout collapsed to a flat 3×2 grid. Caught via DOM eval (all cards were 365×983, same width). Fixed by splitting the prop into `outer` (grid-track classes — col-span, h-full — that land on the `Reveal` wrapper) + `inner` (surface classes — depth tokens — that land on the inner `motion.div`). Re-verified via DOM eval: Disciplina card now 751×295, CSV card 1136×210. Bug resolved.
- Also fixed a rendering bug in the invoice TOTAL: initially passed `value={-total}` to `<Money>` for the TOTAL, but `METRICS.costOfIndiscipline` is already a negative number (-577.10), so `-total` = +577.10 → Money rendered "577,10 US$" without the minus sign. Fixed by changing to `value={total}` (raw signed value) so Money prepends the unicode minus → TOTAL now reads "−577,10 US$" (verified via DOM eval + VLM).

Stage Summary:
- **Bento.tsx** (165 → 608 lines, +443 net): Replaced the simple 2×2 grid with a proper institutional 3-col bento. 6 cards in 3 rows: [ Disciplina hero (col-span-2, depth-3) + Métricas ] / [ Playbook + 100 % local + Calendario P&L ] / [ Importación CSV (col-span-3) ]. NEW components: `IconWell` (machined icon container, accent variant for hero cards), `MiniInvoice` (compact FACTURA preview with header, dashed dividers, 4 line items, subtotal row, red TOTAL with proper minus sign — pulls real METRICS data so it matches MistakeInvoice on other pages), `MiniCalendarHeatmap` (7×5 P&L grid with color-coded cells + Win/Loss/Flat legend), `BentoCard` primitive (outer/inner class split for proper grid-track control). NEW icons: `CalendarIcon`, `ImportIcon`. All cards use the same machined-glass language (`.liquid-glass rounded-card p-6` + hover lift + accent border glow). All card headers follow the strict rhythm: IconWell + `.eyebrow` kicker + `text-xl md:text-2xl font-semibold text-primary` title + `text-sm text-secondary` description. Section gains a `.grain` overlay layer for the premium printed feel. Bilingual via `useLang()`. No new CSS.
- **FeaturePreview.tsx** (188 → 238 lines, +50 net): Refined the 3 image cards to institutional polish. Image frame: `aspect-[16/10]` (was 4/3) + `border-b border-white/10` hairline separator + `group-hover:scale-[1.05]` zoom with `duration-500` (was 1.04 / duration-700). NEW: numbered eyebrow chip (01/02/03) floating top-left of each image — monospace tnum text on a `bg-black/50 backdrop-blur-sm border border-white/15` chip. Content body: `p-5 md:p-6 flex flex-col flex-1 gap-3` (was `p-5` with no gap) — strict `gap-3` rhythm. NEW per-card `.eyebrow` kicker (Métricas / Equity / Disciplina). Title `text-lg font-semibold text-primary` (was `t-h3 text-white`). Description `text-sm text-secondary` (was `text-gray-300`). Link "Más información" / "Learn more" with `mt-auto` (pushes to bottom for consistent card height) + accent-gold hover + arrow `group-hover:translate-x-1`. Section gains a `.grain` overlay layer. Bilingual via `useLang()`. No new CSS.
- **Lint**: `bun run lint` → exit 0, zero errors, zero warnings.
- **Dev server**: HTTP 200 on `/`. DOM-verified card sizes + aspect ratios + chip placements. VLM (glm-4.6v) confirms the institutional refinements render correctly: wide hero card spanning 2 columns, mini FACTURA invoice with red TOTAL showing minus sign, calendar heatmap with Win/Loss/Flat legend, broker chips with monogram + name, 3 FeaturePreview cards with consistent 16/10 image frames, hairline borders, kicker labels, and "Más información →" links.
- **Issues found**: Two real bugs caught and fixed during development — (1) `BentoCard` primitive's `className` prop landed on the inner `motion.div` instead of the outer `Reveal` grid-item wrapper, making `lg:col-span-2`/`lg:col-span-3` ineffective; fixed by splitting into `outer`/`inner` props. (2) Invoice TOTAL initially rendered without the minus sign because `value={-total}` negated the already-negative `METRICS.costOfIndiscipline`; fixed by passing `value={total}` (raw signed value). Both bugs caught via DOM eval + VLM verification before sign-off.

### Pricing.tsx (444 → 447 lines, +3 net)
Two surgical edits to the `PlanCard` component. Bilingual via `useLang()` preserved. No new CSS. Detailed layer-by-layer changes:

1. **Price display refactor** — replaced the `mt-6 flex items-start gap-1` wrapper (which lifted the $ via `mt-2` and put it at the top edge — "superscript prefix" pattern) + the separate `<p className="mt-2 text-sm text-tertiary">USD · pago único</p>` paragraph below, with a single `mt-6 flex items-baseline gap-1` wrapper containing the `$` prefix + `<CountUp>` big number + a new inline `<span className="ml-2 text-sm text-tertiary whitespace-nowrap">/ {es ? "pago único" : "one-time"}</span>`. The `items-baseline` alignment puts the $ on the same baseline as the big number (so the price reads as a unified currency figure rather than a superscript annotation) AND — critically — guarantees both cards' price baselines sit at the exact same y-coordinate (since both wrappers have identical structure + the tagline above has `min-h-[2.6em]` for vertical parity). DOM-verified: both CountUp elements at `top: 2194, bottom: 2251, h: 57` (identical baseline). The `/ pago único` suffix sits inline to the right at the baseline in `text-sm text-tertiary` per spec. The `whitespace-nowrap` ensures the suffix never wraps to a second line. Dropped the "USD" prefix from the suffix since the `$` already conveys USD; the new copy reads cleaner ("$149 / pago único" instead of "$149 USD · pago único").

2. **CTA unification** — replaced the ternary `isPro ? "bg-white text-black shadow-[accent...] hover:bg-gray-100 hover:-translate-y-0.5 hover:shadow-[accent...]" : "liquid-glass border border-white/20 text-primary shadow-[neutral...] hover:bg-white hover:text-black hover:-translate-y-0.5 hover:shadow-[neutral...]"` with a single shared className string `"group w-full flex items-center justify-center gap-2 h-12 px-6 rounded-lg text-sm font-medium transition-all duration-200 bg-white text-black shadow-[0_2px_8px_-2px_rgb(var(--accent-base)/0.40),0_1px_2px_rgb(0_0_0/0.20)] hover:bg-gray-100 hover:-translate-y-0.5 hover:shadow-[0_8px_20px_-4px_rgb(var(--accent-base)/0.55),0_2px_8px_rgb(0_0_0/0.25)]"`. Both Core and Pro CTAs now use the same primary `bg-white text-black` treatment with the same accent-tinted shadow (40% opacity at rest, 55% on hover) and the same `hover:-translate-y-0.5` lift. Rationale: per task spec "CTA buttons: 'Comprar Core' / 'Comprar Pro' — primary `bg-white text-black` + shadow + hover lift. Full-width." The Pro card already differentiates via `gradient-border depth-4` + the floating "Más popular" pill + the "PREMIUM" watermark + the accent glow shadow on the card itself — it doesn't need the CTA to also differentiate. Making both primary means the visitor's eye doesn't read Core as the "lesser" path; the choice becomes about feature scope, not about which CTA "looks better". The `isPro` variable is still used elsewhere in `PlanCard` (gradient-border, depth-4, pill, watermark, accent gold check icons) — only the CTA ternary was removed. DOM-verified: both CTAs at `top: 2598, bottom: 2646, h: 48` (identical position + height — pixel-perfect parity).

### PricingFAQ.tsx (196 → 238 lines, +42 net)
Full rewrite via Write tool. Bilingual via `useLang()` preserved. `useReducedMotion` preserved. No new CSS. Detailed layer-by-layer changes:

1. **Layout refactor — 2-col → centered header above accordion** — replaced the `grid lg:grid-cols-[0.85fr_1.15fr] gap-8 lg:gap-12 items-start` 2-col layout (left = sticky heading + supporting copy + reassurance pills, right = accordion + "More questions?" CTA) with a single centered column: `<Reveal className="text-center max-w-2xl mx-auto">` wrapping the eyebrow + headline + lead + reassurance pills, followed by `<Reveal delay={0.1} y={28}>` wrapping `mt-10 max-w-3xl mx-auto` accordion container + `mt-5 text-center text-sm text-tertiary` "More questions?" CTA + `mt-12 h-px max-w-md mx-auto` accent line. The new layout mirrors the Pricing section's centered header rhythm so the two sections read as one continuous conversion story (the FAQ answers the questions the Pricing cards raise). Removed the `lg:sticky lg:top-24` from the heading since it's no longer in a 2-col grid where sticky would make sense. DOM-verified: `eyebrowCenter: 640 = sectionCenter: 640` ✓, `accordionCenter: 640 = sectionCenter: 640` ✓, `accordionWidth: 744` (within max-w-3xl = 768px minus padding) ✓, `eyebrowTop: 3153, accordionTop: 3413` (accordion 260px below eyebrow — proper header-above-accordion rhythm) ✓.

2. **Section container refinements** — added `bg-black` to the section className (was `section-tight relative overflow-hidden scroll-mt-24`, now `section-tight bg-black relative overflow-hidden scroll-mt-24`) so the FAQ reads as a continuation of the Pricing section's dark canvas (Pricing already had `bg-black`). Added a soft radial accent glow `<div className="absolute top-1/2 left-1/2 -translate-x-1/2 -translate-y-1/2 w-[680px] h-[420px] rounded-full blur-[140px] pointer-events-none" style={{ background: "radial-gradient(circle, rgb(var(--accent-base)), transparent 70%)", opacity: 0.08 }} />` — matches the Pricing section's radial glow at 8% opacity so the two sections share the same atmospheric anchor. Added the opt-in `<div className="grain absolute inset-0 pointer-events-none" aria-hidden="true" />` 3% fractalNoise overlay (matches HeroVideo / Bento / Pricing / FinalCTA so the FAQ reads as the same premium printed surface).

3. **Accordion header refinement** — changed `AccordionTrigger` className from `text-left text-sm md:text-base font-medium text-primary hover:text-primary hover:no-underline py-5 transition-colors [&[data-state=open]>svg]:rotate-180` to `text-left text-sm font-medium text-primary hover:text-primary hover:no-underline py-5 transition-colors [&[data-state=open]>svg]:rotate-180` (dropped `md:text-base` so the header is uniformly `text-sm` across breakpoints per task spec "refined accordion headers (`text-sm font-medium` + chevron)"). The chevron is provided by the shadcn accordion primitive (`ChevronDownIcon` from `lucide-react` with `size-4` + `translate-y-0.5` + `transition-transform duration-200` + `[&[data-state=open]>svg]:rotate-180`), so no custom chevron SVG was needed. The smooth open animation is also handled by the primitive via `data-[state=closed]:animate-accordion-up data-[state=open]:animate-accordion-down` (Radix height transition).

4. **Accordion container preserved** — kept `liquid-glass depth-2 rounded-card p-2 md:p-3` for the container (matches task spec ".liquid-glass rounded-card container"). Kept the per-item `data-[state=open]:border-white/25 data-[state=open]:bg-white/[0.04] data-[state=open]:shadow-[0_0_28px_-6px_rgb(var(--accent-base)_/_0.18)]` open-state styling (matches task spec "subtle `bg-white/[0.04]` tint when open"). Kept the `transition-[border-color,box-shadow,background-color] duration-300` smooth-state-change transition. Kept the `border-white/10 last:border-b-0 rounded-md` per-item border treatment.

5. **Reassurance pills — centered** — moved the 3-pill row (`30 días de garantía` / `30-day guarantee`, `Datos 100 % locales` / `100 % local data`, `Pago único` / `One-time payment`) from the left column (where they were `flex flex-wrap gap-2`) to under the centered lead (`flex flex-wrap items-center justify-center gap-2`). Each pill uses `.pill bg-white/5 text-secondary border border-white/10 tnum` with a `size-1.5 rounded-full bg-white` accent dot. The staggered framer-motion reveal (`delay: 0.18 + i * 0.08`) is preserved.

6. **"More questions?" inline CTA preserved** — kept the `mt-5 text-center text-sm text-tertiary` paragraph with the `<a href={asset("/faq")} className="text-primary hover:text-[rgb(var(--accent-base))] hover:underline font-medium transition-colors duration-200">` link to the full FAQ page. Now centered (was right-aligned via the 2-col layout's right column).

7. **Subtle accent line preserved** — kept the `mt-12 h-px max-w-md mx-auto` gradient line with `linear-gradient(90deg, transparent 0%, rgb(var(--accent-base) / 0.4) 50%, transparent 100%)` + the framer-motion `scaleX: 0 → 1` reveal (respects `prefers-reduced-motion` via `useReducedMotion` → `opacity: 0.6` instead of `1` when reduced).

### FinalCTA.tsx (219 → 245 lines, +26 net)
Targeted additions to layer in the missing atmospheric pieces per spec "aurora-bg + orbs + particles". Bilingual via `useLang()` preserved. No new CSS. Detailed layer-by-layer changes:

1. **ParticleField import + layer (NEW)** — added `import { ParticleField } from "@/components/tj/ParticleField";` at the top. Added a new layer between the orbs and the grain: `<div aria-hidden="true" className="pointer-events-none absolute inset-0"><ParticleField count={28} opacity={0.12} linkDistance={110} /></div>`. The constellation sits above the orbs (which provide the warm gold wash) but below the grain + bottom fade + content. Tuned to be more subtle than not-found.tsx's `count={36} opacity={0.2} linkDistance={130}` — fewer nodes (28 vs 36), lower opacity (0.12 vs 0.20), shorter link distance (110 vs 130) so the constellation reads as ambient floating particles in the air rather than a featured hero effect. DOM-verified: `canvasPresent: true, canvasW: 1280, canvasH: 571` — the canvas covers the full section. The ParticleField's internal `useInView` hook pauses the animation when the section is offscreen (200px rootMargin) so it doesn't burn CPU on long scrolls.

2. **`.aurora-bg opacity-25` base wash (NEW)** — added a new layer beneath the orbs: `<div aria-hidden="true" className="pointer-events-none absolute inset-0 aurora-bg opacity-25" />`. The `.aurora-bg` CSS class (in globals.css) paints 3 radial gradients — gold at 15%/10%, blue at 85%/15%, purple at 50%/100% — at full strength. The `opacity-25` modifier knocks the whole layer down to 25% so the cool blue/purple reads as ambient atmosphere rather than visible color. The accent gold orbs layered above carry the warmth. This is the same `aurora-bg opacity-N` pattern used by Milestones (25%), Values (30%), Changelog (40%), BlogPreview (50%), Wrapped (50%), HowItWorks (60%), Newsletter (100%), StatsBand (100%), FeatureRotator (100%), DownloadCTA (100%) — so it's a well-established site pattern at varying opacities. DOM-verified: `auroraBgPresent: true, auroraW: 1280` ✓.

3. **Existing layers preserved** — kept the 3 accent gold orbs (`-top-24 left-1/4 w-[480px] h-[480px]` at 0.12, `top-1/4 right-1/4 w-[420px] h-[420px]` at 0.08, `-bottom-40 left-1/2 -translate-x-1/2 w-[640px] h-[340px]` at 0.10), the `.grain` overlay, the `h-24` bottom fade gradient (`bg-gradient-to-b from-transparent to-black`), the section container (`relative bg-black overflow-hidden py-20 md:py-28`), the headline (`text-4xl md:text-5xl lg:text-6xl font-semibold tracking-tight text-primary text-balance` with `.text-gradient` on "disciplina."), the lead (`mt-6 text-lg text-secondary leading-relaxed max-w-2xl mx-auto`), the 2 CTAs (primary `bg-white text-black` with accent-tinted shadow + secondary `liquid-glass border border-white/20`), and the 4-item guarantee row (Garantía 30 días · Sin suscripción · Datos 100 % locales · ES + EN, dot-separated, `text-xs text-tertiary tnum` with emerald `CheckSmall` icons). DOM-verified: section `overflowX: hidden, overflowY: hidden` ✓ (no horizontal overflow from orbs/particles), `paddingTop: 112px, paddingBottom: 112px` (= `py-28` at md+ ✓), `h2FontSize: 60px` (= `lg:text-6xl` ✓), `ctaCount: 2` ✓, both CTAs at `h: 48, top: -210` (identical height + y-position ✓ — same `h-12` parity), primary CTA `bg: rgb(255,255,255), color: rgb(0,0,0)` ✓, secondary CTA `bg: rgba(0,0,0,0.4)` (= liquid-glass) + `color: rgb(255,255,255)` ✓, 4 guarantee items present ✓.

4. **JSDoc updated** — expanded the institutional-polish comment block to document the new `.aurora-bg opacity-25` base wash + the `ParticleField` constellation layer (28 nodes / 12% opacity / 110px link distance) + the rationale (premium fintech landing-page pattern, mirrors the not-found page's particle treatment, pauses offscreen, respects `prefers-reduced-motion`).

Stage Summary:
- **Pricing.tsx** (444 → 447 lines, +3 net): Two surgical PlanCard edits. (1) Price display refactor — replaced the `items-start gap-1` $ prefix lift + separate "USD · pago único" paragraph with a single `items-baseline gap-1` row containing $ + CountUp + inline `/ pago único` suffix. The `items-baseline` alignment guarantees both cards' price baselines sit at the exact same y-coordinate (DOM-verified: both at top:2194, bottom:2251). "bg-white text-black..." : "liquid-glass border..."` ternary with a single shared primary `bg-white text-black` className with accent-tinted shadow (40% at rest, 55% on hover) + `hover:-translate-y-0.5` lift. Both CTAs now identical (DOM-verified: both at top:2598, h:48). Pro card still differentiates via `gradient-border depth-4` + "Más popular" pill + "PREMIUM" watermark + accent glow. Bilingual via `useLang()`. No new CSS.
- **PricingFAQ.tsx** (196 → 238 lines, +42 net): Full rewrite from 2-col [heading | accordion] layout to centered header above single accordion container. Header: `text-center max-w-2xl mx-auto` Reveal wrapping `.eyebrow` (centered) + `text-3xl md:text-4xl font-semibold tracking-tight text-primary text-balance` headline with `.text-gradient` on "preguntan." + `text-base md:text-lg text-secondary` lead + 3 reassurance pills (`flex flex-wrap items-center justify-center gap-2`). Accordion: `mt-10 max-w-3xl mx-auto liquid-glass depth-2 rounded-card p-2 md:p-3` container with 5 items, each using `text-sm font-medium text-primary` headers (dropped `md:text-base` per spec) + shadcn chevron + `data-[state=open]:bg-white/[0.04]` tint + `data-[state=open]:border-white/25` + accent glow. Section gains `bg-black` + radial accent glow (8% opacity) + `.grain` texture layer to match the Pricing section's dark-premium surface. "More questions?" inline CTA + accent line preserved (centered). DOM-verified: eyebrowCenter = accordionCenter = sectionCenter = 640 (perfectly centered). Bilingual via `useLang()`. No new CSS.
- **FinalCTA.tsx** (219 → 245 lines, +26 net): Added 2 new atmospheric layers per spec "aurora-bg + orbs + particles". (1) `.aurora-bg opacity-25` base wash — the CSS class paints 3 radial gradients (gold + blue + purple) at 25% opacity so the cool tones read as ambient atmosphere, not visible color; the accent gold orbs layered above carry the warmth. Same pattern as Milestones (25%), Values (30%), Changelog (40%), BlogPreview (50%). (2) `ParticleField` constellation layer — `count={28} opacity={0.12} linkDistance={110}`, canvas-based, pauses offscreen via `useInView`, respects `prefers-reduced-motion`, mouse-repel effect, accent-color nodes + faint link lines. More subtle than not-found.tsx's `count={36} opacity={0.2}` so the constellation reads as ambient floating particles rather than a featured hero effect. All existing layers preserved (3 accent gold orbs at 0.08-0.12, `.grain` overlay, `h-24` bottom fade, `overflow-hidden`, `py-20 md:py-28`, headline `text-4xl md:text-5xl lg:text-6xl font-semibold tracking-tight` with `.text-gradient` on "disciplina.", lead `text-lg text-secondary max-w-2xl mx-auto`, 2 CTAs both at `h-12`, 4-item guarantee row). DOM-verified: section 1280×571, overflowX/Y hidden, padding 112/112, h2 60px, aurora + canvas + grain all present, both CTAs at h:48 top:-210 (identical). Bilingual via `useLang()`. No new CSS.
- **Lint**: `bun run lint` → exit 0, zero errors, zero warnings.
- **Dev server**: HTTP 200 on `/` and `/pricing`. DOM-verified pixel-perfect parity for both Pricing cards (identical 436×616, price baselines at 2194-2251, CTAs at 2598-2646 h:48), centered PricingFAQ layout (eyebrow + accordion both at center:640), and FinalCTA structure (aurora-bg + orbs + particles + grain + fade all layered, both CTAs at h:48 top:-210, 4 guarantee items).
- **Issues found**: None. All edits applied cleanly on first pass; no bugs caught during DOM verification.

Task ID: R2-c
Agent: ticker-home-cta-polish
Task: Pixel-perfect institutional polish of Ticker + HomeCTASection (home page conversion surfaces).

Work Log:
- Read worklog.md (J0→R3-d context: foundation, marketing QA, demo QA, motion polish, content sections) for the established design language — `.liquid-glass` machined-glass surface (1px white top highlight + 1px black bottom inset + ::before rim gradient), `.eyebrow` (0.625rem uppercase tracking-0.16em in `--txt-tertiary`), `.grain` (3% fractalNoise ::after overlay), `.text-gradient` (accent gold gradient clip), `.rounded-card` (8px), `.tnum` (tabular-nums + slashed zeros), `.section-tight` (3–5rem), `--accent-base: 199 167 107` (gold #C7A76B), `--pnl-pos: 52 211 153`, `--pnl-neg: 239 68 68`, `--txt-tertiary: 156 163 175` (gray-400 on dark).
- Read `src/app/globals.css` (1358 lines) — confirmed all utility classes + tokens used by both components resolve correctly under `@layer utilities` and the `@theme inline` block. Confirmed `.liquid-glass` uses `rgba(0,0,0,0.4)` background + `backdrop-filter: blur(4px)` + machined-edge box-shadow + `::before` rim gradient. Confirmed `bg-white text-black` is the established primary CTA pattern (Hero line 151, Navbar lines 284/434, Pricing line 367).
- Read `src/components/marketing/Ticker.tsx` (162 lines) and `src/components/marketing/HomeCTASection.tsx` (185 lines) — found both components already carry comprehensive R2-c-style polish doc comments (a prior run had scaffolded them). Audited every spec line item against the actual JSX.

Spec audit — Ticker (all ✅ after final tweak):
  1. `tnum` on all numbers — line 70 `<span className="tnum text-xs flex items-center gap-2">`. ✅
  2. `text-pnl-pos`/`text-pnl-neg` green/red — line 72 `${pos ? "text-pnl-pos" : "text-pnl-neg"}`. ✅
  3. dot separators `text-tertiary` — **was** `bg-white/15` (raw chrome-neutral). **Changed** to `bg-[rgb(var(--txt-tertiary)/0.5)]` so the 4px rivet uses the design-system tertiary text token (gray-400 on dark, gray-500 on light) at 50% opacity — the dots now shift hue correctly when the theme flips to light, and read as a soft machined-rivet rather than a raw white speck. Updated the doc comment block to reflect the change and rationale.
  4. `liquid-glass` band with `border-y border-white/10` — line 133 `relative border-y border-white/10 py-3 liquid-glass overflow-hidden select-none`. ✅
  5. Slow/dignified speed — `basePxPerMs = half / 50 / 1000` → full track (two rows) in 50 s ≈ 35 px/s on a typical viewport. Was 38 s before — the prior R2-c pass already extended it. ✅
  6. Honor `useReducedMotion` (static fallback) — `const reduce = useReducedMotion();` at line 86, `if (reduce) return;` at line 109 inside `useAnimationFrame`. With reduce set, the RAF callback bails immediately and the track stays at x=0 so the first Row remains statically visible (no infinite scroll). ✅
  7. Seamless loop — line 124 `let next = x.get() - speed * dt;` then line 125 `if (next <= -half) next += half;`. `half = el.scrollWidth / 2` because the track contains two identical `<Row />` children — when the track has scrolled exactly one Row's width, it snaps back by `half` and the second Row is now visually where the first was. No visible jump because the two Rows are identical. Delta also clamped to ≤ 32 ms per frame (line 118) so a backgrounded tab returning with a 100 ms+ delta doesn't catapult the track forward and break the seam. ✅
  8. Left/right fade gradient mask — lines 138–144 (left edge, `linear-gradient(to right, rgba(0,0,0,0.92) 0%, rgba(0,0,0,0.5) 55%, transparent 100%)`) + lines 145–152 (right edge, mirror). Both `w-20 md:w-32` (80–128px wide), `z-10`, `pointer-events-none`, sit over the scrolling track so symbols ease in/out of view at the band edges instead of clipping at a hard edge. The black-tinted gradient matches the dark liquid-glass surface. ✅
  9. Scroll-velocity responsive multiplier — `useScroll()` → `useVelocity(scrollY)` → `useTransform` maps |v| 0..4000 → 1×..2.4×, wrapped in `useSpring({ stiffness: 120, damping: 24, mass: 0.4 })` so the speed-up eases in/out smoothly instead of snapping frame-to-frame. Eliminates the micro-stutter the ticker used to show when the user scrolled aggressively and then stopped. ✅
 10. Symbols + P&L vertically aligned, consistent spacing — the `<span className="tnum text-xs flex items-center gap-2">` wrapper uses `items-center` so the symbol and P&L% share a vertical centerline; `gap-2` (8px) between them; the dot separator uses `mx-3.5` (14px each side, 28px total) before the next item. Consistent across all 14 ticker items. ✅

Spec audit — HomeCTASection (all ✅, no changes needed):
  1. Refined multi-card layout (3 cards) with `.liquid-glass rounded-card p-6` — line 128 `grid md:grid-cols-3 gap-4 md:gap-5` + line 137 `liquid-glass rounded-card p-6 h-full flex flex-col group`. Three cards: Features (`/features`), Demo (`/demo`), Pricing (`/pricing` — primary). ✅
  2. Each with icon + title + description + CTA link — IconWell (line 140–145: `w-10 h-10 rounded-lg bg-white/5 border border-white/10 text-primary shadow-[inset_0_1px_0_rgb(255_255_255/0.08)]`) → title (line 146: `text-xl md:text-2xl font-semibold text-primary leading-tight`) → description (line 149: `text-sm text-secondary leading-relaxed flex-1`) → CTA Link (line 158). The `flex-1` on the description pushes the CTA to the bottom of each card so cards with varying description lengths share the same button baseline. ✅
  3. `.eyebrow` header + title — line 112 `<Eyebrow className="justify-center">{es ? "Profundiza" : "Go deeper"}</Eyebrow>` + line 115 `<h2 className="mt-5 t-h2 text-primary">` with a `.text-gradient` span on the closing clause (lines 118, 122: "una razón para comprar." / "a reason to buy."). ✅
  4. `.grain` texture — line 108 `<div aria-hidden="true" className="grain absolute inset-0 pointer-events-none" />`. The `.grain::after` 3% fractalNoise overlay reads as a printed-paper feel matching Bento + FeaturePreview so the three home-page sections form one continuous surface. ✅
  5. Cards same height (`h-full`), aligned — line 130 `<Reveal key={card.href} delay={i * 0.08} className="h-full">` + line 137 motion.div `h-full flex flex-col`. The grid uses default `auto-rows-fr`-equivalent stretch behavior; combined with `h-full` on both the Reveal wrapper and the motion.div, all three cards share the same row height regardless of description length. ✅
  6. `hover:-translate-y-1` lift + subtle accent glow — line 136 `whileHover={{ y: -4, transition: { type: "spring", stiffness: 300, damping: 24 } }}` (-4px = -translate-y-1 in Tailwind's 0.25rem scale) + line 137 `transition-[background-color,border-color,box-shadow,transform] duration-300 ease-[cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1)] hover:border-white/20 hover:shadow-[0_8px_30px_rgb(var(--accent-base)/0.08)]`. The accent-tinted shadow (8% opacity of `--accent-base` gold) is the "subtle accent glow" — warm but not garish. ✅
  7. CTAs: `bg-white text-black` primary / `liquid-glass border border-white/20` secondary — line 158–164 ternary: `card.primary` → `bg-white text-black shadow-[0_2px_8px_-2px_rgb(var(--accent-base)/0.40),0_1px_2px_rgb(0_0_0/0.20)] hover:bg-gray-100 hover:-translate-y-0.5 hover:shadow-[0_8px_20px_-4px_rgb(var(--accent-base)/0.55),0_2px_8px_rgb(0_0_0/0.25)]`; else → `liquid-glass border border-white/20 text-primary hover:bg-white hover:text-black hover:-translate-y-0.5`. Both share `h-12 px-6 rounded-lg text-sm font-medium` for visual parity. The primary pattern is **identical** to `Pricing.tsx` line 367's primary CTA — same shadow stack, same hover lift, same accent-tinted glow. The secondary pattern (liquid-glass + border + hover invert to bg-white/text-black) matches the visual language of the Navbar's secondary CTA. ✅
  8. Bilingual via `useLang()` — line 42 `const { lang } = useLang();` + line 43 `const es = lang === "es";`; every text node uses `es ? card.titleEs : card.titleEn` (etc.). Three cards × 3 string fields (title/desc/cta) + eyebrow + h2 = 11 bilingual strings, all covered. ✅
  9. No indigo/blue — verified by grep: the only color tokens used are `--accent-base` (gold), `--txt-primary/secondary/tertiary` (grays), `--pnl-pos` (green, not used here), `white`, `black`, `bg-white/5`/`bg-white/10`/`bg-white/15`/`border-white/10`/`border-white/20` (translucent whites), and `gray-100` (hover). No Tailwind `indigo-*`/`blue-*`/`sky-*`/`cyan-*`/`violet-*`/`purple-*` classes. ✅
 10. Dark theme primary — section uses `bg-black` (line 96) which on the dark-first site reads as a distinct anchor band between FeaturePreview and FinalCTA. The Bento + FinalCTA sections also use `bg-black`, so the three home-page dark bands (Bento → HomeCTASection → FinalCTA) form a visual rhythm. ✅

Section placement verified in `src/app/page.tsx` — flow is Hero → Ticker → Bento → FeaturePreview → HomeCTASection → FinalCTA. HomeCTASection sits between FeaturePreview and FinalCTA, exactly per the spec ("mid-page conversion section"). Wrapped in `<div id="cta" className="scroll-mt-16">` so the in-page navigation anchor accounts for the fixed navbar.

Edit made (1 file, 2 hunks):
- `src/components/marketing/Ticker.tsx`:
  - Header comment block (lines 32–36): rewrote the dot-separator doc paragraph to reflect the new `bg-[rgb(var(--txt-tertiary)/0.5)]` token choice and rationale (was documenting `bg-white/15`).
  - Row() JSX (line 80): `<span className="mx-3.5 w-1 h-1 rounded-full bg-white/15" />` → `<span className="mx-3.5 w-1 h-1 rounded-full bg-[rgb(var(--txt-tertiary)/0.5)]" />`.
- `src/components/marketing/HomeCTASection.tsx`: no changes — every spec line item was already met by the prior R2-c scaffold. Audited and verified only.

Verification:
- `bun run lint` → exit 0 (no errors, no warnings). ✅
- `curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:3000/` → 200. ✅ (dev server was already up; no restart needed.)
- `agent-browser open "http://localhost:3000/"` → page loaded ("Trading Journal — Tu operativa, medida."). Scrolled down 1000 px → screenshot `/tmp/r2c-ticker.png` (Ticker band visible). Scrolled down 2500 px → screenshot `/tmp/r2c-cta.png` (HomeCTASection 3-card grid visible).
- DOM eval confirmed the dot's computed background-color is now `rgba(156, 163, 175, 0.5)` (= `rgb(var(--txt-tertiary)/0.5)` resolved against the dark theme's `--txt-tertiary: 156 163 175`), `width: 4px`, `height: 4px`. Spec-compliant. ✅
- DOM eval confirmed the 3 CTA cards carry `liquid-glass rounded-card p-6 h-full flex flex-col group transition-[background-color,border-color,box-shadow,transform]...` and the secondary CTA buttons carry `liquid-glass border border-white/20 text-primary...` — both matching the spec exactly. ✅

Stage Summary:
- Ticker.tsx: 1 polish change (dot separator color) — `bg-white/15` → `bg-[rgb(var(--txt-tertiary)/0.5)]` so the rivet uses the design-system tertiary text token (gray-400/gray-500) at 50% opacity. The token tie means the dots shift hue correctly when the theme flips to light, and they read as a soft machined-rivet rather than a raw white speck. All other spec items (tnum on numbers, text-pnl-pos/neg colorization, liquid-glass + border-y border-white/10 band, 50s slow/dignified loop, useReducedMotion static fallback, seamless two-Row loop with delta clamping, left/right black-tinted gradient fades, scroll-velocity spring multiplier, items-center vertical alignment + mx-3.5 spacing) were already met by a prior R2-c scaffold — audited and verified, no changes needed.
- HomeCTASection.tsx: 0 changes — every spec line item (3-card `.liquid-glass rounded-card p-6 h-full flex flex-col` grid with IconWell→title→description→CTA rhythm, `.eyebrow` + `.text-gradient` header, `.grain` overlay, `hover:-translate-y-1` Framer Motion spring lift + `hover:shadow-[0_8px_30px_rgb(var(--accent-base)/0.08)]` accent glow, primary `bg-white text-black` + secondary `liquid-glass border border-white/20` CTAs matching Pricing/Hero/Navbar patterns exactly, `useLang()` bilingual across 11 strings, no indigo/blue, dark-first `bg-black` section) was already met by the prior R2-c scaffold — audited and verified.
- Lint: clean (exit 0). Dev server: HTTP 200. Screenshots captured at `/tmp/r2c-ticker.png` and `/tmp/r2c-cta.png`.
- Constraints honored: edited only `Ticker.tsx` (the 2 allowed files: Ticker.tsx + HomeCTASection.tsx); no CSS changes to globals.css; prices untouched ($149/$249 — no pricing copy in either file).
- Next actions: (a) If a follow-up wants the Ticker fade masks to be theme-adaptive too (currently `rgba(0,0,0,0.92)` → transparent — appropriate for dark, but on a light theme the black fade would look wrong against a white page), the gradient could be re-tinted to `rgb(var(--background)/0.92)` so it always matches the page surface. Low priority — the site is dark-first and only the Ticker band sits above the page background; the band itself is `liquid-glass` (translucent dark), so the black fade is correct. (b) The HomeCTASection's 3 cards share equal heights via `h-full` + `flex-1` on the description + `mt-6` (not `mt-auto`) on the CTA — if a 4th card is ever added with a much longer description, consider switching to `mt-auto` on the CTA so the description can grow without affecting the CTA's vertical position. (c) The Ticker's scroll-velocity spring uses fixed `stiffness: 120, damping: 24, mass: 0.4` — if the spring feels too sluggish or too snappy on production devices, tuning `damping` between 18–30 is the single-knob adjustment.
